target.title: fix(install): trap SIGINT so Ctrl+C exits cleanly during upgrade doctor
target.body:
Fixes #90011
Fixes #82304

## Problem

When running `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`, pressing Ctrl+C during `openclaw doctor` in the upgrade flow does not abort the installer. The SIGINT kills the doctor subprocess but the installer catches it as a regular failure, continues past the prompt, and opens a browser to an unconfigured dashboard URL.

## Fix

1. **Add SIGINT/SIGTERM trap**: `abort_install_int`/`abort_install_term` handlers clean up temp files, show a warning, and exit 130/143
2. **Propagate doctor exit code 130**: capture `openclaw doctor` exit code separately; if 130 (SIGINT), call `abort_install_int` instead of treating it as a regular failure
3. **Preserve signal exit codes through `run_quiet_step`**: the wrapper previously normalized all failures to exit 1, which swallowed SIGINT (130). Now returns signal exit codes (>128) so the doctor exit-130 check fires correctly, while preserving exit 1 for non-signal failures to maintain existing caller semantics across all 66+ installer call sites
4. **Clear dashboard flag on doctor failure**: when the interactive upgrade-doctor exits non-zero (Clack cancellation exit 1, SIGINT exit 130, or ordinary failure), clear `should_open_dashboard` so the installer does not launch a dead dashboard after an incomplete upgrade
5. **Guard every run_doctor caller**: the existing-config fresh-install path called `run_doctor` without checking its return value, so a failed doctor would still set `should_open_dashboard=true`. Now guarded with `if run_doctor; then should_open_dashboard=true; fi`
6. **Fix Clack cancel exit code**: `guardCancel` in `onboard-helpers.ts` called `runtime.exit(0)` when the user cancelled at a Clack prompt (Escape key), making the install script treat cancellation as success. Changed to `runtime.exit(1)` to match the `WizardCancelledError` pattern used by `clack-prompter.ts` and `onboard-interactive.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** (1) Pressing Ctrl+C (SIGINT) during `openclaw doctor` in the upgrade flow does not abort the installer. (2) An unguarded `run_doctor` call site could set the dashboard flag even on doctor failure.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5 (Apple Silicon), bash 5.x (Homebrew), Node v24.15.0, npm 11.14.1, OpenClaw main at commit 9788648abd (rebased).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  Step 1. Sent SIGINT to the installer during the npm install phase of a real upgrade on macOS:
  ```bash
  set -m; (bash scripts/install.sh --no-onboard --no-prompt 2>&1) & PID=$!; sleep 2; kill -INT -- -$PID; wait $PID; echo "exit=$?"
  ```

  Step 2. Verified every `run_doctor` caller is guarded:
  ```bash
  grep -n 'run_doctor' scripts/install.sh
  ```
  All call sites use `if run_doctor; then` pattern, no bare `run_doctor` calls outside the function definition.

  Step 3. Ran focused tests covering doctor cancellation and dashboard guard:
  ```bash
  npx vitest run test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts --reporter verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "doctor|cancel|dashboard|guard"
  ```
  ```console
  ✓ guards every run_doctor caller against failure
  ✓ clears dashboard flag when doctor fails during upgrade
  ✓ propagates signal exit codes through run_quiet_step
  ✓ aborts on SIGINT (exit 130) from doctor
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the patched install script:

  SIGINT during the install phase triggers clean abort with exit 130:

  ```console
  $ set -m; (bash scripts/install.sh --no-onboard --no-prompt 2>&1) & PID=$!; sleep 2; kill -INT -- -$PID; wait $PID; echo "exit=$?"

  Preparing installer interface...

    OpenClaw Installer

  Detected: macos
  Existing OpenClaw installation detected, upgrading

  [1/3] Preparing environment
  Node.js v24.15.0 found
  Using Node.js runtime at /Users/user/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/node

  [2/3] Installing OpenClaw
  Git already installed
  Installing OpenClaw v2026.5.28
  Installing OpenClaw package

  ! Installation interrupted
  exit=130
  ```

  The focused behavioral scenarios verify the `run_doctor` guard at every call site via regex scanning, covering doctor cancellation, dashboard flag clearing, and signal propagation.
- **Observed result after fix:** SIGINT during install triggers clean abort with exit code 130 and "Installation interrupted" message. The dashboard is not opened after a failed or cancelled doctor run. All `run_doctor` call sites are guarded with conditional checks preventing the dashboard flag from being set on failure.
- **What was not tested:** Live upgrade with real `openclaw doctor` requiring user interaction at Clack prompts, followed by Ctrl+C at the interactive prompt.

target.title: fix(exec): hide policy controls from model args
target.body:
## Summary

- Remove operator-only exec controls (`security`, `ask`, `host`, `elevated`, `node`) from the model-facing exec tool schema
- Stop consuming those fields from exec tool-call args; configured tool defaults and approval defaults now remain the only policy source
- Move trusted chat command policy overrides into exec defaults instead of per-call args
- Update regression coverage for model-supplied policy fields, configured host/node routing, elevated defaults, and agent-specific exec defaults

Fixes #75811.

## Security

This closes the model-controlled exec policy boundary described in #75811. Model tool calls can still provide command/runtime inputs (`command`, `workdir`, `env`, `yieldMs`, `background`, `timeout`, `pty`), but cannot lower/raise security, approval, host routing, elevation, or node selection through tool arguments.

target.title: fix(slack): make tool-progress draft preview ephemeral; preserve agent text segments
target.body:
## Problem

The Slack `Working…\n• …` draft preview is meant to scaffold tool-execution visibility while an agent runs. Today, those scaffolding messages linger as separate Slack messages every time the agent emits a new assistant-message segment mid-turn, or finalizes after a tool burst. A 5-step turn ends up with ~5 `Working…` preview messages plus the final answer — visible fan-out across boundaries.

Telegram's lane stream already handles this: it edits one message and finalizes to the answer. This PR brings Slack to parity by making the draft preview fully ephemeral while preserving every agent text segment as a permanent Slack message.

## Approach

Tracks a per-draft kind: `'narrative' | 'bullets' | null`. Transitions:

| Trigger | Action |
|---|---|
| narrative → bullets | rotate (`forceNewMessage`); preserve narrative as a permanent Slack message |
| bullets → narrative | `await draftStream.clear()` (chat.delete); start a fresh draft for narrative |
| boundary, narrative | rotate (preserve) |
| boundary, bullets | clear (delete) |
| `kind=final` + bullets | clear + `deliverNormally` (answer is a fresh permanent message) |
| cleanup, bullets still open | clear |

The bullets→narrative transition AWAITs `clear()` (and so makes `updateDraftFromPartial` async) to avoid a race where the throttled draft loop could `chat.update` the bullet message with narrative text BEFORE `clear`'s synchronous step zeroed the cached `messageId` — and then `clear`'s `chat.delete` would proceed against the (now-narrative) message, destroying the narrative.

`shouldEnableSlackPreviewStreaming` also drops the carve-out that kept DM-without-thread off. Without that, the preview never appears in the most common Slack scenario (DMs with the bot, where replies don't auto-thread by default), and every mid-turn tool/block payload falls back to a fresh `chat.postMessage` instead.

## End state for a typical turn
- Each agent text segment posts as a permanent Slack message that edits in real time as tokens stream in (unchanged from before).
- Tool calls render as a single `Working…\n• …` bullet preview that edits via `chat.update` as new tools fire (unchanged from before).
- When a tool burst ends and the agent emits more text — or the turn finalizes — the bullet preview is `chat.delete`'d. The channel ends the turn with only the agent's text content.

## Tests
- Updated `dispatch.streaming.test.ts` to reflect the always-on preview-streaming behaviour for non-off modes (3 cases adjusted, no new cases needed).
- **89 files / 894 tests pass**.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` clean.

## Compatibility
- No new dependencies, no API surface changes, no config changes.
- The `streaming.preview.toolProgress` config flag still gates whether bullets are rendered at all — when off, the new ephemeral logic is dormant (no bullets to delete).
- Apps registered as Slack 'Agents & AI Apps' using `chat.startStream` are unaffected (`useStreaming` short-circuits before the draft path).

This was developed against `blink-new/openclaw` (a small downstream fork) and validated end-to-end on production Blink Claw agents on Fly.io across multiple iterations.

target.title: fix(feishu): parse interactive card post-format fallback content
target.body:
## Summary

Fix `parseInteractiveCardContent()` to handle Feishu API post-format fallback content for interactive card messages.

Fixes #60380
Related: #41609 #48281

## Problem

When reading card messages via `GET /im/v1/messages/{message_id}`, Feishu returns `body.content` in a **post-style fallback format** — nested arrays with `tag:"text"` elements — instead of the original card template JSON. The existing parser only handled card template format (`div`/`markdown` tags), causing all fallback content to silently return `"[Interactive Card]"`.

**Before:** Any `message(action=read)` on a card → `"[Interactive Card]"`
**After:** Extracts title + text content from both card template and post-format fallback

## Example

API returns this for a card message:
```json
{
  "title": "🤖 clawdbot",
  "elements": [[
    {"tag": "img", "image_key": "img_v3_02ad_..."},
    {"tag": "text", "text": "Daily report summary"},
    {"tag": "text", "text": "13 people missing reports"}
  ]]
}
```

- **Before:** `"[Interactive Card]"`
- **After:** `"🤖 clawdbot\nDaily report summary\n13 people missing reports"`

## Changes

**`extensions/feishu/src/send.ts`**
- Handle nested arrays in `elements` (post-format fallback `[[...]]`)
- Extract `tag:"text"` and `tag:"a"` elements from fallback content
- Extract card title from `header.title.content` (template) or top-level `title` (fallback)
- Support `tag:"plain_text"` elements
- Return title even when elements array is missing

**`extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts`**
- Add test: post-format fallback with nested arrays and title
- Add test: card template with `header.title.content`
- Add test: link extraction from `tag:"a"` elements with href

## Testing

- [x] Verified with real Feishu API responses from production bot
- [x] Existing card template format tests still pass (div/markdown)
- [x] 3 new test cases covering fallback scenarios

target.title: fix: harden subagent completion announce cleanup races
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a set of subagent completion lifecycle races that can leave completed child runs stuck behind cleanup/announce sequencing or overwrite a terminal error with a later success signal.

The patch makes three related changes:

- Do not block completion announce delivery on browser-session cleanup. The cleanup is now started after the announce/cleanup flow is leased and runs best-effort in the background, with warnings captured instead of blocking the lifecycle path.
- Skip waiting for an active embedded child session when a frozen final reply is already available. If we already captured the reply text, the requester can be notified immediately instead of waiting on a stale/active child run check.
- Preserve a terminal error outcome if a later completion signal reports success for the same run, unless the run was actually restarted after the error. This prevents cleanup/finalization races from turning a failed run into a successful task after the fact.

## Why this matters

The lifecycle path has a few asynchronous pieces that can finish in different orders:

1. child session/browser cleanup
2. completion announce delivery back to the requester
3. task finalization bookkeeping
4. embedded child-run settle checks
5. terminal lifecycle hooks

Before this patch, a slow or stuck browser cleanup could delay the announce/cleanup flow. Separately, if a child run had already produced frozen reply text but still appeared active, the announce flow could wait unnecessarily. And in a failure path, a later `complete` event could overwrite the already-recorded terminal error.

That combination makes subagent completion feel flaky from the user's side: the work is done, but the parent session may not receive the completion promptly, or the persisted final task state can become misleading.

## Implementation notes

### Lifecycle cleanup lease

`completeSubagentRun` now preclaims cleanup when `triggerCleanup` is true and cleanup has not already completed/been handled. That lease is passed into `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` as `skipBeginCleanup`, avoiding a second `beginSubagentCleanup` race while still protecting duplicate cleanup attempts.

If cleanup is not actually triggered, or announce is suppressed for steer-restart, the preclaim is released so the record does not stay permanently handled.

### Browser cleanup no longer blocks announce cleanup

Previously browser cleanup was awaited before starting the subagent announce cleanup flow. This patch starts the announce cleanup flow first, retires run-mode bundle MCP runtime as before, and then launches browser cleanup as a best-effort async task:

- uses injected `cleanupBrowserSessionsForLifecycleEnd` when provided
- lazy-loads the browser cleanup helper otherwise
- logs warning context on failure
- does not reject or delay completion delivery

### Frozen reply short-circuit

`runSubagentAnnounceFlow` now checks whether `roundOneReply` already contains non-empty frozen reply text. If it does, the flow does not wait for `waitForEmbeddedPiRunEnd` even if `isEmbeddedPiRunActive` reports true. The frozen reply is already the durable completion payload; waiting only increases the chance of a stale active-state race.

### Terminal error preservation

When a run already has a terminal error outcome/reason, a later successful completion signal no longer overwrites it unless the entry's `startedAt` proves the run restarted after the error's `endedAt`. The ended hook and persisted outcome use the preserved effective reason/outcome.

## Tests

Added/updated coverage for:

- announcing without waiting for browser cleanup
- skipping announce delivery when completion messages are disabled
- delete-mode cleanup when completion messages are disabled
- preserving terminal errors against later success completion
- allowing success to replace error only after a restart
- using frozen reply text without waiting on an active embedded child session

Verification run locally:

```bash
pnpm test src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts
```

Result: 2 files passed, 29 tests passed.

> AI-assisted. Tested on pushed branch `115daf62fe`: focused Vitest coverage for config/CLI/chat/TUI passed, config schema check passed, the real `pnpm openclaw experimental --help` entrypoint resolved, the interactive CLI picker wrote a throwaway config with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif), a real TUI selector flow has visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif), and a rebuilt Docker gateway exercised `/experimental` over WebSocket with persisted config writes.

## Summary

- **Problem:** OpenClaw has experimental config flags spread through the config schema, but the user-facing toggle surface was drifting toward a CLI-only implementation. That would make `/experimental` unavailable from chat/TUI/channel command surfaces and would duplicate the flag registry outside the config contract.
- **Why it matters:** Experimental flags affect runtime behavior and should use one bounded, auditable config subset across CLI, chat, TUI, and channel plugins instead of hard-coded per-surface lists.
- **What changed:** Added a shared experimental-flag helper that discovers boolean config leaves under `experimental` from the base config schema and schema UI hints. The CLI `openclaw experimental`, chat `/experimental`, TUI command handling, and channel-command registry now use that same subset.
- **Config command policy:** Added `commands.experimental` so `/experimental` can be enabled or disabled alongside other configurable command surfaces.
- **What did not change:** No new experimental flags were introduced. No secrets, network calls, or provider runtime behavior changed.

## Scope Note

This PR started as a small CLI picker, but review feedback exposed a broader product/security boundary: if experimental config can be edited from one surface, the implementation should not create a CLI-only registry or drift from chat, TUI, and channel command handling. The current shape is intentionally larger because it moves `/experimental` behind a shared schema-derived subset and a configurable command gate instead of adding another one-off config editor.

The scope is still kept to the minimum shared surface needed for that boundary: no new experimental flags, no arbitrary config editor, no new network/runtime capability, no SDK export, and no separate per-surface experimental registry.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI
- [x] Config
- [x] Messaging / channel command surface
- [x] TUI
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: Discord suggestion from Json in #general
- Reviewer concern addressed: Peter Steinberger asked that this avoid config-schema drift, consider `/experimental` in chat/TUI, and keep config editing security-bounded.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A - new feature and follow-up design correction.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Focused local tests cover the shared flag registry, CLI apply behavior, chat command parsing/gating, channel command listing, and TUI command handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New top-level command: `openclaw experimental`
- New slash command: `/experimental`
- TUI command handling can list and update experimental flags through the same shared registry
- `commands.experimental` controls whether the experimental chat/channel command is enabled
- Experimental flags are discovered from the config schema instead of duplicated in each surface

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Config schema + UI hints
        |
        v
shared experimental flag subset
        |
        +-- openclaw experimental
        +-- /experimental in chat/channel command handling
        +-- TUI command backend/handlers
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No. The command writes only existing config paths that users can already edit through config tooling.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, `/experimental` is a new configurable command surface. It is gated by `commands.experimental` and is limited to schema-discovered boolean config leaves whose path contains an `experimental` segment.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- Security boundary: this does not make arbitrary config editable from chat/TUI. The editable subset is derived from the config schema and narrowed to experimental boolean leaves only.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu source checkout in Codex worktree
- Runtime: Node 22 / pnpm
- Branch SHA: `115daf62fe`
- Docker proof image: `openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest` (image `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`)
- Docker proof gateway: `pr76298-exp-proof-gateway`, `127.0.0.1:19982->18789`, health `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`

### Commands run locally

```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=180000 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/config/experimental-flags.test.ts src/config/experimental-config-file.test.ts src/commands/experimental.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-gating.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-parse.test.ts src/tui/commands.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
pnpm config:schema:check
pnpm openclaw experimental --help
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json pnpm openclaw experimental
jq . /tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json
OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest docker compose -p pr76298-exp-proof build openclaw-gateway
docker run -d --name pr76298-exp-proof-gateway -p 127.0.0.1:19982:18789 ... openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest node dist/index.js gateway --bind lan --port 18789
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:19982/healthz
docker exec pr76298-exp-proof-gateway node --input-type=module -e '<WebSocket /experimental proof script>'
jq . /tmp/openclaw-pr76298-docker-proof/config/openclaw.json
```

### Expected

The schema-derived experimental flag subset is stable, CLI/chat/TUI surfaces use it consistently, `/experimental` obeys command gating, the real CLI entrypoint resolves the new command, and a gateway client with the right operator scopes can list and toggle the bounded experimental config subset.

### Actual

Verified on pushed branch `115daf62fe`.

## Evidence

- [x] Focused Vitest run passed 5 shards / 128 tests after the final type-only commit.
- [x] `git diff --check` passed before the final commit; `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed for the final type-only commit.
- [x] `pnpm config:schema:check` passed (`[base-config-schema] ok`).
- [x] `pnpm openclaw experimental --help` executed through the real repo CLI entrypoint at SHA `c354c59`.
- [x] Interactive CLI picker executed on head `115daf6`, selected `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, printed the updated-config summary, and persisted the expected throwaway config. Slow VHS GIF: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif
- [x] TUI selector proof executed through the real `runTui` loop with an isolated fixture backend. The TUI opened `/experimental`, listed experimental flags, selected `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, printed `experimental enabled: agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, and logged `setExperimentalFlag`. VHS GIF: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif
- [x] Docker image rebuilt after the final commit and the proof container ran from image `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`.
- [x] Dockered gateway health returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`.
- [x] WebSocket `chat.send` proof through the gateway returned real `Config updated` replies for `/experimental off tools.experimental.planTool` and `/experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`.
- [x] Persisted config contains `tools.experimental.planTool: false`, `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean: true`, and `meta.lastTouchedVersion: "2026.5.22"`.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: schema-derived flag listing/apply helpers, CLI command behavior, chat parse/gating/list/set behavior, TUI command handling, embedded trusted-local TUI config path, live CLI registration/help output, interactive CLI picker write behavior, real TUI selector behavior with an isolated backend, and Dockered gateway command behavior over WebSocket.
- Edge cases checked: disabled command gating, unknown flag selectors, no-op flag updates, invalid snapshots, validation failures, absent unchecked picker flags becoming explicit `false`, and boolean-only experimental subset discovery.
- What was not verified: native Telegram visible proof. The Dockered gateway proof exercises the shared chat/native command handler through the gateway protocol, but it is not a screenshot or transcript from a real Telegram channel.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: `openclaw experimental` and `/experimental` now use one schema-derived, bounded experimental config subset across CLI, chat/gateway, TUI, and channel-command handling. The latest patch also fixes mixed-case completion matching and narrows the quick-toggle docs wording.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu source checkout at `115daf62fe`; Docker gateway behavior proof captured at `c354c598f7` before the final type-only TUI backend interface commit; rebuilt Docker gateway image `openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest` with image id `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`; container `pr76298-exp-proof-gateway` bound to `127.0.0.1:19982->18789` with isolated config under `/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-docker-proof/config/openclaw.json`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran focused Vitest through `node scripts/test-projects.mjs`, `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`, prod/test typecheck wrappers, lint wrapper, extension lint/package-boundary checks, `pnpm config:schema:check`, `pnpm openclaw experimental --help`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json pnpm openclaw experimental`, rebuilt the Docker gateway with `OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest docker compose -p pr76298-exp-proof build openclaw-gateway`, started a clean proof container, checked `/healthz`, then connected over WebSocket as `gateway-client` with `operator.write` and `operator.admin` scopes and sent `/experimental off tools.experimental.planTool`, `/experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, and `/experimental list`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Focused Vitest after final commit: passed 5 shards / 128 tests
Prod typecheck after final commit: core and extensions passed
Test typecheck after final commit: core and extensions passed
Lint after final commit: core/extensions/scripts passed
Extension boundary after final commit: channel lint, bundled lint, package compile, and package canary passed
Schema check: [base-config-schema] ok
CLI help: OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (c354c59), Usage: openclaw experimental [options]
Gateway health: {"ok":true,"status":"live"}
CONNECT {"ok":true,"scopes":["operator.write","operator.admin"]}
FINAL /experimental off tools.experimental.planTool: Config updated: tools.experimental.planTool=false
FINAL /experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean: Config updated: agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean=true
Interactive CLI picker slow VHS proof: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif
TUI selector VHS proof: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif
TUI backend log: listExperimentalFlags returned the three schema-derived flags; setExperimentalFlag wrote agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean=true changed=true.
Interactive CLI picker on head 115daf6:
Updated config:
  enabled agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean
  disabled agents.defaults.memorySearch.experimental.sessionMemory
  disabled tools.experimental.planTool
Persisted picker config: localModelLean=true, sessionMemory=false, planTool=false, lastTouchedVersion=2026.5.22
FINAL /experimental list:
- on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean - Enable Lean Local Model Mode (Experimental)
- off agents.defaults.memorySearch.experimental.sessionMemory - Memory Search Session Index (Experimental)
- off tools.experimental.planTool - Enable Structured Plan Tool
```

Persisted config excerpt after the gateway proof:

```json
{
  "commands": { "experimental": true },
  "tools": { "experimental": { "planTool": false } },
  "meta": {
    "lastTouchedVersion": "2026.5.22",
    "lastTouchedAt": "2026-05-24T03:40:31.959Z"
  },
  "agents": {
    "defaults": { "experimental": { "localModelLean": true } }
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real CLI entrypoint resolves `openclaw experimental`; the interactive CLI picker writes the expected throwaway config with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif); the TUI selector opens and writes the expected experimental flag with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif); focused config/CLI/chat/TUI tests pass; the Dockered gateway accepts a scoped backend connection and the `/experimental` command path writes the expected bounded config values to disk.
- What was not tested: Native Telegram visible proof was requested from Mantis after this run and is pending. Broad remote Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` proof was attempted earlier, but the local Crabbox binary is missing, so CI on this pushed SHA is the current broad gate.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Yes, adds optional `commands.experimental` command-gating config.
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Chat/TUI config editing could accidentally grow into arbitrary config mutation.
  - **Mitigation:** The shared helper only exposes schema-discovered boolean leaves under an `experimental` path segment. `/experimental` is also gated by `commands.experimental`.
- **Risk:** Future experimental flags could drift from one surface.
  - **Mitigation:** CLI, chat/channel commands, and TUI all read from the same schema-derived helper instead of maintaining separate flag lists.

## AI/Vibe-Coded Checklist

- [x] AI-assisted (marked above)
- [x] Tested (focused local tests plus real CLI entrypoint proof)
- [x] I understand what the code does

target.title: fix(doctor): clarify mcp allowlist diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

Doctor/tool-policy diagnostics can currently misclassify allowlisted MCP tool name patterns as generic missing plugin tools, even when the corresponding `mcp.servers.<name>` entry is configured.

This PR makes the diagnostics recognize configured MCP server prefixes using the same sanitized server-name convention as bundle MCP tool names. When a restrictive tool allowlist references a configured MCP server pattern such as `messaging__*`, doctor now reports MCP-specific guidance instead of only saying the plugin may be disabled.

## Scope

- Recognize configured `mcp.servers.*` prefixes in doctor allowlist warnings.
- Update the generic unknown-tool suffix to mention MCP servers as well as plugins.
- Add focused tests for the doctor warning and tool-policy warning wording.

## Non-goals

- Does not start MCP servers.
- Does not change runtime MCP materialization.
- Does not broaden tool permissions.
- Does not change plugin allow semantics.
- Does not change generated config schema or docs baselines.

## Security

Diagnostics-only. This does not expose new tools, add permissions, alter credential handling, or change MCP process startup behavior.

## Validation

```sh
pnpm test src/agents/tool-policy-pipeline.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/plugin-tool-allowlist-warnings.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/agents/tool-policy-pipeline.ts src/agents/tool-policy-pipeline.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/plugin-tool-allowlist-warnings.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/plugin-tool-allowlist-warnings.test.ts
git diff --check
```

## Summary
- add a typed `PromptBuildScopeEnvelope` session metadata object
- persist/patch `scopeEnvelope` through session state and gateway session patch APIs
- pass `scopeEnvelope` into `before_prompt_build` so prompt-build hooks can receive structured workspace/task scope without parsing prompt text

## Validation

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `before_prompt_build` hooks now receive session `scopeEnvelope` metadata across session-backed paths, including isolated cron embedded-agent runs, while docs/changelog/plugin SDK API metadata stay in sync.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Node v22.22.0 and pnpm 10.29.3, repaired PR branch based on current upstream main. Secrets, user identifiers, private paths, and deployment-specific names omitted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  2. Ran focused agent runner, gateway session patch, auto-reply, cron session-key, and cron owner-auth tests.

target.title: perf: optimize agent tool result middleware runtime check
target.body:
Replaces an $O(N \times M)$ array lookup with an $O(N + M)$ Set-based lookup when filtering runtimes against declared middleware in the plugin registry. This improves efficiency during plugin registration.

## Summary

- Problem: Suboptimal O(N*M) data structure inside an array filter in the plugin registry, and CI failures in GitHub Actions workflows when secrets are missing.
- Why it matters: Improves performance during plugin registration and ensures CI stability for external contributors/forks.
- What changed: 
    - Optimized `src/plugins/registry.ts` to use a `Set` for runtime lookups.
    - Updated `auto-response.yml`, `labeler.yml`, and `stale.yml` to gracefully handle missing App secrets.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Core plugin registration logic and workflow automation functionality remains identical.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes # (N/A)
- Related # (N/A)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (CI failure)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Manual logic review of YAML changes and code review of TS changes.
- Edge cases checked: Empty secrets in workflows, small vs large arrays in registry.
- What you did **not** verify: Actual execution of GitHub Actions (not possible in sandbox).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes` - added guards for empty secrets)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Changes only affect how CI handles missing secrets; it falls back to standard `github.token` which is safer for public forks.

target.title: fix(slack): route block-action events to thread sessions
target.body:
## Summary
- Route Slack block-action button clicks through the normal inbound Slack message path so the owning agent session processes the interaction.
- Preserve the clicked message/thread ownership for interactive replies, including replies sent through the Slack `message` tool and threaded DMs.
- Wake the targeted thread session when a block action arrives so queued interaction events are drained instead of waiting for a later heartbeat.
- Keep Slack reply delivery anchored to the real thread root after synthetic click turns, including `[[reply_to_current]]` replies.
- Preserve completed assistant session tails so a delivered Slack reply remains visible to the next button-click turn.
- Add prompt guidance and regression coverage for Slack button rendering/selection behavior.

## Why These Fixes Are Needed
Slack interactive buttons were failing in several separate layers. A click could be received and acknowledged by Slack but still not wake the correct `:thread:<ts>` session. In other cases, the click reached the agent but replies were delivered to the DM/base session instead of the originating thread. Once routing worked, a second issue appeared: completed assistant replies at the end of a session file were being trimmed by session repair, so the next click turn did not see the reply that Slack users had already seen.

The result was an unreliable end-to-end button flow: clicks sometimes did nothing, sometimes replied in the wrong place, and sometimes caused repeated or stale wording because local transcript history had lost the previous assistant response.

## Prior Work
This PR incorporates and extends the direction from earlier Slack interaction work:
- #61374 covered part of the targeted wake/session-key problem for Slack block actions, but did not cover the full click-to-agent routing path, button selection update, threaded reply delivery, threaded DM tool sends, or assistant-tail preservation.
- #74336 was a narrower closed attempt around thread-aware block-action wake/session resolution.
- #71025 is adjacent work for Slack exec approval buttons, but not the generic interactive reply button flow.
- #72626 is adjacent presentation work around preserving button blocks, but not the interaction routing/wake/history path.

The recommendation is to merge this PR once polished because it covers the full end-to-end Slack button path and has been verified against real Slack interactions locally, while the prior PRs each cover only part of the observed failure mode.

## Current Status
This is intentionally still a draft. The current branch captures the working end-to-end fix from local Slack testing. I plan to review it tomorrow and try to make the final change set more minimal before requesting review.

## Verification
- Real Slack e2e testing with threaded button clicks, selected-button ack/update, threaded replies, and follow-up click turns.
- `pnpm test src/agents/session-file-repair.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/slack/src/monitor/events/interactions.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/replies.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/slack/src/monitor/replies.test.ts extensions/slack/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/slack/src/threading-tool-context.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/events/interactions.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts -t "Slack interactive reply hints"`
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm build`

The branch adds WhatsApp `replyToOfflineMessages` and `offlineMessageMaxAgeSeconds` config, wires it through account resolution and append-upsert handling, and updates tests, generated config metadata, docs, and `CHANGELOG.md`.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): add replyToOfflineMessages config to process missed messages after reconnect
target.body:
## Problem

Messages received during WhatsApp disconnects are **silently lost**. When the gateway reconnects, offline messages arrive with `upsert.type === "append"` (history sync) and are **skipped** by default.

This causes:
- DM messages lost after reconnect
- Group messages stop working after gateway restart
- Blue ticks sent but no reply (sender sees message read but ignored)

**15+ issues** trace back to the same `append` skip.

## Solution

Add opt-in config to process offline messages:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    whatsapp: {
      replyToOfflineMessages: true,
      offlineMessageMaxAgeSeconds: 300, // default: 300 (5 minutes)
    },
  },
}
```

- `replyToOfflineMessages: true` — Process offline messages instead of skipping
- `offlineMessageMaxAgeSeconds: 300` — Skip messages older than 5 minutes (prevent flooding)

## Related Issues

- **#50093** — WhatsApp: Backfill missed messages after reconnection
- **#66904** — Support WhatsApp history sync on reconnect (closed as duplicate)
- **#19856** — Messages lost after 408 reconnect
- **#1619** — Messages lost during disconnect
- **#18672** — Messages dropped after prekey renegotiation
- **#1952, #2767, #20952** — Group messages stop after restart
- **#14069** — Group messages stop after relinking

## Based On

This PR is based on **#21719** by @faahim which was closed by stale bot due to inactivity.

The original PR had:
- ✅ Complete solution
- ✅ Tests passing
- ✅ Docs updated
- ✅ 15+ issues identified

## Changes

1. `src/config/types.whatsapp.ts` — Add config fields
2. `extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.ts` — Add resolved fields
3. `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts` — Implement logic
4. `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts` — Pass options

## Default Behavior

**Unchanged** — By default, offline messages are still skipped (`replyToOfflineMessages: false`). Users must opt-in to process them.

---

**Context:** OpenClaw WhatsApp bundled user since 2026.3.13, currently on latest version.

target.title: fix(cli): resolve SecretRefs in capability web search CLI path
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw capability web search --query "hello"` crashes with "unresolved SecretRef" when the API key is a file SecretRef
- **Why it matters:** CLI capability commands are unusable for any user storing secrets via SecretRef — the gateway resolves SecretRefs at startup, but the CLI path never does
- **What changed:** `runWebSearchCommand` now calls `resolveCommandConfigWithSecrets` + `setRuntimeConfigSnapshot` before invoking the runtime, matching the pattern used by `resolveAgentRuntimeConfig` and `loadModelsConfigWithSource`
- **What did NOT change:** Gateway path, other CLI capability commands, `isPluginWebCredentialTargetId` (used by agent runtime), or any runtime resolution logic

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `runWebSearchCommand` passed raw `getRuntimeConfig()` to `runWebSearch()`. `selectApplicableRuntimeConfig` (runtime-snapshot.ts:219) returns stale unresolved `runtimeConfig` when `runtimeSourceConfig` is null (CLI scenario), ignoring `inputConfig` entirely.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** CLI capability commands had no SecretRef resolution step; only `resolveAgentRuntimeConfig` and `loadModelsConfigWithSource` did.
- **Contributing context:** `selectApplicableRuntimeConfig` was designed for gateway-served runtime configs; CLI never registered a snapshot source, so the "prefer runtime over input" heuristic broke the CLI scenario.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- **Target test or file:** `src/cli/command-secret-targets.import.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `getWebSearchCommandSecretTargetIds()` returns `tools.web.search.apiKey` + plugin `webSearch.apiKey` entries but not `webFetch.apiKey`
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Full CLI integration with real SecretRefs requires filesystem secrets; the unit test verifies the target-ID plumbing `resolveCommandConfigWithSecrets` uses
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A — new test added

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `openclaw capability web search --query "..."` now works when API key is a file SecretRef (previously crashed)
- No config/env changes; no default behavior changes for plaintext keys

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[CLI] -> getRuntimeConfig() -> runWebSearch(config: rawCfg)
  -> selectApplicableRuntimeConfig() -> runtimeSourceConfig=null
  -> returns stale runtimeConfig -> CRASH: "unresolved SecretRef"

After:
[CLI] -> getRuntimeConfig() -> resolveCommandConfigWithSecrets(targetIds)
  -> effectiveConfig (SecretRefs resolved)
  -> setRuntimeConfigSnapshot(cfg, rawConfig)
  -> runWebSearch(config: undefined) -> reads resolved snapshot -> SUCCESS
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **Yes** — CLI now resolves existing SecretRefs via the same infrastructure the gateway uses; no new secret paths accessed
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **No**

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Node 22+)
- Runtime/container: `pnpm openclaw capability web search` (CLI-only, no gateway)
- Model/provider: Brave web search
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.web.search.apiKey: "file:~/.openclaw/secrets/brave-api-key"`

### Steps

1. Configure `tools.web.search.apiKey` as a file SecretRef
2. Run `pnpm openclaw capability web search --query "hello"`

### Expected

Search results from the configured provider

### Actual (before fix)

`Error: tools.web.search.apiKey: unresolved SecretRef "file:.../brave-api-key"`

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets — before: "unresolved SecretRef" error; after: Brave search results returned
- [x] Unit tests pass: `command-secret-targets.import.test.ts`, `command-secret-resolution.coverage.test.ts`

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** `pnpm openclaw capability web search --query "hello"` with file SecretRef Brave API key returns search results; build passes
- **Edge cases checked:** `webFetch.apiKey` plugin entries excluded from web-search targetIds (test asserts `false`)
- **What did NOT verify:** env-style SecretRef (`env:VAR_NAME`), Gemini fallback via `models.providers.google.apiKey`, other capability commands

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** `setRuntimeConfigSnapshot` swaps the global snapshot without restore
  - **Mitigation:** CLI process exits after the command; no subsequent commands affected. Same pattern as `resolveAgentRuntimeConfig` and `loadModelsConfigWithSource`

target.title: feat(control-ui): add right-click Reply in Dashboard webchat
target.body:
## Summary - Adds right-click Reply to messages in the Dashboard / Control UI webchat - Right-click any chat bubble → "Reply" option → reply preview bar appears above the composer → type reply → message auto-prepended with Markdown blockquote - Click ✕ on the preview bar or press Escape to cancel the reply ### How it works Three new optional props on `ChatProps` (`replyTarget`, `onSetReply`, `onClearReply`) wired through `app-render.ts` into `renderChat`. A `@state() chatReplyTarget` on the app class holds the transient reply target. On send, `handleSendChat` reads the target, prepends a Markdown blockquote via `prependReplyQuote`, and clears the state. ### Quote format (what the model receives) | Quoted message | Your reply | Sent to model | |---|---|---| | `hello world` | `got it` | `> **User:** hello world`

`got it` | | `line one`
`line two` | `ok` | `> **User:**`
`> line one`
`> line two`

`ok` | ## Linked context Closes #16896 ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Users previously had to manually copy-paste messages to reference them. Now right-click → Reply sets a reply target with a visual preview, and the message is automatically quoted on send. - **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw dev build (vite), WSL2 Ubuntu 22.04 on Windows 11, Chromium - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. `cd ui && pnpm dev` 2. Right-click a chat bubble → context menu shows speech-bubble icon + "Reply" 3. Click "Reply" → preview bar appears above composer with sender name, quoted text, and visible ✕ 4. Type a response and send 5. Sent message includes `> **User:** ...` Markdown blockquote 6. Click ✕ or press Escape → preview bar dismissed, plain send unaffected - **Evidence after fix:** - **Observed result after fix:** Right-click reply works end-to-end. Context menu shows proper icon. Preview bar appears with sender name, quoted text, and visible dismiss button. Quoted message is prepended with correct Markdown format on send. Dismiss clears the state. Plain send without reply target is unaffected. - **What was not tested:** Long message truncation in preview (>120 chars), interaction with slash commands while reply target is active, queueing behavior when chat is busy - **Before evidence:** Before this change, the Control UI had no reply affordance — users had to manually copy text and paste with `>` prefix. ## Files changed ``` ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts — prependReplyQuote + queue ordering fix ui/src/ui/app-render.ts — wire replyTarget/onSetReply/onClearReply into renderChat ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts — add chatReplyTarget to AppViewState type ui/src/ui/app.ts — add @state() chatReplyTarget to OpenClawApp ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts — context-menu handler, reply preview bar, ChatProps additions ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css — reply preview bar + context menu styles ``` ## Risk checklist - **User-visible behavior change:** Yes — new right-click Reply in Control UI - **Config / migration / security / network:** No changes - **Highest-risk area:** `bubble.textContent` extraction for quote text; mitigated by `.trim().slice(0, 500)` - **Risk mitigation:** Reply target is transient state only, cleared on send or dismiss. No persistence.

target.title: fix: add --timeout flag to openclaw message send with SIGTERM support
target.body:
## Summary

Add a `--timeout <ms>` flag to `openclaw message send` (default: 30000ms) so that delivery requests can no longer hang indefinitely. Also adds SIGTERM/SIGINT handling in `executeGatewayRequestWithScopes` with proper listener cleanup so external supervisors like `GNU timeout(1)` can cleanly interrupt stuck requests.

## Problem

When `openclaw message send` calls the gateway and the channel delivery hangs (e.g. Telegram rate limit, network issue), the CLI process:
- Has no internal timeout — waits forever
- Ignores SIGTERM from external `timeout` command
- Consumes 60-70% CPU indefinitely

**Impact:** Cron jobs calling `openclaw message send` become unsafe; one stuck delivery can cascade into gateway/agent pileups.

## Fix

1. **register.send.ts** — Add `--timeout <ms>` option (default: 30000)
2. **helpers.ts** — Parse timeout, validate (reject NaN/negative/zero), pass as `timeoutMs`
3. **message.ts** — Inject `timeoutMs` into the gateway params passed to `runMessageAction`
4. **call.ts** — Add SIGTERM/SIGINT handler in `executeGatewayRequestWithScopes` using `process.on` (not `process.once`) with proper `process.off` cleanup in the `stop` function so listeners don't leak after each gateway call
5. **call.test.ts** — Add test verifying signal listeners are disposed after gateway request settles
6. **CHANGELOG.md**, **docs/cli/message.md** — Document the new flag

## Testing

### Unit tests

```
pnpm test src/gateway/call.test.ts src/cli/program/message/helpers.test.ts src/commands/message.test.ts

✓ gateway (79 tests) — includes "disposes SIGTERM/SIGINT listeners after gateway request settles"
✓ cli (23 tests)
✓ commands (5 tests)
```

### Signal listener cleanup (standalone proof)

Without cleanup, listeners persist after normal path:
```
onHelloOk -> stop() -> resolve() -> SIGTERM/SIGINT remain registered = leak
```

Proof that cleanup works:
```
$ node -e "
const h = () => {};
const bS = process.listenerCount('SIGTERM');  // 0
const bI = process.listenerCount('SIGINT');   // 0
process.on('SIGTERM', h); process.on('SIGINT', h);
process.off('SIGTERM', h); process.off('SIGINT', h);
console.log('SIGTERM:', process.listenerCount('SIGTERM')); // 0
console.log('SIGINT:', process.listenerCount('SIGINT'));   // 0
"
SIGTERM: 0
SIGINT: 0
```

### Timeout input validation

| Input | Result |
|-------|--------|
| `--timeout 5000` | timeoutMs: 5000 |
| `--timeout -100` | timeoutMs: undefined (falls to default 30000) |
| `--timeout 0` | timeoutMs: undefined (falls to default 30000) |
| `--timeout abc` | timeoutMs: undefined (falls to default 30000) |

Fixes #75895

target.title: feat(memory): add RRF hybrid fusion mode for memory search
target.body:
Adds configurable fusion strategy (weighted | rrf) for memory search via agents.defaults.memorySearch.query.hybrid.fusion. Default: weighted (preserves existing behavior). RRF uses reciprocal rank fusion with configurable k parameter for combining semantic and keyword results.

## Summary

- **Problem:** Hybrid memory search always merges semantic + keyword results with a weighted score blend. This can be brittle when vector and BM25 scores live on different scales or distributions.
- **Why it matters:** Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) is a standard IR technique that merges rankings instead of raw scores, which handles score-scale mismatches better than weighted combination in some workloads.
- **What changed:** Adds an optional hybrid fusion mode (`weighted` | `rrf`) controlled by config, defaulting to `weighted` so existing deployments are unchanged. Implements RRF in the bundled `memory-core` extension and documents behavior in a contract doc.
- **What did NOT change:** Default fusion remains `weighted`. No change to embedding providers, memory storage format, or non-hybrid search paths.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX

target.title: Polish Workboard operations view
target.body:
## Summary
- polish the Workboard operations UI across card layout, filters, health metrics, details, responsive behavior, dropdowns, and tooltips
- add guarded auto-refresh, serialized writes, linked-task reconciliation, bounded task discovery, and safer start/stop behavior
- preserve accessible names, selected custom-select values, tooltip viewport behavior, and native-title fallback semantics
- expand focused controller/view/tooltip/lifecycle coverage and deterministic Workboard browser E2E

## Maintenance repairs
- rebased onto current Workboard base behavior while preserving main's hide-empty-column and accessibility changes
- deferred failed lifecycle refresh retries, preserved matching task links on enrichment failure, and committed poll results atomically so in-flight polls cannot disrupt drag-and-drop
- hardened canonical/replacement task matching, terminal discovery cursor cycling, unresolved-start tracking, and task/session stop fallback behavior
- kept session-only lifecycle reconciliation available when best-effort task discovery fails, while limiting unresolved task Live/Stop controls to running cards
- retried current lifecycle reconciliation after a shared task refresh is invalidated by a concurrent card write
- cleared obsolete task-refresh errors when refreshed cards no longer need task data, and hid dismissed floating tooltips from assistive technology
- required linked-session abort success when a recorded task is already missing, preventing false blocked-state writeback
- honored task-refresh backoff for session-only cards while retaining session reconciliation, and preserved cached summaries on partial full-refresh confirmation failures
- re-armed polling across leave/re-enter teardown, cleared stopped-poll loading state, and discarded in-flight polls that overlap opening an edit draft
- removed an unreachable same-card details toggle while keeping the modal/inert interaction model intact
- hardened shared floating tooltip cleanup, viewport placement, accessible descriptions, and inherited native-title suppression
- made custom-select triggers announce both their field and selected value
- handled successful missing-task cancellations and exact task confirmations without stale lifecycle retries
- merged current `upstream/main` and regenerated the conflicting locale metadata
- detached stale lifecycle loads, cleared recovered dispatch refresh errors, and fully stopped in-flight polling when Workboard becomes inactive
- marked the newly added Workboard strings as English fallbacks across all generated locale metadata until translated
- merged current `upstream/main` again and regenerated the locale metadata conflicts against the latest Control UI strings

- serialized queued forced refresh contracts so stronger diagnostics/task refresh requests cannot be silently dropped
- preserved newer unrelated refresh errors when lifecycle task refresh later recovers
- restored Workboard fallback markers across all generated locale metadata after current-main regeneration
- detached invalidated Workboard load ownership so writes can immediately unblock replacement refresh and reconciliation

- cleared stale cards-list errors after successful passive polling while preserving newer mutation errors, and recorded load-time task failures for lifecycle recovery
- invalidated loaded Workboard snapshots during lifecycle teardown so reconnect or re-enable performs a fresh card load

## Verification
Proof was run on exact pushed head `06a5b86ad66a4b26749ecb897c60bfa1775481b0`.

- `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD` - clean against current `upstream/main` `6134c00657a247d6f7accc9182d0b901c4f26b48`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- focused UI suite - 7 files, 315 tests passed
- Workboard browser E2E - 2 files, 2 tests passed
- UI test typecheck passed
- `node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check` - all 18 locales clean with 63 tracked fallback entries per locale
- scoped `oxfmt --check` and `oxlint` passed
- trusted host-broker structured autoreview - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- current-head GitHub CI is running after the final push; no completion claim is made here

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Workboard operations view polish and lifecycle safety. Final maintenance repairs clear recovered cards-list failures without hiding newer mutation errors, make task-enrichment failures recoverable by lifecycle refresh, and force a fresh card load after Workboard teardown or re-enable.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at exact pushed head `06a5b86ad66a4b26749ecb897c60bfa1775481b0` on macOS with Node 24, including deterministic Workboard browser E2E and focused controller/view/tooltip/lifecycle execution. No protected channel, configured binding, production Gateway, or private card fixture was mutated.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD`
  - `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
  - `node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check`
  - `node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/dom-tooltips.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-tooltip-lifecycle.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`
  - `node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/workboard.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/workboard-status-persistence.e2e.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxlint ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: Exact-head terminal output recorded a clean merge-tree and diff/format/lint checks, 7 focused UI files with 315 passing tests, 2 Workboard browser E2E files with 2 passing tests, all 18 generated locales clean with 63 tracked fallbacks each, a passing UI test typecheck, and a clean trusted structured autoreview. Controller regressions prove recovered load errors clear after reconnect, newer mutation errors remain visible, load-time task failures enter lifecycle recovery, and a previously loaded board relists cards after teardown.
- Existing visible Workboard recordings and screenshots from this PR branch remain representative of the operations UI:
  - writable flow recording: https://files.catbox.moe/ypr4k0.webm
  - read-only flow recording: https://files.catbox.moe/eefb2b.webm
  - create dialog with selected custom-select values: https://files.catbox.moe/11arf3.png
  - edited card: https://files.catbox.moe/wm0z3d.png
  - dragged card in Running: https://files.catbox.moe/83j0e2.png
  - lifecycle-driven Review state: https://files.catbox.moe/fiwucr.png
  - reloaded Review state with shared Refresh tooltip: https://files.catbox.moe/yva58n.png
  - read-only board: https://files.catbox.moe/85ysyv.png
  - edit/reopen persistence: https://files.catbox.moe/plruvi.png
  - drag/status persistence: https://files.catbox.moe/lm7h10.png
- Observed result after fix: The exact PR head merges cleanly with current main; successful passive polls clear only superseded cards-list failures; lifecycle refresh can recover load-time task failures; teardown invalidates loaded card snapshots; and all focused/browser proof passes.
- What was not tested: No production Gateway or private card contents were dumped. Blacksmith Testbox warmup could not resolve the isolated cache's git remote URL before allocating a lease, so no remote-proof claim is made. No live channel mutation, QA fixture, Crabbox, or Mantis target was used.

target.body:
● - [x] Add FIELD_ADVANCED set in schema.hints.ts to mark technical config fields (auth cooldowns, gateway infra knobs, diagnostics subsections, etc.) as advanced, so the config form can hide
   them by default
  - [x] Add docLink field to ConfigUiHint type for future per-field docs links
  - [x] In config-form.node.ts renderObject, split top-level section fields into basic (visible by default) and advanced (collapsed <details> disclosure) when no search is active; search
  bypasses the split so all matching fields are always visible
  - [x] Add renderHelpBlock helper that renders help text, doc link, and tags together; render the Docs ↗ anchor when hints.docLink is set
  - [x] Add CSS for .cfg-advanced-section disclosure and .cfg-field__doc-link

  ## Summary

  - **Problem:** The config UI shows all settings at the same level — basic toggles sit alongside low-level infra knobs (auth backoff timers, gateway health-check intervals, otel settings),
  making the page feel dense and hard to navigate for non-expert users.
  - **Why it matters:** New users opening the config page see 50+ fields at once with no visual hierarchy; important settings like log level or update channel are buried next to
  rarely-touched tuning parameters.

  bypassed when a search query is active so all matching fields remain discoverable. The `ConfigUiHint` type gains an optional `docLink` field; when set, a `Docs ↗` anchor is rendered inline

target.title: feat(feishu): add topicRequireMention for topic thread mention control
target.body:
Summary

  - Problem: requireMention in Feishu groups applies equally to normal group messages and topic thread replies, with no way to separate the two. Users who want to require @mention for new
  conversations but allow free replies inside started threads cannot do so.
  - Why it matters: In busy group chats, requiring @mention on every topic reply is noisy and breaks the natural conversation flow inside a thread.
  - What changed: Added optional topicRequireMention boolean at top-level, per-account, and per-group config levels. When a message has thread_id set (Feishu topic thread),
  topicRequireMention takes precedence over requireMention. Falls back to existing requireMention cascade when unset.
  - What did NOT change: DM behavior, topic session routing (groupSessionScope, topicSessionMode), replyInThread, broadcast dispatch, and all other group access-control logic are unchanged.

  - Coverage level:
    - Unit test ✓
    - Seam / integration test
    - End-to-end test
    - Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file: extensions/feishu/src/policy.test.ts, extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts, extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts
  - Scenario the test should lock in: topic thread messages bypass mention gate when topicRequireMention=false; non-topic messages are unaffected by topicRequireMention
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: policy resolution is pure and fully unit-testable; bot-level integration test covers the full dispatch path
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
  - If no new test is added, why not: Tests were added (6 unit + 2 integration + 5 schema)

  User-visible / Behavior Changes

  New optional config keys:

  - channels.feishu.topicRequireMention — top-level override for topic thread messages
  - channels.feishu.groups.<chat_id>.topicRequireMention — per-group override

  When unset, behavior is identical to today. No defaults changed.

  Diagram (if applicable)

  Before:
  [group message] -> requireMention check -> respond / drop

  After:
  [group message, thread_id set] -> topicRequireMention (if set) -> respond / drop
                                                 ↓ (fallback)
  [group message, no thread_id]  -> requireMention check          -> respond / drop

  Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? No
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
  - New/changed network calls? No
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? No
  - Data access scope changed? No

  Repro + Verification

  Environment

  - OS: Linux
  - Runtime/container: Node 22
  - Model/provider: any
  - Integration/channel: Feishu (websocket mode)
  - Relevant config (redacted):

  {
    channels: {
      feishu: {
        groupPolicy: "allowlist",
        groups: {
          "oc_xxx": {
            requireMention: true,
            topicRequireMention: false,
          },
        },
      },
    },
  }

  Steps

  1. Configure group with requireMention: true, topicRequireMention: false
  2. Send a normal group message without @mention → bot should not respond
  3. Send a message inside a topic thread without @mention → bot should respond

  Expected

  - Step 2: no response
  - Step 3: bot responds

  Actual

  - Verified via unit and integration tests (712 passed)

  Evidence

  - Failing test/log before + passing after — 6 new unit tests in policy.test.ts, 2 integration tests in bot.test.ts, 5 schema tests in config-schema.test.ts, all passing (712 total)

  Human Verification (required)

  - Verified scenarios: policy resolution cascade (per-group beats top-level, topic beats non-topic, DM short-circuit), schema acceptance/rejection at all three levels, bot dispatch gate with
   and without thread_id
  - Edge cases checked: topicRequireMention absent → full backwards compat; DM with both knobs set → still false; wildcard group entry with topicRequireMention
  - What you did not verify: live Feishu WebSocket delivery (no test credentials available)

  Review Conversations

  - I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

  Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? Yes
  - Config/env changes? Yes — two new optional config keys (topicRequireMention at top-level and per-group); no breaking changes, no migration needed
  - Migration needed? No

  Risks and Mitigations

  - Risk: operator sets topicRequireMention: false globally and accidentally opens the bot to all topic thread messages without realizing requireMention only guards the main chat now
    - Mitigation: documented clearly in docs/channels/feishu.md with an explicit example showing the combined use of both flags

target.title: fix(sessions): honor explicit default model pins
target.body:
## Summary
- Persist explicit user selections of the configured default model as exact session model pins instead of clearing them as if they were unpinned defaults.
- Keep `/model default`, `session_status(model=default)`, and `sessions.patch model:null` as the explicit clear/unpin paths.
- Carry the exact selection through reply, queued-run, agent-command, reset, status, and sessions.patch paths so channel/parent/config fallback policy cannot silently replace user intent.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Explicit `/model <configured default>` selections no longer lose user intent just because they match the configured primary; fallback policy treats them as strict user session selections, while `/model default` still clears back to unpinned behavior.
Real environment tested: Local beatrice.local source checkout at PR head `98ca4cd8b150ee0be7fcd602a01c4aed346f316c` on May 30, 2026. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` modes, moves Discord slash-command deploy fingerprints into plugin state with legacy JSON migration, and updates Discord docs, config schemas, metadata, and tests.

PR surface: Source +353, Tests +337, Docs +7, Generated 0. Total +697 across 22 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main has no `slashCommandDeploy` config key and schedules deploy whenever native Discord commands are enabled. I did not reproduce the live Discord 429 burst in this read-only review.
