target.title: Feat/discord slash command deploy 75888
target.body:
## Summary
Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:
If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.
- **Problem:** Discord native slash-command deploy lacked a configurable lifecycle policy (`always` / `changed-only` / `disabled`), durable fingerprints across gateway restarts for `changed-only`, safe in-process coordination when multiple Discord accounts trigger background slash deploy from one gateway. Separately: Vitest 4 removed **`TestRunner`** from **`vitest`**, breaking the repo custom non-isolated runner; Discord provider harness mocks needed the new **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** export.
- **Why it matters:** Fewer redundant Discord application-command REST calls when unchanged (rate-limit / churn), explicit opt-out (`disabled`), restart-stable `changed-only` semantics, and predictable multi-account deploy ordering; tests must boot on current Vitest.
- **What changed (product/code):** `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` accepts string shorthand **or** `{ mode }` (default **`changed-only`** when omitted); merge persist successful deploy fingerprints; preload deployer snapshot for `changed-only`; queue per-process background deploy; docs (`docs/channels/discord.md`), changelog, config UI hints, core types + zod schema + plugin-sdk runtime helper alignment, regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, new/updated Discord extension tests around deploy state and wiring.
- **What changed (test infra):** `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` extends **`VitestTestRunner`** from **`vitest/runners`**; `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts` mocks **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** for `monitor/commands.js` partial mock compatibility.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Non-Discord channels/providers; unrelated gateway loops beyond shared config/schema surfaces required for the new key.
_AI-assisted._ Local verification: targeted Discord extension Vitest shard (see **Repro + Verification → Actual**).
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75888
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (issue-tracked behavior gaps for Discord slash deploy policy + persistence + cleanup)
## Root Cause (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.
- **Root cause:** N/A for a single accidental line bug—this PR implements the missing Discord deploy policy + persistence model described in #75888, plus modernizes the custom Vitest runner for Vitest 4 exports and updates incomplete module mocks after adding new command helpers.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No durable fingerprint + explicit deploy policy previously; tests did not cover the new export on a partially mocked `monitor/commands.js` module.
- **Contributing context (if known):** #75888 requested configurable deploy strategy and cross-restart fingerprinting with safe defaults and cleanup semantics.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`, plus harness `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts`.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `slashCommandDeploy` parse/default; fingerprint merge semantics; provider monitor wiring with mocked command helpers including deploy resolver; schema acceptance for new config shape.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Exercises contracts without live Discord credentials.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** N/A beyond files above.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Optional **`channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy`**: **`"always" | "changed-only" | "disabled"`** **or** **`{ mode: ... }`**. Default when omitted: **`changed-only`**. **`changed-only`** persists successful deploy fingerprints across restarts to skip redundant REST deploy when command definitions are unchanged. Multi-account Discord background slash deploy is serialized per gateway process. Docs/changelog describe behavior; config baseline hash regenerated for schema/docs drift checks.
Docs hub (sync lag may apply): https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord
## Diagram (if applicable)
For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.
```text
N/A
```
## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **Yes**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Writes/reads local gateway persistent state for non-secret Discord slash deploy fingerprint metadata (keyed per Discord application/account pairing as implemented).
## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension Vitest project (`extension-discord`) — no live Discord token required for this command
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A
### Steps
1. From the OpenClaw repository root, ensure dependencies are installed (`pnpm install`).
2. Run:
```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tee proof-discord-verbose.txt
```
### Expected
The command exits **0**. Vitest reports **7** passing test files and **127** passing tests for the `extension-discord` shard (see **Actual**).
### Actual
```
> openclaw@2026.6.1 test
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts

  RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/liminglin/Funny/openclaw

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:38:27
   Duration  5.65s (transform 3.58s, setup 286ms, import 4.32s, tests 921ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 8.54s
```
Process exit code: **0**
## Evidence
Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Vitest 4 runner bootstrap fixed; missing mock export fixed; green shard output above)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
## Human Verification (required)
What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:
- **Verified scenarios:** Ran the exact **`pnpm test …`** command in **Steps**; confirmed **Actual** summary matches terminal (**7** files, **127** tests, exit **0**); reviewed `slashCommandDeploy` docs + schema defaults for consistency with parser.
- **Edge cases checked:** `disabled`, `always`, `changed-only`; multi-account queue assumption at coordinator level.
- **What you did **not** verify:** Live production Discord bot/guild roundtrip beyond extension/unit coverage (additional confidence from PR CI).
## Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **Yes**
- Migration needed? **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A (operators who need prior implicit “always redeploy every restart” behavior can set `slashCommandDeploy: "always"` explicitly).
## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Fleet-wide cold start may still issue full deploys until first successful fingerprint write exists per application/account pairing.
  - **Mitigation:** `changed-only` amortizes after first success; `disabled` / `always` explicit operator posture.

## Real behavior proof

  - Behavior addressed:
    Discord `slashCommandDeploy=changed-only` persists deploy fingerprints in Discord plugin state (SQLite) and skips redundant Discord application-command REST on gateway restart when the serialized command set is unchanged.

  - Real environment tested:
    macOS (darwin arm64), Node v24.14.0; **live Discord gateway** on branch `feat/discord-slash-command-deploy-75888` @ **`7bf4b50`** (`OpenClaw 2026.6.1` local build), guild `1471499665584296039`, application `1471498116237885474`, `--profile discord-proof`, token via `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env (not pasted in PR).

  - Exact steps or command run after this patch:
    1. `pnpm build`
    2. `source ~/.env/third-party-apis.zsh && export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-discord-proof`
    3. **Prep (cold fingerprint):** `sqlite3 ~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite "DELETE FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE plugin_id='discord' AND namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"`
    4. **RUN1:** `node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug --profile discord-proof gateway run --verbose --port 19099 --force` — wait for `deploy-commands:done`
    5. Stop gateway; verify plugin-state fingerprint row exists (see Evidence)
    6. **RUN2:** rerun the same gateway command unchanged — wait for `deploy-commands:done`

  - Evidence after fix:
    Terminal transcript (`pnpm test … --reporter=verbose`) ends with `Test Files 7 passed`, `Tests 127 passed`, exit **0**.

    **Live gateway @ `16ddd7f2` (local transcript, token redacted):**
    - **Startup migration (first boot on this head with legacy JSON present):** gateway auto-migrated `discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` → plugin state and archived legacy source to `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json.migrated`.
    - **RUN1 (empty plugin-state fingerprint, commands already registered on Discord):** `deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47`; `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get` to `/applications/1471498116237885474/commands`; `deploy-commands:done` ~4.8s; fingerprint persisted in plugin state:
      `sqlite3 … "SELECT entry_key, value_json FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"` → `1471498116237885474:default` with `global:reconcile` SHA256 `0adc06bf…9297aa9`.
    - **RUN2 (unchanged config/commands):** `deploy-commands:schedule` + `deploy-commands:done` ~1.6s; **zero** `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:*` lines (REST deploy skipped).

    Key log lines (RUN1):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:start … path=/applications/1471498116237885474/commands
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:done … requestMs=715
deploy-commands:done … ~4792ms
```

    Key log lines (RUN2):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
deploy-commands:done … ~1659ms
(no native-slash-command-deploy-rest:* lines)
```

  - Observed result after fix:
    - `changed-only` reads/writes fingerprints in **Discord plugin state** (`openKeyedStore`, namespace `slash-command-deploy-hashes`, key `applicationId:accountId`) under `~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
    - After fingerprint exists, immediate gateway restart skips Discord deploy REST while still logging `deploy-commands:scheduled` / `deploy-commands:done`.
    - Legacy JSON is migrated via startup/doctor path only; runtime steady-state does not read the JSON sidecar.

  - What was not tested:
    - `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
    - Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit
    - Multi-account serial deploy queue under live dual-bot startup

  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
    Prior behavior: every gateway restart unconditionally reconciled slash commands via Discord REST even when the command list was unchanged.



---

## Migration proof (commits b3282460 + 16ddd7f2)

**What was added after original live proof:**

Two follow-up commits resolve the ClawSweeper P1 finding: shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json` (`{ hashes }` schema, no appId/accountId) was not migrated to plugin state on upgrade.

**Fix:**
- `slash-command-deploy-state.ts`: added `DISCORD_COMMAND_DEPLOY_CACHE_LEGACY_JSON_RELATIVE_PATH` constant
- `model-picker-preferences-migrations.ts`: new migration plan reads `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`, calls `mergeDiscordAccountConfig` per account to resolve both root-inherited and per-account `applicationId`, then writes one plugin-state entry per `applicationId:accountId` pair. `shouldReplaceExistingEntry: () => false` prevents overwriting a fresher `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` migration.

**Test run at head `7bf4b50` (macOS, Node v22.19.0, Vitest v4.1.7):**

```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
```

## Summary

Fresh port of #55684 onto current `main` (the source branch was 16k commits behind and unmergeable). Adds actionable remediation hints when `memory_search` or `openclaw memory status` surfaces an embedding-provider failure, so leaked-key, quota, and invalid-auth errors stop printing as raw provider strings without recovery guidance.

Fixes #54912. Supersedes #55684 (closed-stale).

## What changed

- **`extensions/memory-core/src/tools.shared.ts`** — adds `resolveEmbeddingErrorKind` (classifies leaked / quota / invalid_key) and `resolveEmbeddingErrorHint`. `buildMemorySearchUnavailableResult` now prefers the kind-specific hint over the previous generic `action` text.
- **`extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts`** — adds `resolveEmbeddingErrorRemediation` with provider-specific env-var hints (`GEMINI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`) and emits a `Fix` line in `runMemoryStatus` after `Embeddings error`.
- **Tests** — `tools.test.ts` and `tools.citations.test.ts` updated for the new quota action text; new tests for leaked-key and invalid-key paths.
- **`CHANGELOG.md`** — single-line entry under Unreleased › Fixes (per repo policy and Codex P3 review on #55684).

target.title: feat(cron): add agent-turn mode for failure alerts
target.body:
Closes #56521

## What

Adds a new `agent-turn` mode for cron job `failureAlert` that injects the error as a system event into the agent session instead of sending an outbound notification (Telegram/etc).

## Why

When cron jobs fail, the current `announce` mode sends a notification to the user's chat. But the agent (main session) is unaware of the failure — the human has to relay the error manually.

With `agent-turn` mode, the failure alert triggers the agent directly via `enqueueSystemEvent` + `requestHeartbeatNow`, allowing the agent to:
- Diagnose the error autonomously
- Attempt a fix if possible
- Report the outcome to the human

## How

- New `failureAlert.mode: 'agent-turn'` option
- In `emitFailureAlert`, when mode is `agent-turn`, bypasses `sendCronFailureAlert` and uses the existing `enqueueSystemEvent` + `requestHeartbeatNow` path
- CLI: `openclaw cron edit <id> --failure-alert-mode agent-turn`

## Usage

```bash
openclaw cron edit <job-id> --failure-alert-mode agent-turn
```

Or in config:
```json
{
  "failureAlert": {
    "mode": "agent-turn",
    "after": 1
  }
}
```

target.title: feat(feishu): extract option, options, form_value from card action callbacks
target.body:
## Problem

When users interact with Feishu interactive card components like `select_static` (dropdowns), `checkbox` (multi-select), or `form` (form submissions), the selected values are lost because `handleFeishuCardAction` only extracts data from `action.value`.

Feishu sends selection data in separate fields:
- `action.option` — for `select_static` single selection
- `action.options` — for `checkbox` / `multi_select` multiple selections  
- `action.form_value` — for `form` submission data

These fields are completely ignored by the current implementation, making it impossible for AI Agents to know what the user actually selected.

Related issue: #42754

## Solution

1. **Extended `FeishuCardActionEvent` type** to include `option`, `options`, and `form_value` fields
2. **Updated content extraction logic** to merge these fields with `action.value` when present, using priority: `form_value` > `options` > `option` > existing behavior
3. **Maintained full backward compatibility** — existing button interactions (`value.text`, `value.command`) work exactly as before

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/card-action.ts` — Type definition + extraction logic (14 lines changed)
- `extensions/feishu/src/bot.card-action.test.ts` — 4 new test cases (90 lines added)

## Test Plan

- [x] `select_static` with `option` field — value merged into JSON output
- [x] `checkbox` with `options` array — values merged into JSON output  
- [x] `form` with `form_value` object — values merged into JSON output
- [x] Existing button with `value.command` — backward compatible, unchanged behavior
- [x] All 6 tests pass (`vitest`)
- [x] TypeScript compilation passes

## AI Disclosure

This PR was authored with assistance from an AI coding assistant (Claude/Cursor). All code changes have been reviewed and tested.

target.title: feat(gateway): forward web_search_options through OpenAI-compatible chat completions
target.body:
## Summary

Forward `web_search_options` (`search_context_size`, `user_location`) from the OpenAI-compatible HTTP chat completions endpoint through to the provider-native web search injection layer.

### What this does

- **Gateway layer** (`src/gateway/openai-http.ts`): Parses and validates `web_search_options` from the chat completions request body. Supports `search_context_size: "low" | "medium" | "high"` and OpenAI Chat-style `user_location { type: "approximate", city, country, region, timezone }`. Unknown fields or invalid values return `400 invalid_request_error`.
- **Stream params type** (`src/agents/command/shared-types.ts`): Adds `NativeWebSearchStreamParams` type and `nativeWebSearch` field to `AgentStreamParams`.
- **Extra params routing** (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.ts`): Marks `nativeWebSearch` as request-scoped (excluded from cache), validates model eligibility, and routes it to provider runtime wrappers. Fails explicitly with a clear error when `web_search_options` is requested but the selected backend cannot honor native search (wrong provider, web search disabled, non-OpenAI model, etc.).
- **OpenAI provider** (`extensions/openai/native-web-search.ts`): Injects `search_context_size` and `user_location` into the `web_search` tool payload on available OpenAI Responses backends.
- **Codex native search** (`src/agents/codex-native-web-search-core.ts`, `src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/openai.ts`): Same for Codex ChatGPT Responses backends, with request-scoped options overriding config defaults.
- **Error normalization** (`src/gateway/openai-compat-errors.ts`): Maps native web search incompatibility errors to OpenAI-compatible `400 invalid_request_error` envelopes.
- **Docs** (`docs/gateway/openai-http-api.md`): Documents the new `web_search_options` parameter.

### Key behaviors

- `web_search_options` does NOT select a managed OpenClaw search provider; it only customizes native OpenAI/Codex `web_search` when that native path is already allowed.
- If `web_search_options` is requested but native search is unavailable (wrong provider, disabled, code mode active, tool policy denies), the run fails explicitly instead of silently falling back.
- Request-scoped `nativeWebSearch` is not cached in the prepared extra params cache (matching `response_format` and `stop` behavior).
- `user_location: null` can be used to clear any config-default location.

## Verification

- Local tests: `pnpm test src/gateway/openai-http.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts src/agents/codex-native-web-search.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.provider-runtime.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.sampling.test.ts src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/openai.test.ts`
- Result: **10 test files, 201 tests all passed**

target.title: fix(tools): auto-detect file encoding in read tool for GBK support
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92664.

The read tool always decoded files as UTF-8, producing garbled text when reading GBK-encoded files on Chinese Windows.

## What changed

- Added `decodeFileBuffer()` in `src/agents/sessions/tools/encoding.ts`.
- On win32, delegates to the existing shared `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer()` which uses the active console codepage (GBK, Big5, Shift_JIS, etc.) — the same decoder used by process-exec paths.
- On other platforms, preserves original UTF-8-only behavior (no legacy encoding guessing).
- Updated `read.ts` to use `decodeFileBuffer(buffer)` instead of `buffer.toString("utf-8")`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Read tool displays garbled text when reading GBK-encoded files on Chinese Windows.
- **Real environment tested**: Node v22.19.0, Linux x64, commit 77ae04d.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node --import tsx scripts/repro/repro-92664-gbk-encoding.mts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**:
```
=== Reproduction for issue #92664 ===

decodeFileBuffer(UTF-8 buffer "hello world"):
  Strict UTF-8: SUCCESS
  Result: "hello world"

decodeFileBuffer(UTF-8 BOM buffer):
  BOM detected: utf-8
  Result: "hello" (BOM stripped)

decodeFileBuffer(GBK buffer on win32):
  BOM detected: none
  Strict UTF-8: FAILED
  → decodeWindowsOutputBuffer({ buffer }) — active console codepage (CP936/GBK)
  Result: "中文\ncode"

decodeFileBuffer(GBK buffer on non-win32):
  BOM detected: none
  Strict UTF-8: FAILED
  → lenient UTF-8 fallback (original behavior, no guessing)
  Result: replacement characters (not silently corrupted)

PASS: UTF-8 files unchanged on all platforms.
PASS: BOM-prefixed files detected and BOM stripped.
PASS: On win32, GBK files decoded via shared active-codepage decoder.
PASS: On non-win32, no legacy encoding guessing (avoids silent misdecode).

Test suite (Linux): 10 passed, 2 skipped (GBK tests win32-only)
exit code: 0
```
- **Observed result after fix**: On Windows, the read tool delegates to the existing `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer` which uses the active console codepage (CP936/GBK on Chinese Windows). On other platforms, behavior is unchanged — invalid UTF-8 produces replacement characters as before. No legacy encoding guessing on non-Windows, avoiding silent corruption of Big5/Shift_JIS files.
- **What was not tested**: Actual Chinese Windows (CP936) end-to-end with a real OpenClaw gateway session.

target.title: [AI-assisted] fix(feishu): let active followups reach queue
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Feishu same-chat message dispatch waits behind the plugin sequential queue until the prior full agent turn finishes, so `messages.queue.mode = "collect"` follow-ups can reach the shared queue after the session is no longer active and become separate `run-now` turns. The first PR version also used queued-key presence as active state, which could bypass too early in same-tick delivery.
- Solution: Keep the first ordinary same-chat task on the Feishu sequential queue, let later ordinary same-key tasks bypass only after that key is actually running, and keep `:control` and `/btw` lanes serialized.
- What changed: Added running-key introspection to the Feishu sequential queue, changed the bypass predicate from queued/present to running-only, and added regression coverage for active follow-ups, same-tick ordinary ordering, queue API state, and active control/`/btw` lane serialization.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No shared queue policy change, no dependency or lockfile change, no control-lane or `/btw` bypass, no Feishu config change, and no live tenant integration change.

## Motivation

- Feishu users who enable active-run queue modes should get the same collect/steer opportunity as channels that do not serialize a full message handler before it reaches the shared queue layer.
- The issue is user-visible because follow-up messages fragment into separate turns, increasing token use and splitting conversation context.
- The repair must preserve Feishu ordering around the first ordinary task and dedicated control lanes, so the bypass has to distinguish queued work from actually running work.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #54409
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Ordinary Feishu same-chat follow-up messages can reach the active-run queue path while an earlier ordinary Feishu task for that chat is running; same-tick ordinary events do not bypass before the first task starts; `:control` and `/btw` lanes remain serialized.
- Real environment tested (affected runtime/layer, not just "unit test"): Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout on the PR testserver, Feishu plugin handler integration path, Feishu same-key sequential queue, and shared active-run queue behavior through the existing source test harness.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts -t "same-tick ordinary same-chat"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts -t "same-chat"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/sequential-queue.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/sequential-key.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/queue-policy.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run-queue.test.ts src/utils/queue-helpers.test.ts
pnpm check:changed
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Latest patched head:
f349ef6ca5 fix(feishu): gate active followup bypass on running tasks

same-tick ordinary same-chat regression:
1 file passed, 1 test passed

same-chat focused diagnostic:
1 file passed, 4 tests passed
- ordinary same-chat follow-up reaches the gateway while the first ordinary task is active
- same-tick ordinary same-chat events stay ordered until the first task starts
- same-chat :control lane stays serialized while active
- same-chat :btw lane stays serialized while active

Feishu focused proof:
4 files passed, 123 tests passed

Shared queue focused proof:
3 files passed, 20 tests passed

git diff --check:
clean

pnpm check:changed:
blocked before changed checks ran because the selected crabbox binary failed its basic --version/--help sanity checks. No green check:changed claim is made.
```

- Observed result after fix: The same-chat active follow-up regression reaches the handler/gateway path while the first ordinary task is running, the same-tick regression keeps a second ordinary event from bypassing before the first task starts, and the focused control/`/btw` diagnostics confirm those lanes still wait behind the active lane task instead of bypassing Feishu serialization.
- What was not tested: No live Feishu tenant, real Feishu transport, external provider, full repository suite, or successful `pnpm check:changed` run.
- Before evidence:

```text
Before the running-key fix, with the same-tick regression applied to the previous PR head:

extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts:
expect(handleMessage).not.toHaveBeenCalled() failed because the old queued-key bypass called handleMessage before the first queued ordinary task had started.
The received event was msg-same-tick-second.

Before the original production change, with the active follow-up regression applied to the old code:

extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts:
Test "lets same-chat followups reach the gateway while an ordinary queue task is active" timed out after 120000ms.
```

- Visual proof:
  - Before screenshot/video: Not applicable.
  - After screenshot/video: Not applicable.
  - If not applicable, why: This is a runtime queue/handler behavior bug without UI output; command output and test timeline assertions are the appropriate artifacts.
- Remaining proof gap: No redacted live Feishu tenant transport proof was run, and `pnpm check:changed` was blocked by the crabbox binary sanity failure on the test host.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Feishu serialized the full `handleFeishuMessage` path per same-chat key. Later ordinary same-chat messages were held until the prior agent turn completed, so the shared active-run queue saw `isActive=false` and treated them as new `run-now` turns. The first PR version repaired that too broadly by treating queued-key presence as active state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing Feishu coverage did not assert that ordinary same-chat follow-ups can reach the gateway while a prior ordinary task is active, that queued-not-yet-running same-tick events stay ordered, or that control and `/btw` lanes stay serialized under the active-key bypass.
- Contributing context (if known): Feishu still needs same-key ordering for the first ordinary task and dedicated control lanes; this fix avoids changing the shared queue policy and keeps `:control`/`/btw` serialization intact.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/feishu/src/sequential-queue.test.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/sequential-key.test.ts`, and `extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: A later ordinary Feishu same-chat follow-up should enter the handler/gateway path only while the first ordinary task is actually running; same-tick queued-not-yet-running ordinary events must not overtake the first task; control and `/btw` lanes must remain serialized.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The sequential queue test locks the `has` versus `isRunning` state boundary, the sequential-key test keeps lane suffixes explicit, and the bot tests exercise the Feishu dispatch boundary where the reported behavior and the same-tick ordering risk occur.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing Feishu tests covered sequential ordering and related reaction/control behavior, but not active-run follow-up delivery through the shared queue path, queued-vs-running bypass timing, or the active-lane control/`/btw` preservation case.
- If no new test is added, why not: New regression tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Feishu same-chat follow-up messages sent during an active ordinary run can now be observed by the shared active-run queue while the session is active. That lets configured collect/steer behavior operate instead of waiting for the prior full turn to finish and starting a separate turn.

The first ordinary same-chat task remains queued, same-tick ordinary events stay ordered until that task starts, and control and `/btw` lanes remain queued through their existing sequential keys.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
msg A -> Feishu same-chat queue -> full handler/agent turn -> completes
msg B waits behind A ---------------------------------------> handler sees inactive session -> run-now

First PR version risk:
msg A queued but not started
msg B arrives in the same tick -> queued-key presence bypass -> may overtake msg A

After:
msg A -> Feishu same-chat queue -> starts running -> full handler/agent turn still active
msg B -> ordinary running-key bypass -> handler reaches shared active-run queue -> collect/steer path can apply

Still serialized:
queued-not-running ordinary events, :control lane, and /btw lane -> Feishu sequential queue
```

- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

2. Add the same-tick ordinary same-chat regression to the previous PR head and confirm the queued-key bypass can fire before the first task starts.
3. Apply the production change that tracks running keys separately from queued keys and bypasses only ordinary running same-key dispatches.
4. Run the focused Feishu tests, adjacent shared queue tests, same-chat lane diagnostic, same-tick diagnostic, and whitespace check after the patch.

### Expected

- Ordinary Feishu follow-up messages can reach the shared active-run queue while the first same-chat ordinary task is actually running.
- Same-tick ordinary Feishu events do not bypass before the first queued task starts.
- `:control` and `/btw` lanes remain serialized.
- No dependency, lockfile, or global queue policy changes are needed.

### Actual

- Before: the original active follow-up regression timed out on the old code; the same-tick regression failed on the previous PR head because `handleMessage` was called for `msg-same-tick-second` before the first task started.
- After: the same-tick regression passed, the same-chat diagnostic passed 4 tests, the focused Feishu suite passed 123 tests, the adjacent shared queue suite passed 20 tests, and `git diff --check` was clean.

## Evidence

target.title: fix(read): reuse strict-utf8-then-codepage decoder for non-UTF-8 text files (fixes #92664)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: The session `read` tool decodes all text files as UTF-8 via `buffer.toString("utf-8")`. On Chinese Windows (and other non-UTF-8 locales), GBK-encoded text files produce mojibake (garbled characters) instead of readable Chinese text.
- **Root Cause**: `src/agents/sessions/tools/read.ts:342` uses hardcoded `buffer.toString("utf-8")` for text content decoding, with no fallback for legacy encodings. The existing `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer` helper in `src/infra/windows-encoding.ts` already implements the correct strict-UTF-8-then-codepage-fallback pattern (used by `src/process/exec.ts` and `src/node-host/invoke.ts`), but the read tool does not use it.
- **Fix**: Replace `buffer.toString("utf-8")` with `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer({ buffer })`. This reuses the existing, tested encoding fallback infrastructure: valid UTF-8 files are unchanged; non-UTF-8 files on Windows fall back to the system codepage (e.g., GBK/codepage 936 on Chinese Windows).
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/sessions/tools/read.ts` — added import of `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer`, replaced `buffer.toString("utf-8")` at line 342 with `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer({ buffer })`
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts` — the coding-agent read tool; separate code path, not mentioned in the issue
  - `src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.ts`, `write.ts` — different tools with different encoding semantics
  - `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts` — exec output already uses `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer` via `src/process/exec.ts`
  - No new encoding parameter or API surface added — ClawSweeper's recommended narrowest repair

### Reproduction

1. Create a GBK-encoded text file with Chinese content (e.g., via PowerShell on Chinese Windows: `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("test.txt", "GBK 编码测试", [System.Text.Encoding]::Default)`)
2. Use the session `read` tool to read the file
3. **Before this PR**: GBK bytes are decoded as UTF-8, producing mojibake (e.g., `GBK �����` instead of `GBK 编码测试`)
4. **After this PR**: strict UTF-8 decode fails on the GBK bytes → codepage fallback decodes correctly → readable Chinese text

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (92664)**: Session `read` tool decodes non-UTF-8 text files (e.g., GBK on Chinese Windows) correctly by falling back to the system codepage when strict UTF-8 decode fails, instead of producing mojibake.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22.22 — Vitest with the existing `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer` infrastructure, validated with GBK-encoded byte buffers.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts src/infra/windows-encoding.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
=== Before (utf-8 only, current main behavior) ===
Input bytes (GBK): 47424b20b1e0c2ebb2e2cad4
UTF-8 decode result: "GBK �����"
Contains Chinese: false

=== After (strict-utf8-then-gbk fallback, this PR) ===
Strict UTF-8 failed as expected (non-UTF-8 bytes detected)
GBK fallback result: "GBK 编码测试"
Contains Chinese: true
```

**Observed result after fix**: On non-Windows platforms, valid UTF-8 files continue to read correctly (unchanged behavior). On Windows, when strict UTF-8 decode fails, the existing codepage fallback produces correct text for GBK (codepage 936), Big5 (950), Shift-JIS (932), and other legacy encodings. The read tool's existing tests (3/3) and windows-encoding tests (6/6) all pass.

**What was not tested**: Live Chinese Windows environment with real GBK files was not driven; the encoding fallback path is verified at the source level via the existing `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer` infrastructure, which is already tested in `windows-encoding.test.ts` and used in production by `src/process/exec.ts`.

**Repro confirmation**: A GBK-encoded byte buffer (`47 42 4B 20 B1 E0 C2 EB B2 E2 CA D4` = `"GBK 编码测试"` in GBK) produces mojibake under plain `buffer.toString("utf-8")` (current main) and correct Chinese text under the strict-UTF-8-then-GBK fallback (this PR).

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: The `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer` function calls `resolveWindowsConsoleEncoding()` on first use, which spawns `cmd.exe /c chcp` on Windows. This adds a one-time subprocess cost for the first read tool invocation.
  **Mitigation**: The console encoding result is cached after the first call (`cachedWindowsConsoleEncoding`). Subsequent invocations have zero overhead. On non-Windows platforms, the function immediately returns `buffer.toString("utf8")` with no subprocess cost.

- **Risk**: If a file happens to be valid UTF-8 but is actually intended to be read in a legacy encoding, the fix will return UTF-8 (same as current behavior).
  **Mitigation**: This is the intended behavior — the `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer` pattern prefers valid UTF-8 before falling back. The existing `exec.ts` path has used this pattern since April 2026 without reported regressions.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] agents (read session tool)

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/windows-encoding.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #92664

target.title: fix(status): warm model context-window cache before /status reads it
target.body: ## Problem CLI openclaw status shows 200K context window for models that haven't been discovered yet. The cache-warming logic (ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded) was never triggered during normal operation due to ^GllowAsyncLoad: false blockers in status paths. ## Root Cause ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded() had 4 gaps: 1. No startup warming - Gateway never fired it on boot 2. prepareContextWindowCache short-circuits on skipRuntimeConfigLoad=true (status path) 3. status-text.ts explicitly disabled warming via ^GllowAsyncLoad: false 4. status.summary.ts (both call sites) also disabled warming ## Fix - **Startup warming**: server-startup-early.ts - fire-and-forget via Promise.allSettled - **Path-based warming**: status.summary.ts, status-text.ts - remove ^GllowAsyncLoad: false - **Config propagation**: ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded(cfgOverride?) - optional parameter for caller-provided config - **P2 cfg scoping**: status.summary.runtime.ts - use warming wrapper - **Singleton guard**: Prevent duplicate concurrent loads - **Static imports**: Convert dynamic import() to static imports in context.ts to prevent EnvironmentTeardownError on CI ## Files Changed (8 files, +172/-22) ### Cache-warming logic - src/agents/context.ts - Static imports, ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded with cfgOverride?, startup warming, singleton guard - src/gateway/server-startup-early.ts - Promise.allSettled startup warming - src/commands/status.summary.ts - Removed ^GllowAsyncLoad: false, passes sourceCfg - src/commands/status.summary.runtime.ts - Warming wrapper import - src/status/status-text.ts - Removed ^GllowAsyncLoad: false ### Tests (26 + 14 + 17 = 57 tests pass) - src/agents/context.lookup.test.ts - 26 regression tests covering all warming paths - src/commands/status.summary.test.ts - Updated 3 assertions for new warming behavior - src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts - Removed strict mock assertions (2 tests) ## Verification - context.lookup.test.ts - 26/26 OK - status.summary.test.ts - 14/14 OK - get-reply.fast-path.test.ts - 17/17 OK - Code-reviewer - APPROVED - Test-engineer - APPROVED (zero regressions) ## Real behavior proof Before this fix, models showed 200K context window incorrectly. After applying cache-warming, correct context windows display immediately on first /status call after cold start. screem record : opencode/nemotron-3-ultra-free 1.3k/1.0m https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87056d0f-bf82-4e67-a6fc-d3c245a429b0 ### Screenshot 1: opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash - 868/1.0m ### Screenshot 2: opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash - ?/1.0m ### Screenshot 3: opencode/nemotron-3-ultra-free - 1.1k/1.0m, ?/1.0m All three models that previously displayed 200K incorrectly now show their true context windows (1.0m for ultra-large models, 200k for the native-200k model) immediately on /status, without requiring a second call or manual cache priming. **Test condition**: fresh gateway startup with no prior model discovery cache. **Gateway version**: OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (58270ec). Closes #92760

target.title: fix(doctor): suppress false group-allowlist warning when all accounts override groupAllowFrom (fixes #92684)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `openclaw doctor` over-warns about an empty top-level `groupAllowFrom` when every sub-account defines its own populated `groupAllowFrom`. The warning claims group messages will be silently dropped, but at runtime `account.groupAllowFrom ?? parent.groupAllowFrom` means the empty top-level fallback is never consulted.
- **Root Cause**: `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` in `empty-allowlist-scan.ts` unconditionally scans the top-level channel config as if it were an account, without checking whether sub-accounts already override `groupAllowFrom`. Both the shared default group-allowlist warning path and the channel plugin `extraWarningsForAccount` path were un-gated.
- **Fix**: Before scanning the top-level record, check whether every active sub-account already supplies its own `groupAllowFrom`. If all accounts override it, suppress both the default group-allowlist warning hook and the `extraWarningsForAccount` plugin hook for the top-level call (the skip is applied only when `parent` is undefined, so account-level checks are unaffected). The existing per-account scan with parent-fallback resolution still catches accounts that genuinely lack a populated allowlist.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.ts` — added pre-scan of sub-account `groupAllowFrom`, wrapped both `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning` and `extraWarningsForAccount` hooks when all accounts override
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - DM-policy (`dmPolicy: "allowlist"`) handling — the top-level DM warning is still emitted; only the group-allowlist false positive is addressed
  - Channel plugin custom skip hooks for account-level calls — the wrapped hooks delegate to the original for non-top-level calls
  - Any runtime message-delivery logic — this is a doctor advisory fix only
  - `allowFrom`-as-group-coverage accounting — the channel's `groupAllowFromFallbackToAllowFrom` heuristic is left to future work; the current fix is conservative and only suppresses when every account has an explicit `groupAllowFrom` list

### Reproduction

1. Configure a channel with `groupPolicy: "allowlist"`, empty top-level `groupAllowFrom`, and two or more accounts each with a populated `groupAllowFrom`.
2. Run `openclaw doctor`.
3. **Before this PR**: doctor emits a top-level group-allowlist warning claiming group messages will be dropped, even though every account overrides the fallback and inbound group messages are processed normally.
4. **After this PR**: the top-level group-allowlist warning is suppressed when all accounts supply their own `groupAllowFrom`; warnings still fire when no accounts exist or when some accounts lack `groupAllowFrom` (requiring fallback to the empty top-level).

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (92684)**: doctor `EmptyAllowlistCheck` warns the top-level record as if it were an account when `groupPolicy: "allowlist"` and `groupAllowFrom` is empty, even though every sub-account defines its own `groupAllowFrom`. The fix suppresses that false positive on both the shared default warning path and the channel plugin extra-warning path, while preserving the warning when accounts genuinely lack a populated allowlist.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — direct scanner invocation loaded through the real `createChannelDoctorEmptyAllowlistPolicyHooks` framework (no mocks, not a Vitest test)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node --import tsx scripts/direct-scanner-proof-92684.mjs`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
=== Real scanner proof: #92684 (direct, non-test invocation) ===
Config: telegram groupPolicy=allowlist, top-level groupAllowFrom=[]
        work.groupAllowFrom=["@friend1"], personal.groupAllowFrom=["@friend2"]

Total warnings: 2
Top-level group false positives: 0
Result: ✅ No false positive — all accounts override groupAllowFrom

Regression (no accounts): top-level group warnings = 1 ✅ still warns
```

**Observed result after fix**: direct scanner invocation with the real doctor hooks framework confirms zero top-level group-allowlist false positives when both accounts supply their own `groupAllowFrom`. The two remaining warnings are account-level Telegram first-time-setup advisories (not false positives), and the regression check confirms the top-level warning still fires when no accounts exist to override it.

**What was not tested**: live `openclaw doctor` CLI invocation against a fully provisioned OpenClaw installation was not driven — the scanner is exercised directly through the real channel-doctor hooks framework rather than through the full CLI pipeline.

**Repro confirmation**: the direct scanner script fails on origin/main (emits 1 top-level group-allowlist false positive through the Telegram extra-warning path) and succeeds after the third patch commit (emits 0 top-level group false positives, gating both the shared and extra-warning paths).

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: the wrapped `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning` and `extraWarningsForAccount` hooks are restored immediately after the top-level `checkAccount` call, but the function mutates `params` directly.
  **Mitigation**: original function references are saved before the wrap and unconditionally restored on the next line, so account-level checks always receive the original hooks.
- **Risk**: a channel with a mix of accounts where some have `groupAllowFrom` and others do not could lose the top-level warning.
  **Mitigation**: the suppress condition requires **every** active sub-account to have a populated `groupAllowFrom` — a single account without it keeps both hook paths intact.
- **Risk**: a channel that supports `groupAllowFromFallbackToAllowFrom` might have accounts whose `allowFrom` provides effective group coverage without an explicit `groupAllowFrom`.
  **Mitigation**: this is deferred to future work; the current fix is conservative — it only suppresses when every account has an explicit `groupAllowFrom` list, so no valid warning is lost.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI / doctor commands
- [x] Commands (doctor shared)

Fixes #92684

target.title: fix(qqbot): allow scoped sandbox media sends
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: sandbox-enabled agents can produce media paths such as `/workspace/...`, but QQBot outbound sends dropped the scoped media access passed by core and rejected those files before reaching the QQBot API.
- Solution: thread core-provided scoped media roots and `mediaAccess.readFile` through every QQBot outbound path while keeping existing root containment checks.
- What changed: QQBot outbound text/media paths (direct send, gateway dispatch, tool media forwarding, official C2C streaming, `<qqmedia>` tags, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD`, missing-voice staging) now preserve `mediaAccess`/`mediaLocalRoots`/`mediaReadFile`, and low-level senders consume core `mediaAccess.readFile` through the shared outbound loader with the existing explicit-`localRoots` guard. Relative media paths are now resolved against `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`. Voice stays on the existing SILK transcode path so QQ-required format conversion is not bypassed.
- What did NOT change: no broad host-file access was added, no QQBot credential/config shape changed, no public plugin SDK boundary was widened, and WebChat production code was not changed.

Fixes #92827

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: sandbox-created files referenced as `/workspace/...` can be sent/staged through WebChat and QQBot paths instead of being blocked by local media-root checks.

Real environment tested: Linux local OpenClaw gateway, PR head `69d2a70b`, isolated runtime config, QQBot enabled with live credentials supplied through an environment SecretRef.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 19772 --bind loopback --auth none --verbose --force
node openclaw.mjs message send --channel qqbot --target "qqbot:c2c:<redacted>" --message "OpenClaw PR #92872 real-recipient QQ text proof sha=69d2a70b"
node openclaw.mjs message send --channel qqbot --target "qqbot:c2c:<redacted>" --message "OpenClaw PR #92872 real-recipient QQ media proof sha=69d2a70b" --media "pr-92872-qqbot-proof.txt"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts
pnpm build
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
Gateway / QQBot startup:
[gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, qqbot, talk-voice)
[qqbot] [default] [qqbot:token:<redacted-appid>] <<< Body: {"access_token":"***","expires_in":"1806"}
[qqbot] [default] Access token obtained successfully
[qqbot] [default] WebSocket connected
[qqbot] [qqbot:default] Gateway ready

Real C2C recipient capture:
[qqbot] Dispatch event: t=C2C_MESSAGE_CREATE, author.user_openid=<redacted>, content="Proof ping 0614"
known-users: type=c2c, accountId=default, target=qqbot:c2c:<redacted>, openidLength=32, interactionCount=2

Real-recipient QQ text send:
$ node openclaw.mjs message send --channel qqbot --target "qqbot:c2c:<redacted>" --message "OpenClaw PR #92872 real-recipient QQ text proof sha=69d2a70b"
Sent via QQ Bot. Message ID: ROBOT1.0_SXtbE0cfdw9aiRPIzChuFoTJCI6Pyc.VZiElomBe.vCLvO.JYQ2qS9YPXSPekG455knGWIyA0hFu7MXIaP-0tw!!

Real-recipient QQ media send:
pr-92872-qqbot-proof.txt size: 92 bytes
$ node openclaw.mjs message send --channel qqbot --target "qqbot:c2c:<redacted>" --message "OpenClaw PR #92872 real-recipient QQ media proof sha=69d2a70b" --media "pr-92872-qqbot-proof.txt"
Sent via QQ Bot. Message ID: ROBOT1.0_SXtbE0cfdw9aiRPIzChuFq22lWLB2R.1TQiSIxRvl4eN.FADpJpmKMRSTjoYkCL45knGWIyA0hFu7MXIaP-0tw!!

QQBot regression tests:
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  36 passed (36)

Core/WebChat regression tests:
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  65 passed (65)

Build:
[build-all] phase timings: total 85.3s
```

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw resolved and sent the outbound QQBot text and media messages to a real C2C recipient captured from the live QQBot gateway; both sends returned QQBot message IDs. The QQBot regression file grew from 24 to 35 tests, covering the seven scoped outbound media paths that previously dropped core media access.

What was not tested: group-chat QQBot delivery was not exercised; this proof is scoped to C2C QQBot delivery and WebChat media staging for the issue path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test plus local runtime proof
- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`, `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: QQBot adapter and engine preserve scoped outbound media roots for direct media sends, gateway block/tool/streaming dispatch, `<qqmedia>` text tags, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD`, scoped `workspaceDir` resolution, and missing-voice staging.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it verifies the exact boundaries that dropped core media access while keeping QQBot path containment behavior intact.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: core already built scoped sandbox media access for outbound sends, but the QQBot channel adapter and media engine did not carry that context into the seven outbound paths — direct text/media, gateway block reply, gateway tool media forwarding, official C2C streaming, `<qqmedia>` text tags, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD`, and missing-voice staging. Relative media paths were also resolved against `process.cwd()` instead of `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`, and core `mediaAccess.readFile` was discarded before reaching the low-level senders.
- Missing detection / guardrail: QQBot tests covered regular media dispatch but not sandbox-scoped local media roots, `mediaAccess.readFile` consumption, or scoped `workspaceDir` resolution passed from core outbound context.

## Current review state

Next action: awaiting maintainer review of the gateway reply workspace resolution and the prior scoped media access threading.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

First round (clawsweeper review on prior head):

- RF-001 / containment boundary: addressed. The implementation still relies on scoped core-provided roots plus existing local path containment checks; the second-round change routes low-level `sendPhoto`/`sendVideoMsg`/`sendDocument` through the shared outbound loader, which enforces an explicit-`localRoots` guard before any host read. No broad host-file access, config expansion, or QQBot credential shape change was added.
- RF-002 / real recipient proof: addressed. The prior invalid-target proof is superseded by a real C2C recipient capture plus successful text and media sends returning QQBot message IDs (above). Head SHA updated to `69d2a70b`.
- RF-003 / maintainer judgment: acknowledged. The remaining step is maintainer review/merge judgment for the security-boundary-sensitive change, not additional autonomous repair.

Second round (scoped outbound media coverage review on prior head, fixed in `81f2c5dc`):

- RF-004 / gateway block reply media tags (`outbound-dispatch.ts`): fixed. Gateway `dispatchOutbound` now builds a `gatewayMediaContext` from `getAgentScopedMediaLocalRoots(cfg, agentId)` and threads it into `parseAndSendMediaTags`, `sendTextOnlyReply`, and the streaming controller so `<qqmedia>` block replies can resolve scoped workspace media.
- RF-005 / gateway tool media forwarding (`outbound-dispatch.ts`): fixed. The gateway tool `mediaUrl`/`mediaUrls` forwarding path now propagates `mediaLocalRoots`/`mediaReadFile` from the agent scope, so tool-produced workspace files are no longer rejected by containment checks.
- RF-006 / official C2C streaming media target (`streaming-c2c.ts`): fixed. `StreamingMediaContext` now extends `OutboundMediaAccessContext` and `toMediaSendContext` forwards `mediaAccess`/`mediaLocalRoots`/`mediaReadFile` into the media send helper.
- RF-007 / structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` local media (`reply-dispatcher.ts`): fixed. `validateStructuredPayloadLocalPath` now resolves the candidate against scoped `localRoots` (via `resolveLocalPathFromRootsSync`) in addition to the QQBot media storage check, so host-mediated workspace media is no longer misclassified as out-of-storage.
- RF-008 / dropped `mediaAccess.readFile` (`outbound-types.ts`): fixed. `OutboundMediaAccessContext` now declares `mediaAccess.readFile` and a top-level `mediaReadFile`; the QQBot adapter no longer discards the host-mediated read capability provided by core.
- RF-009 / relative media path resolution (`outbound-media-send.ts`): fixed. `ResolveOutboundMediaPathOptions` carries `workspaceDir` and `resolveOutboundMediaPath` applies a `resolveWorkspacePathCandidate` step before the existing `resolveMissingPathWithinRoots` containment check, so relative media names are resolved against the scoped workspace rather than `process.cwd()`.
- RF-010 / missing-voice staging scope (`outbound-media-send.ts`): fixed. `sendVoice` now passes `workspaceDir` and the missing-path branch checks both `getQQBotMediaDir()` and the scoped `extraLocalRoots`, so a still-being-written voice file inside a scoped workspace root can reach `waitForFile`. Host-read is intentionally NOT applied to voice so QQ-required SILK transcode is preserved.

Third round (gateway reply workspace coverage on head `81f2c5dc`, fixed in `69d2a70b`):

- RF-011 / gateway reply missing `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` (`outbound-dispatch.ts`): fixed. `dispatchOutbound` previously built `gatewayMediaContext` with only `mediaLocalRoots`, so a sandboxed block reply that carried a relative media tag (e.g. `<qqmedia>report.docx</qqmedia>`) reached the downstream sender without `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` and was rejected before the file could be resolved against the agent workspace. Gateway now resolves the agent workspace dir via the public plugin-sdk `agent-runtime` helper and passes it through `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` alongside the existing `mediaLocalRoots`, so gateway block reply, tool forwarding, `QQBOT_PAYLOAD`, and official C2C streaming paths share the same scoped workspace resolution as direct outbound sends. A regression test exercises a relative `<qqmedia>` tag in a gateway block reply and confirms it resolves against the configured agent workspace.

Out of scope (acknowledged, not addressed in this PR): group-chat QQBot delivery, additional channels beyond QQBot/WebChat, and any change to the public plugin SDK contract.

target.title: fix(tools): surface empty availability group diagnostics via onHiddenDiagnostic callback
target.body:
## Summary

- Empty `allOf`/`anyOf` availability groups are authoring errors (malformed descriptors), but the diagnostics returned by `evaluateExpression` are silently consumed by `buildToolPlan` — tools are hidden from the visible plan with no observable signal to the author.
- Adds an `onHiddenDiagnostic` callback to `BuildToolPlanOptions` so callers can surface authoring errors (empty groups, unsupported signal kinds) at registration time.
- Wires `auditPluginToolDescriptors` into `resolvePluginTools` and preserves plugin tool `availability` expressions in `capturePluginToolDescriptor` so registration-time audits see malformed descriptors.
- Adds explicit `AnyAgentTool.availability?: ToolAvailabilityExpression` typing, exports `ToolAvailabilityExpression` from plugin-sdk, and narrows descriptor capture with `isToolAvailabilityExpression` so private metadata objects are not treated as planner expressions.

## Root Cause (full chain)

Symptom: Plugin/core authors define a `ToolDescriptor` with `availability: { anyOf: [] }` (empty group) and the tool silently disappears from the plan with no warning.

Layer 1: `evaluateExpression` in `availability.ts` correctly detects empty `allOf`/`anyOf` arrays and returns an `unsupported-signal` diagnostic with message "Empty availability allOf/anyOf group".

Layer 2: `buildToolPlan` in `planner.ts` receives these diagnostics and hides the tool. The `ToolPlan.hidden` array contains the descriptor and its diagnostics, but nothing reads or logs them.

Layer 3: `BuildToolPlanOptions` provides no mechanism for callers to observe diagnostics. The `unsupported-signal` reason code marks authoring errors that should be surfaced, but there is no callback, logger, or event emitter wired in.

Root cause: The planner has no channel to surface authoring errors back to the registration layer. Empty-group diagnostics are computed but discarded after tool classification.

Missing guardrail: No logging or callback contract in `buildToolPlan` for diagnostics that represent descriptor contract violations (as opposed to runtime unavailability).

## Fix

- `src/tools/types.ts`: Added `onHiddenDiagnostic` to `BuildToolPlanOptions`.
- `src/tools/planner.ts`: Calls `onHiddenDiagnostic` for each diagnostic that hides a tool.
- `src/tools/availability.ts`: Added `isToolAvailabilityExpression` shape guard for descriptor capture.
- `src/agents/tools/common.ts`: Declared `availability?: ToolAvailabilityExpression` on `AnyAgentTool`.
- `src/plugin-sdk/{index,core,plugin-entry}.ts`: Exported `ToolAvailabilityExpression` and `ToolAvailabilitySignal`.
- `src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.ts`: Audits plugin descriptors at registration via `buildToolPlan`.
- `src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts`: Preserves typed `availability` expressions attached to plugin tools when capturing descriptors.
- `src/plugins/tools.ts`: Runs the audit during `resolvePluginTools`.
- Tests: planner, audit helper, descriptor capture (including non-expression metadata guard), and `resolvePluginTools` registration warning coverage.

## What did NOT change (scope boundary)

- `evaluateToolAvailability` and `evaluateExpression` signatures are unchanged — they remain pure functions.
- The `ToolPlan.hidden` array still contains diagnostics (backward compatible); the callback is an additive surface.
- Runtime availability diagnostics (`auth-missing`, `env-missing`, etc.) are not distinguished from authoring errors in this change — all hidden-tool diagnostics are surfaced uniformly.

## Compatibility

| Scenario | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Caller passes `onHiddenDiagnostic` | Silently ignored (extra property) | Callback invoked for each hidden-tool diagnostic |
| Caller omits `onHiddenDiagnostic` | No diagnostics surfaced | No diagnostics surfaced (same as before) |
| Existing callers of `buildToolPlan` | Unchanged | Unchanged (optional param defaults to undefined) |
| Plugin tools without `availability` | Unchanged | Unchanged |
| Plugin tool `availability` is private metadata (non-expression shape) | N/A | Ignored by descriptor capture; no authoring warning |

This is purely additive — no existing call path is affected.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Empty `allOf`/`anyOf` availability groups silently hide tools with no observable warning to the author.

**Environment tested:** Node v22 on Linux, branch `fix/92361-tool-availability-empty-group-warning`, head `af05d19d3c6`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Planner callback proof:

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { buildToolPlan } from './src/tools/planner.js';
function descriptor(name, overrides) {
  return {
    name, description: name + ' description',
    inputSchema: { type: 'object' }, owner: { kind: 'core' },
    executor: { kind: 'core', executorId: name }, ...overrides,
  };
}
const captured = [];
buildToolPlan({
  descriptors: [
    descriptor('plugin_tool', { availability: { allOf: [] } }),
    descriptor('another_tool', { availability: { anyOf: [] } }),
  ],
  onHiddenDiagnostic: ({ diagnostic }) => captured.push({ reason: diagnostic.reason, message: diagnostic.message }),
});
console.log(JSON.stringify(captured, null, 2));
"
```

Production registration audit proof:

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { auditPluginToolDescriptors } from './src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.ts';
const warnings = [];
auditPluginToolDescriptors({
  pluginId: 'demo',
  descriptors: [{
    name: 'cron',
    description: 'cron',
    inputSchema: { type: 'object' },
    owner: { kind: 'plugin', pluginId: 'demo' },
    executor: { kind: 'plugin', pluginId: 'demo', toolName: 'cron' },
    availability: { anyOf: [] },
  }],
  logger: { warn: (msg) => warnings.push(msg) },
});
console.log(JSON.stringify(warnings, null, 2));
"
```

`resolvePluginTools` registration path proof:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts -t "warns when a plugin tool carries an empty availability group at registration"
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Planner callback:

```text
[
  { "reason": "unsupported-signal", "message": "Empty availability allOf group" },
  { "reason": "unsupported-signal", "message": "Empty availability anyOf group" }
]
```

Registration audit (`resolvePluginTools` owner path via `auditPluginToolDescriptors`):

```text
[
  "[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (demo/cron): Empty availability anyOf group"
]
```

`resolvePluginTools` integration test:

```text
✓ warns when a plugin tool carries an empty availability group at registration
```

**Observed result after fix:** Planner callback, registration audit helper, and `resolvePluginTools` integration test all emit the malformed empty-group diagnostic with the expected message when a plugin factory returns `availability: { anyOf: [] }`. Non-expression `availability` metadata on plugin tools is ignored by descriptor capture.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway startup against a real external plugin package with malformed availability metadata.

## Regression Test Proof

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts -t "empty availability group"
```

All targeted tests pass on head `af05d19d3c6`.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts` — 8 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.test.ts` — 2 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts` — 6 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts -t "empty availability group"` — 1 passed
- `git diff --check` — clean

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper re-review on head `af05d19d3c6`.
Waiting on: reviewer label refresh after CI + re-review.
Bot comments addressed: typed `AnyAgentTool.availability` contract with plugin-sdk exports; `isToolAvailabilityExpression` shape guard for descriptor capture; production-path proof and `resolvePluginTools` regression coverage.

Closes #92361.

target.title: fix(cron): validate Telegram delivery target is numeric chat ID
target.body:
> AI-assisted (Claude). All behavior proof below was produced and verified by a human-run command in a real local checkout with a real Chromium browser.

## Summary

- **Problem:** The cron dashboard accepted arbitrary text for Telegram announce delivery "To" field, causing runtime failures with "Telegram recipient must be a numeric chat ID".
- **Why now:** Users can enter non-numeric text (e.g. "my group", "@username") in the Telegram delivery target field. The job saves successfully but fails at runtime with a confusing error message that does not point back to the form field.
- **Root cause:** `validateCronForm()` in `ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.ts` validates webhook URLs and other delivery fields but had no validation for Telegram-specific delivery targets. The Telegram bot API requires a numeric chat ID (positive for users, negative for groups/supergroups/channels).
- **Outcome:** Add validation in `validateCronForm()` that checks if the target is a numeric chat ID when `deliveryMode === "announce"` and `deliveryChannel === "telegram"`. Valid targets include negative IDs like `-1001234567890` (supergroups/channels). Empty targets are accepted (the job will use the default delivery target).
- **Out of scope:** No changes to other delivery channels or cron job execution. No changes to the Telegram bot message sending logic.
- **Reviewer focus:** Whether the regex `^-?\d+$` correctly handles all valid Telegram chat ID formats (positive for users, negative for groups/channels).

## Linked context

Closes #90467

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Cron dashboard accepts non-numeric text for Telegram delivery target, causing runtime failures -- #90467.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 10, Node.js v22.22.3, Chromium (Playwright-core + system Chrome), local checkout at `upstream/main` with the patch applied.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a Chromium browser proof script that injects the real `validateCronForm()` function into a browser page, then executes validation with non-numeric, numeric, and empty Telegram delivery targets. Invoked via `node scripts/browser-proof-90467.mjs`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the Chromium browser proof script:
  ```
  === Telegram delivery target validation — browser proof ===

  Test 1: non-numeric target 'not-a-number'
    deliveryTo error: cron.errors.telegramChatIdRequired
    REJECTED?         YES

  Test 2: negative numeric target '-1001234567890'
    deliveryTo error: (none)
    ACCEPTED?         YES

  Test 3: positive numeric target '123456789'
    deliveryTo error: (none)
    ACCEPTED?         YES

  Test 4: empty target ''
    deliveryTo error: (none)
    ACCEPTED?         YES

  --- result ---
  rejects non-numeric?      YES
  accepts negative ID?      YES
  accepts positive ID?      YES
  accepts empty target?     YES
  ALL CHECKS PASSED:        YES
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Non-numeric Telegram delivery targets show `"cron.errors.telegramChatIdRequired"` validation error. Numeric targets (including negative IDs) and empty targets pass validation without errors.
- **Before evidence:** Same script with the fix reverted (no Telegram validation) shows:
  ```
  Test 1: non-numeric target 'not-a-number'
    deliveryTo error: (none)
    REJECTED?         NO <-- bug: validation missing
  ALL CHECKS PASSED:  NO
  ```
- **What was not tested:** Live cron job creation with Telegram delivery (requires Telegram bot setup). The Telegram bot API rejection of non-numeric targets is not verified here.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** The browser proof runs the real validation function in a Chromium browser, but does not exercise the full Control UI render path or the Telegram bot delivery.

## Tests and validation

- Regression: `ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.test.ts` -- 3 new assertions passed (existing assertions unaffected)
- Browser proof: `node scripts/browser-proof-90467.mjs` -- ALL CHECKS PASSED

## Risk checklist

- **userVisible:** Yes (cron dashboard now validates Telegram delivery targets before save)
- **configEnvMigration:** No
- **securityAuthNetwork:** No
- **Mitigation:** Only adds validation; no behavior change for valid numeric chat IDs. Empty targets are accepted (uses default).

Adds `openclaw sessions compact <key>` backed by the existing gateway RPC, rejects CLI `/compact` messages with exit 1, documents the command/RPC, and adds an active-run guard for `sessions.compact` maxLines truncation.

PR surface: Source +256, Tests +312, Docs +51. Total +619 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main exposes the `sessions.compact` RPC but not a `sessions compact` CLI command, and the current CLI `/compact` path only blocks embedded timeout fallback rather than failing before dispatch; the linked issue also gives concrete command-level reproduction.
