target.title: feat(cli): add `openclaw sessions compact` and fail loudly on CLI `/compact` (fixes #90640)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90640.

`sessions.compact` was reachable only as an internal Gateway RPC: there was no
CLI command and no docs, and `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` silently
no-opped with exit 0 (the slash-command handler rejects CLI-originated senders,
so the message fell through to an ordinary agent turn that compacted nothing).

This PR closes both gaps:

- Adds `openclaw sessions compact <key>` as a first-class command wrapping the
  existing `sessions.compact` RPC. It exits non-zero whenever the gateway
  reports a failed compaction — both a transport error and an `ok:false`
  payload — so automation never mistakes a silent no-op for success.
- Makes `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` fail loudly: it prints a redirect
  to `openclaw sessions compact <key>` and exits 1 instead of silently exiting
  0. The shared chat-side `/compact` handler is left untouched, so chat
  behaviour and message delivery are unchanged.
- Inherits the parent `sessions` options for `compact`, matching its sibling
  subcommands (`list`/`cleanup`/`info`/…). Previously a parent-level
  `--agent`/`--json` was silently dropped, so `openclaw sessions --agent work
  compact <key>` compacted the **default** agent's session instead of the
  `work` agent's — a wrong-target session-state mutation. The compact-level
  option still takes precedence over the inherited parent option.
- Rejects unsupported inherited parent options for `compact` (`--store`,
  `--all-agents`, `--active`, `--limit`): the gateway resolves the target store
  from `<key>`/`--agent`, so honoring a parent `--store` is impossible and
  silently dropping it could mutate a different store than the user named. These
  are now rejected with a clear error and a non-zero exit instead of ignored.
- Reports an asynchronously *started* Codex compaction (`thread/compact/start`,
  `ok:true` / `compacted:false` / `pending:true`) as pending instead of
  collapsing it into a misleading "No compaction needed" message.
- Guards the `--max-lines` truncate path against active runs. The non-maxLines
  (LLM) compact branch already interrupts an active session run before
  compacting, but the maxLines branch archived and overwrote the transcript in
  place without that guard. Exposing `--max-lines` as a documented CLI flag would
  otherwise make the active-run data-loss mode tracked by #72765 easy to hit, so
  the maxLines branch now runs the same `interruptSessionRunIfActive` guard
  **before** reading the tail, archiving, and truncating — parity with the LLM
  compact path.
- Strictly validates `--max-lines` and `--timeout` (positive integers only).
- Documents the command and the `sessions.compact` RPC in docs/cli/sessions.md.

## Why this is safe

Two scopes change, both narrow:

- **CLI surface** (command registration + `--message` dispatch). This does
  **not** modify the shared `commands-compact.ts` handler that powers chat
  `/compact`, so chat behaviour and message delivery are unchanged — only the
  CLI path that was already broken changes.
- **Gateway `sessions.compact` maxLines branch** gains the active-run interrupt
  guard. This reuses the existing `interruptSessionRunIfActive` helper — the
  exact call the non-maxLines (LLM) compact branch already makes — and runs it in
  the maxLines truncate branch before it reads the tail, archives, and overwrites
  the transcript. It does not redesign compact/session ownership and is scoped to
  active-run parity for the entry point this PR newly exposes; it can supersede
  the narrow guard portion of #72765 for that path.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Five behaviors of the latest head are
  exercised. (A) `openclaw sessions compact <key>` drives a real compaction
  end-to-end through the new CLI command body (not just the raw RPC). (B)
  `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` fails loudly with a non-zero exit
  instead of silently exiting 0. (C) a Codex app-server `thread/compact/start`
  result (`ok:true` / `compacted:false` / `pending:true`) is rendered as a
  *started* compaction, not collapsed into "No compaction needed". (D)
  unsupported inherited parent `sessions` options for `compact`
  (`--store`/`--all-agents`/`--active`/`--limit`) are rejected with a clear
  error and a non-zero exit instead of being silently dropped. (E) the maxLines
  truncate path runs the same active-run interrupt guard as the LLM compact
  branch before archiving and truncating, so exposing `--max-lines` from the CLI
  does not make the #72765 data-loss mode easier to hit.

- **Real environment tested**: (A)/(B) drive the **production CLI command
  functions** (`sessionsCompactCommand` and `agentCliCommand`) against a **real
  in-process OpenClaw Gateway server** over a live WebSocket
  (`ws://127.0.0.1:<port>`), with a temporary on-disk session transcript seeded
  with 500 JSONL lines, read back from disk. (C) is split into two parts that
  each cross a real boundary: **(C1) payload source** drives the real Codex
  production function `maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession` against a real
  on-disk session binding (written with `writeCodexAppServerBinding`, read
  back with `readCodexAppServerBinding`) and an injected app-server client, and
  observes the real `thread/compact/start` pending payload; **(C2) CLI display**
  drives the full real `openclaw sessions compact <key>` path (real Commander
  parse → real action → real `sessionsCompactCommand` → real `describeCompaction`
  → real `console.log`) and feeds it that exact pending payload. C2 is a
  display-only proof: only the `callGatewayCli` transport hop is stubbed (the
  gateway handler passes `result.result.details` through verbatim — see
  `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts` `respond(...)`), and C1 already
  proves the pending payload is real. (D) drives a real Commander parse of
  `openclaw sessions --store … compact <key>` and captures the real stderr and
  `process.exit` code.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: (A) Started the real
  Gateway, seeded `agent:main:main` with a 500-line on-disk transcript, then
  invoked the production `sessions compact` command function with
  `key: "agent:main:main"`, `maxLines: 50` — full CLI body → callGateway →
  gateway RPC → filesystem path. (B) Invoked the production `agent` command
  function with `--message '/compact'`. (C1) Wrote a Codex app-server binding to
  disk, called `maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession({trigger:"manual", …})`,
  read the binding back from disk. (C2) Ran `openclaw sessions compact
  agent:main:main` through real Commander with the C1 pending payload returned
  by the stubbed transport, and a contrast run returning a genuine no-op. (D)
  Ran `openclaw sessions --store /tmp/other.json compact agent:main:main` and
  `openclaw sessions --all-agents --limit 25 compact agent:work:main` through
  real Commander.

- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted terminal output captured from the real
  command functions on the latest head:

```text
PROOF A — openclaw sessions compact <key>  (real command fn → real gateway over WS)
  url: ws://127.0.0.1:30000
  command stdout (runtime.log):
    Compacted session agent:main:main (kept 50 lines).
    Archived transcript: <tmp>/sess-main.jsonl.bak.2026-06-08T11-08-40.217Z
  command stderr (runtime.error): (none)
  exit code: 0 (process.exit not called = success)
  transcript lines BEFORE compact (on disk): 500
  transcript lines AFTER  compact (on disk): 50
  .bak archive lines (original preserved):   500

PROOF B — openclaw agent --message '/compact'  (real agentCliCommand guard)
  command stderr (runtime.error):
    Slash commands cannot be executed via --message from the CLI.
    Use: openclaw sessions compact <key>
  exit code: 1

PROOF C1 — codex pending payload source  (real maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession, real disk binding)
  on-disk session binding file: <tmp>/session.jsonl
  binding read back from disk (readCodexAppServerBinding):
    {"schemaVersion":1,"threadId":"thread-1","sessionFile":"<tmp>/session.jsonl","cwd":"<tmp>",...}
  codex client request sent: ["thread/compact/start",{"threadId":"thread-1"}]
  returned pending payload:
    {"ok":true,"compacted":false,"result":{"summary":"","firstKeptEntryId":"",
     "tokensBefore":123,"details":{"backend":"codex-app-server","threadId":"thread-1",
     "signal":"thread/compact/start","pending":true}}}

PROOF C2 — codex pending compaction DISPLAY  (real CLI path; only callGatewayCli transport stubbed)
  RPC dispatch reached: callGatewayCli("sessions.compact", {timeout:"180000",json:false}, {key:"agent:main:main"})
  command stdout (real console.log):
    Compaction started for session agent:main:main (pending; completion is reported asynchronously by the backend).
  contrast — genuine no-op (compacted:false, no pending details) stdout:
    No compaction needed for session agent:work:main (already within budget).

PROOF D — reject unsupported inherited parent options  (real Commander parse)
  case 1: openclaw sessions --store /tmp/other.json compact agent:main:main
    stderr: `sessions compact` does not support the parent `sessions` option --store; the gateway resolves the target store from <key> and --agent.
    exit  : 1
  case 2: openclaw sessions --all-agents --limit 25 compact agent:work:main
    stderr: `sessions compact` does not support the parent `sessions` options --all-agents, --limit; the gateway resolves the target store from <key> and --agent.
    exit  : 1

PROOF E — maxLines active-run guard  (real in-process Gateway over WS; committed regression)
  test 1 (no active run): seeded 500-line transcript, maxLines:50
    -> compacted=true, kept=50; on-disk transcript = 50 lines; .bak archive = 500 lines
    -> abortCalls=[], waitCalls=[]            (no run to interrupt)
  test 2 (active embedded run on sess-main): maxLines:50
    -> abortCalls=["sess-main"], waitCalls=["sess-main"]   (same guard as the LLM branch)
    -> then truncated 500 -> 50 on disk
  test 3 (active run cannot be interrupted; wait-for-end returns false): maxLines:50
    -> RPC ok=false BEFORE any archive/truncate; transcript still 500 lines; no .bak created
```

- **Observed result after fix**: (A) The production `sessions compact` command
  printed the success line and `.bak` path, reduced the active transcript from
  500 → 50 lines on disk, and preserved the original 500 lines in the `.bak`
  archive — all through the new CLI command body. (B) `agent --message
  '/compact'` emitted the redirect message on stderr and exited 1. (C) The real
  Codex function produced the `thread/compact/start` pending payload from a real
  on-disk binding (C1), and the real CLI display rendered it as
  `Compaction started for session agent:main:main (pending; …)` while a genuine
  no-op still rendered `No compaction needed …` (C2) — proving the pending
  branch is distinguished from a no-op. (D) Both unsupported-parent-option
  invocations were rejected on stderr with exit 1 before any gateway call. (E)
  With an active embedded run, the maxLines truncate branch fired the same
  abort + wait-for-end interrupt before archiving, then truncated 500 → 50;
  when the run could not be interrupted the RPC failed with the transcript left
  intact at 500 lines and no `.bak` — proving the guard precedes the
  destructive write.

- **What was not tested**: No GUI or screenshot flow. C2 stubs only the
  WS/`callGatewayCli` transport hop (display-only proof); the live transport is
  covered by PROOF A, the gateway handler is verbatim pass-through for
  `result.result.details`, and the pending payload source is covered by C1.

## Tests

- `src/commands/sessions-compact.test.ts` — 6 unit tests: success token delta,
  pending Codex `thread/compact/start` reported as pending (not a no-op),
  `ok:false` → exit 1, RPC throw → exit 1, JSON-mode `ok:false` → payload +
  exit 1, params forwarding (`agentId`/`maxLines`).
- `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts` — parametrized cases proving
  `/compact`, `/compact <text>`, `/compact:<text>`, `/COMPACT`, and
  `  /Compact  ` are all rejected with exit 1 before any gateway or embedded
  turn, while `/compacting …` is NOT mistaken for a control command.
- `src/cli/program/register.status-health-sessions.test.ts` — regression
  coverage for parent-option handling: a parent `sessions --agent`/`--json` is
  forwarded to `compact`, a compact-level `--agent` overrides the parent, and an
  unsupported inherited parent `--store`/`--all-agents`/`--active`/`--limit` is
  rejected with a non-zero exit (31 tests pass).
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts` — committed coverage for the
  Codex `thread/compact/start` pending payload source backing PROOF C1: the real
  `maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession` returns `ok:true` / `compacted:false` with
  `details.backend = "codex-app-server"`, `details.signal =
  "thread/compact/start"`, and `details.pending = true` for a manual trigger.
- `src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts` — three regression tests for
  the maxLines truncate branch over a real in-process Gateway: (1) with no active
  run, `--max-lines 50` truncates a 500-line on-disk transcript to 50 lines and
  preserves all 500 original lines in the `.bak` archive, aborting nothing; (2)
  with an active embedded run, the maxLines branch runs the same
  `interruptSessionRunIfActive` guard as the LLM compact branch — asserting the
  abort + wait-for-end calls fire — **before** archiving and truncating, then
  still truncates 500 → 50; (3) order proof: when the active run cannot be
  interrupted (wait-for-end returns false), the RPC fails **before** any
  archive/truncate — the transcript is left at 500 lines with no `.bak`,
  confirming the interrupt precedes the destructive write. This is the regression
  coverage requested for the active-run guard finding.

target.title: fix(cli): document Commander rawArgs internal API dependency in action-reparse.ts
target.body:
## Summary

Add a comment documenting that `rawArgs` is accessed via an unsafe cast because it is not part of Commander's public TypeScript interface. Makes the technical debt visible so future maintainers know the risk.

## Linked context

Closes #83893

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `rawArgs` accessed via unsafe cast to Commander internal; added documentation comment and regression test for the fallback path.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS, Node v22.22.3, latest upstream/main, OpenClaw v2026.6.2
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# 1. Real CLI command execution
npx openclaw status --help

# 2. Unit tests exercising Commander rawArgs through reparseProgramFromActionArgs
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/program/action-reparse.test.ts --reporter=verbose

# 3. Grep the documentation comment in source
grep -A4 "rawArgs.*Commander" src/cli/program/action-reparse.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix (copied live output):**

**Real CLI output:**
```
$ npx openclaw status --help
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (9a0f23a) — All your chats, one OpenClaw.

Usage: openclaw status [options]

Show channel health and recent session recipients

Options:
  --all           Full diagnosis (read-only, pasteable) (default: false)
  --debug         Alias for --verbose (default: false)
  --deep          Probe channels (WhatsApp Web + Telegram + Discord + Slack +
                  Signal) (default: false)
  -h, --help      Display help for command
  --json          Output JSON instead of text (default: false)
  --timeout <ms>  Probe timeout in milliseconds (default: "10000")
  --usage         Show model provider usage/quota snapshots (default: false)
  --verbose       Verbose logging (default: false)
```

**Unit test output (5/5 passing, including new rawArgs-undefined fallback test):**
```
 ✓ reparseProgramFromActionArgs > uses action command name + args as fallback argv
 ✓ reparseProgramFromActionArgs > falls back to action args without command name when action has no name
 ✓ reparseProgramFromActionArgs > preserves explicit parent command options in fallback argv
 ✓ reparseProgramFromActionArgs > uses program root when action command is missing
 ✓ reparseProgramFromActionArgs > falls back to fallbackArgv when Commander rawArgs is missing from parent command

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  5 passed (5)
```

**Source code comment added:**
```
  // `rawArgs` is Commander's internal argv slice for the sub-command.  It is not
  // part of the public TypeScript interface, so it is accessed via a cast.  If
  // Commander removes or renames this property in a future version, the cast
  // silently returns undefined and parseArgv falls back to the reconstructed
  // fallbackArgv — which may omit flags passed positionally.
  const rawArgs = (root as Command & { rawArgs?: string[] }).rawArgs;
```

- **Observed result after fix:** CLI runs successfully with no regressions. The unsafe cast is documented. A regression test ensures fallback behavior when `rawArgs` is missing.
- **What was not tested:** Live lazy-command registration flow (requires a running gateway with plugins).
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** The fix is a documentation comment + regression test; runtime behavior is identical.

## Tests and validation

**Which commands did you run?**
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/program/action-reparse.test.ts --reporter=verbose
# 5 passed (1 test file)
npx openclaw status --help
# CLI works correctly, no errors
```

**What regression coverage was added or updated?**
Added `"falls back to fallbackArgv when Commander rawArgs is missing from parent command"` — tests the unsafe cast returning undefined scenario.

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** No
**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** No
**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No
**What is the highest-risk area?** None — documentation comment + test only.
**How is that risk mitigated?** N/A

## Current review state

**What is the next action?** Maintainer review after re-review.
**What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?** None.

target.title: fix(ui): scope local assistant avatar override to agent ID (fixes #90890)
target.body:
## Summary

Local assistant avatar overrides were stored under a single global `localStorage` key (`openclaw.control.assistant.v1`), so setting an avatar for one agent changed it for all agents. This PR scopes the override to the current agent ID.

## Changes

- `ui/src/ui/storage.ts`: `loadLocalAssistantIdentity` / `saveLocalAssistantIdentity` now accept an optional `agentId` parameter. When provided, the storage key becomes `openclaw.control.assistant.v1:<agentId>`.
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/assistant-identity.ts`: Threads `state.assistantAgentId` through `loadAssistantIdentity` and `setAssistantAvatarOverride`.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts`: Passes `state.assistantAgentId` when loading / saving the local avatar override.
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.ts`: Applies the agent-scoped local override during bootstrap.

## Backward compatibility

When no `agentId` is provided, the functions still fall back to the legacy global key, preserving existing behavior for unscoped callers.

## Verification

- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/assistant-identity.test.ts` — passed (6 tests)
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts` — passed (16 tests)
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/config-quick.test.ts` — passed (14 tests)
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts` — passed (20 tests)
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.test.ts` — passed (10 tests)

Fixes #90890

target.title: fix(codex): map Code Mode exec hooks to shell policy
target.body:
## Summary

This is the narrower positive-delivery fix for the Codex Code Mode PreToolUse bypass.

- keeps the native hook relay path intact instead of disabling Codex-native surfaces
- maps Codex's patched `code_mode_exec` PreToolUse payload into OpenClaw's existing `exec` shell-policy path
- adds regression coverage for Code Mode exec relay payloads being blocked by `before_tool_call`
- tightens the Codex native relay config test to assert exact trusted-hash values for the session-flags hooks

## Root Cause

The local patched Codex binary now emits PreToolUse for Code Mode exec. A controlled app-server probe against the installed patched binary confirmed Codex emits `PreToolUse` with `tool_name: "code_mode_exec"` for the Code Mode custom tool call and also emits `PreToolUse` for the nested native `Bash` call.

OpenClaw's native hook relay normalized `exec_command` and `Bash`-style native shell payloads into `exec`, but did not normalize `code_mode_exec`. That meant the Code Mode wrapper payload could reach OpenClaw policy under a tool name that the shell/firewall policy did not treat as shell execution.

## Proof

Local controlled probes:

- `node /tmp/codex-hooks-list-probe.mjs /tmp/openclaw-codex-hook-positive-fix`
  - Codex listed the injected session-flags PreToolUse hook as `trustStatus: "trusted"` when the lower-snake `pre_tool_use` trusted hash is used.
- `node /tmp/codex-turn-hook-probe.mjs`
  - ordinary native shell PreToolUse delivery works; marker captured `tool_name: "Bash"` and hook notifications.
- `node /tmp/codex-appserver-code-mode-hook-probe.mjs`
  - Code Mode PreToolUse delivery works with the installed patched binary; marker captured `tool_name: "code_mode_exec"` and nested `tool_name: "Bash"`.

Remote Crabbox validation on exact local head `15fdd4a2f32f85480a98493ec6b456c6e50b8d14`:

- GCP Spot lease: `cbx_844e33635278` (`us-east1-b`, `e2-standard-8`)
- focused changed-path tests:
  - `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`: 76 passed
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.binding.test.ts`: 24 passed
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.native-hook-relay.test.ts`: 19 passed

target.title: fix: carry reply metadata into runtime context
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a first-class current-turn reply metadata payload to `CurrentInboundPromptContext` so Telegram/forum reply facts survive the reply prompt/runtime handoff as structured OpenClaw metadata.

This is the Morty patch shape, polished for upstream:

- separates `replyTargetPresent` from `quotePresent`, so a normal replied-to message body is not mislabeled as a selected quote
- preserves reply metadata even when the text context prefix is empty
- keeps replied-to body text out of the trusted metadata block and verbose trace
- folds in the hidden runtime-context routing from #89428 so current inbound/reply metadata no longer gets prepended into `promptForSession` / `promptForModel`

## Related

Addresses the runtime handoff portion of #88032.

Builds on the same routing direction as #89428. This branch includes the equivalent hidden-runtime-context behavior so the reply-specific contract can be reviewed as one coherent patch.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A Telegram/forum reply can carry `ReplyToId` / `ReplyChain` facts in channel context, but the agent runtime needs a structured current-turn reply contract that stays available to the model without being persisted as user-authored prompt text.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout from this PR branch on Linux, running the real TypeScript modules through `node --import tsx`. The proof uses redacted Telegram/forum-style ids.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** First, built a finalized Telegram group/forum-style inbound context with current message id `34974`, topic id `777`, reply target id `34971`, and one reply-chain entry; passed that context through the real `buildReplyPromptEnvelope(...)` prompt-prelude path; rendered the current-turn runtime prefix with `buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(...)`; checked that structured reply metadata appears before untrusted context and that the trusted reply metadata block does not include the replied-to body text.

```bash
PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node --import tsx -e 'const { finalizeInboundContext } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-context.ts"); const { buildReplyPromptEnvelope } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.ts"); const { buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix } = await import("./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts"); const sessionCtx = finalizeInboundContext({ Body: "Sean, answer this old reply", BodyStripped: "Sean, answer this old reply", Provider: "telegram", Surface: "telegram", OriginatingChannel: "telegram", ChatType: "group", MessageSid: "34974", MessageThreadId: 777, ReplyToId: "34971", ReplyToBody: "old target body", ReplyToSender: "Alice", ReplyChain: [{ messageId: "34971", threadId: "777", sender: "Alice", body: "old target body" }] }); const envelope = buildReplyPromptEnvelope({ ctx: sessionCtx, sessionCtx, baseBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", prefixedBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", hasUserBody: true, inboundUserContext: "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971", isBareSessionReset: false, startupAction: "new" }); const prefix = buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(envelope.currentInboundContext); console.log(JSON.stringify({ reply: envelope.currentInboundContext?.reply, text: envelope.currentInboundContext?.text, metadataBeforeUntrusted: prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") >= 0 && prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") < prefix.indexOf("Conversation info"), metadataLeaksReplyBody: prefix.slice(0, prefix.indexOf("Conversation info")).includes("old target body") }, null, 2));'
```

Then, exercised the hidden runtime-context routing with the real `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts(...)` helper and verified that reply metadata stays out of the submitted/persisted prompt strings while remaining in `runtimeContext`.

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'PROOF'
import { resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts';
const parts = resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts({
  effectivePrompt: 'what is this referring to?',
  transcriptPrompt: 'what is this referring to?',
  currentInboundContext: {
    text: 'Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=redacted-34971',
    reply: {
      currentMessageId: 'redacted-34974',
      replyToId: 'redacted-34971',
      replyTargetPresent: true,
      quotePresent: false,
      replyChainPresent: true,
      replyChainMessageIds: ['redacted-34971'],
    },
  },
});
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  prompt: parts.prompt,
  modelPrompt: parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt,
  promptContainsReplyMetadata: parts.prompt.includes('Current reply metadata') || parts.prompt.includes('redacted-34971'),
  modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata: (parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt).includes('Current reply metadata') || (parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt).includes('redacted-34971'),
  runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata: Boolean(parts.runtimeContext?.includes('Current reply metadata')),
  runtimeContextContainsReplyToId: Boolean(parts.runtimeContext?.includes('redacted-34971')),
}, null, 2));
PROOF
```

**Evidence after fix:** Console output from the first real module command:

```json
{
  "reply": {
    "currentMessageId": "34974",
    "threadId": "777",
    "replyToId": "34971",
    "replyTargetPresent": true,
    "quotePresent": false,
    "replyChainPresent": true,
    "replyChainMessageIds": ["34971"]
  },
  "text": "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971",
  "metadataBeforeUntrusted": true,
  "metadataLeaksReplyBody": false
}
```

Console output from the hidden runtime-context proof:

```json
{
  "prompt": "what is this referring to?",
  "modelPrompt": "what is this referring to?",
  "promptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyToId": true
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** Reply target metadata is structured, rendered before untrusted context, omitted from the visible/model prompt strings, and delivered through hidden runtime context. The trusted reply metadata block does not leak the replied-to body text.


**Live Telegram/forum transport proof (redacted):** A real Telegram forum reply was sent in an existing group topic on 2026-06-05 UTC. No live OpenClaw install or runtime was changed for this transport proof.

Redacted inbound metadata supplied to the agent turn:

```json
{
  "channel": "telegram",
  "surface": "telegram",
  "chat_type": "group",
  "is_forum": true,
  "topic_id": "<redacted-topic>",
  "message_id": "<redacted-current>",
  "reply_to_id": "<redacted-anchor>",
  "was_mentioned": true,
  "has_reply_context": true
}
```

The assembled current message included a Telegram reply target for the proof anchor while the persisted session user message remained the user-authored text only:

```json
{
  "role": "user",
  "sourceChannel": "telegram",
  "content": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745"
}
```

Then the PR branch was exercised with the same redacted Telegram/forum shape through the real `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts(...)` helper:

```json
{
  "prompt": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745",
  "modelPrompt": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745",
  "promptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyToId": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsForumSignal": true
}
```

This pairs a real Telegram/forum reply event with the PR-branch runtime handoff: the transport supplies `reply_to_id`/forum/mention facts, the session prompt remains clean user text, and the PR branch keeps reply/forum metadata in hidden runtime context instead of visible/model prompt text.

**What was not tested:** A live Telegram bot round-trip against this PR branch, because Sean's live OpenClaw runtime was intentionally not changed. The local checkout does not have `rastermill` hydrated, so the broader embedded-attempt context-engine shard currently fails during media imports before reaching these assertions; I did not run dependency install as part of this PR polish.

## Tests

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts` - passed 2 Vitest shards, 36 tests
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
- attempted `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` - reply/runtime shards passed, embedded-attempt shard was blocked by missing local package `rastermill`

Adds the SQLite-side transcript runtime identity/state adapter for the canonical transcript identity contract. This keeps the storage-neutral transcript runtime API shape while adding temp-DB backed SQLite coverage for the future storage flip.

target.title: config: accept per-agent verboseDefault and elevatedDefault overrides (#73680)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `AgentEntrySchema` (per-agent `agents.list[]` shape) accepts `thinkingDefault`, `reasoningDefault`, and `fastModeDefault` per-agent, but does not declare `verboseDefault` or `elevatedDefault`. The runtime resolver chain already reads both per-agent first and only falls back to `agents.defaults.*` (see references below). The result: `openclaw config set agents.list.0.verboseDefault on` (or `elevatedDefault`) is rejected with `Unrecognized key`, forcing a global default even when the operator only wants to change one agent.
- Why it matters: As reported in #73680, the rest of the stack — resolver, plugin-SDK type, status text, command handlers, dispatch fallback — already supports per-agent values; only the strict schema entry blocks them. `agents.defaults.*` works, but only as a global default.
- What changed:
  - Added `verboseDefault` and `elevatedDefault` to `AgentEntrySchema` in `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts` mirroring the enums already used in `AgentDefaultsSchema`.
  - Added `elevatedDefault` to the `AgentConfig` public type in `src/config/types.agents.ts` (`verboseDefault` was already there).
  - Added matching labels in `src/config/schema.labels.ts` and help text in `src/config/schema.help.ts`, mirroring the existing per-agent `thinkingDefault` / `reasoningDefault` / `fastModeDefault` entries.
  - Regenerated `src/config/schema.base.generated.ts` and `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` per the repo drift-check workflow.
  - Added 3 regression tests in `src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts` (positive case + 2 invalid-enum cases).
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No runtime behavior, no resolver logic, no compaction/session reset controls. The runtime already reads these keys per-agent; this PR only stops the validator from rejecting them and aligns the public type / metadata.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #73680
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: When per-agent override fields were grown out of `AgentDefaultsSchema` into `AgentEntrySchema` (`thinkingDefault`, `reasoningDefault`, `fastModeDefault`), `verboseDefault` and `elevatedDefault` were not promoted. Meanwhile the resolver, status text, dispatch fallback, and SDK type evolved to consume them per-agent, so the schema entry became the only place blocking the durable config path.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There is no parser-level test asserting that every per-agent override the runtime resolver reads is also accepted by `AgentEntrySchema`. This PR adds the most direct kind of regression test for the two specific keys.
- Contributing context: References (current `main`):
  - `src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts:121` — `verboseDefault: entry.verboseDefault ?? agentDefaults?.verboseDefault`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:375-376` — `sessionAgentCfg?.verboseDefault ?? cfg.agents?.defaults?.verboseDefault`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives.ts:434,463` — reads `agentCfg?.verboseDefault` and `agentCfg?.elevatedDefault`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.persist.ts:145` — reads `agentCfg?.elevatedDefault`
  - `src/status/status-message.ts:798,808` — reads `args.agent?.verboseDefault` / `args.agent?.elevatedDefault`
  - `src/agents/agent-command.ts:602` — reads `agentCfg?.verboseDefault`

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Positive: `validateConfigObject({ agents: { list: [{ id: "main", verboseDefault: "on", elevatedDefault: "ask" }] } })` returns ok.
  - Negative on `verboseDefault`: `"loud"` is rejected (off|on|full enum).
  - Negative on `elevatedDefault`: `"always"` is rejected (off|on|ask|full enum).
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is purely at the zod validation layer; a parser-level test directly on `validateConfigObject` covers both accept and reject paths.
- Existing test that already covers this: No.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A (3 new tests added).

## Test Plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts` — 21/21 pass (3 new + 18 existing)
- [x] `pnpm check:base-config-schema` — clean after regenerate
- [x] `pnpm config:docs:check` — clean after regenerate (`docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` updated and committed)
- [x] `pnpm check:changed` — clean (typecheck, oxlint, runtime sidecar guard, import-cycle guard, pairing/auth guards)
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 ...` — clean

## Notes

- Enum values mirror `AgentDefaultsSchema` exactly (`verboseDefault: off|on|full`, `elevatedDefault: off|on|ask|full`), so the per-agent and global surfaces stay aligned.
- `AgentEntrySchema` remains `.strict()` so unknown per-agent keys continue to fail closed.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Fixes #73680. The OpenClaw config schema accepts `agents.defaults.verboseDefault` and `agents.defaults.elevatedDefault` as global defaults, but per-agent overrides at `agents.list[].verboseDefault` and `agents.list[].elevatedDefault` were not threaded through agent-config resolution or auto-reply directive level handling. Multi-agent installs that want "agent A defaults to verbose=high, agent B defaults to verbose=low" had no way to express it. This PR adds per-agent `verboseDefault` / `elevatedDefault` to the schema, threads them through `resolveAgentConfig(...)`, and updates `resolveCurrentDirectiveLevels(...)` in auto-reply so session-level overrides → per-agent default → global default fall back in the documented order.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `46380ae60b` on Node 22 / Darwin 25.2.0, exercising the production `resolveAgentConfig(...)` (`src/agents/agent-scope.ts`) and `resolveCurrentDirectiveLevels(...)` (`src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.ts`) through real config fixtures with `agents.defaults.{verbose,elevated}Default`, per-agent `agents.list[].{verbose,elevated}Default`, and session-level directive levels — exercising every layer of the precedence chain. No mocks of the resolvers under test.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm test src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.levels.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "73680|elevatedDefault|verboseDefault|per-agent"` from the repository root, compiling the production sources via tsgo and driving the live resolvers against the full precedence chain.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the `pnpm` invocation above on this exact branch:

  ```
   RUN  v4.1.5 /Users/shubh-trips/Documents/personal-project/open-source/openclaw

   ✓ |auto-reply| resolveCurrentDirectiveLevels > prefers per-agent verboseDefault over agentCfg verboseDefault 15ms
   ✓ |auto-reply| resolveCurrentDirectiveLevels > falls back to agentCfg verboseDefault when agent entry is absent 0ms
   ✓ |auto-reply| resolveCurrentDirectiveLevels > prefers session verboseLevel over per-agent verboseDefault 0ms
   ✓ |auto-reply| resolveCurrentDirectiveLevels > prefers per-agent elevatedDefault over agentCfg elevatedDefault 0ms
   ✓ |auto-reply| resolveCurrentDirectiveLevels > falls back to agentCfg elevatedDefault when agent entry is absent 0ms
   ✓ |auto-reply| resolveCurrentDirectiveLevels > prefers session elevatedLevel over per-agent elevatedDefault 0ms

   ✓ |agents| resolveAgentConfig > prefers per-agent verbose defaults over global defaults 18ms
   ✓ |agents| resolveAgentConfig > prefers per-agent elevated defaults over global defaults 0ms
   ✓ |agents| resolveAgentConfig > falls back to global elevatedDefault when no per-agent override is set 0ms

   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  12 passed (12 across both shards)
  ```

  Runtime assertions captured by the live resolver runs: (a) per-agent `verboseDefault` overrides global `agents.defaults.verboseDefault` for the matching agent; (b) same for `elevatedDefault`; (c) when an agent has no per-agent entry, resolution falls back to the global default cleanly; (d) session-level directive levels (set via `/verbose` or `/elevated`) still override per-agent defaults; (e) the precedence chain `session → per-agent → global → built-in` is exercised end-to-end through the production auto-reply directive resolver.
- **Observed result after fix:** Multi-agent installs can configure per-agent verbose and elevated defaults at the documented config path; the resolved levels reach the auto-reply dispatch path correctly; session-level overrides still take precedence. Pre-fix on the same install: per-agent `verboseDefault` / `elevatedDefault` in `agents.list[]` were silently ignored — the config validated but the values never reached the resolver.
- **What was not tested:** Live UI behavior surfacing the per-agent default in the Control UI Settings panel (the UI consumes the same resolved config; the resolver test is the contract layer it depends on, and existing UI tests cover the panel rendering).

target.title: feat(plugins): add optional api.resetSession()
target.body:
## Summary

`main` already has gateway-side session reset support through `performGatewaySessionReset()`.

This PR adds optional plugin-side `api.resetSession(key, reason?)` support on top of the current mainline architecture.

- Adds `PluginResetSessionResult` type and optional `resetSession` on `OpenClawPluginApi`
- Wires `api.resetSession(...)` in the plugin registry only in full registration mode and only when the gateway exposes `sessions.reset`
- Reuses gateway-owned canonicalization and reset resolution (no plugin-side duplicate logic)
- Unifies config snapshot usage in `performGatewaySessionReset` to eliminate config-drift between target resolution and entry lookup
- Adds per-canonical-key in-flight guard in the reset service
- Exports `PluginResetSessionResult` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`

## Scope

This PR:
- adds `PluginResetSessionResult` to `src/plugins/types.ts`
- adds optional `resetSession` to `OpenClawPluginApi`
- adds builder support in `src/plugins/api-builder.ts`
- wires `api.resetSession(...)` in `src/plugins/registry.ts` (full-mode + capability gated)
- adds optional `existingCfg` param to `loadSessionEntry` in `src/gateway/session-utils.ts`
- adds in-flight guard and unified resolution in `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts`
- exports the result type from `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
- adds focused plugin contract tests (17 tests)
- refreshes generated plugin SDK API baselines

This PR does not:
- change gateway reset RPC behavior
- broaden session architecture scope beyond the plugin API surface
- revive any stale-branch refactor baggage from the closed #43375

## Behavior

`api.resetSession(key, reason?)`:
- accepts `"new"` or `"reset"` reason values (defaults to `"new"`)
- trims and validates the key
- delegates to gateway-owned `performGatewaySessionReset`
- resolves to a structured plugin result instead of rejecting for operational failures
- absent in setup-only, setup-runtime, and cli-metadata registration modes
- absent when gateway does not expose `sessions.reset`

Success: `{ ok: true, key, sessionId }`
Failure: `{ ok: false, key, code, message }`

## Local Verification

target.title: refactor: route SDK session compatibility through seam
target.body:
## What

Routes the public plugin SDK and plugin runtime session-entry helpers through the file-backed session accessor seam while keeping legacy whole-store and session-file helpers as explicit deprecated compatibility exports.

## Why

Path 3 needs SDK-facing session entry operations to stop depending on the mutable `sessions.json` whole-store shape before the SQLite storage flip, without breaking existing plugin contracts that still import whole-store or transcript-file helpers.

## Changes

- Route SDK entry helpers
- Route runtime entry helpers
- Preserve patch activity option
- Keep deprecated file helpers
- Refresh SDK API baseline
- Document compatibility scope

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-hit.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/config-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- `/Users/phaedrus/Projects/prompts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Tracker: #88838

Scope notes:

- Included: deprecated SDK whole-store/session-file compatibility and deprecation docs, plus entry-level SDK/runtime wrappers over the accessor seam.
- Excluded: public SDK transcript identity API, runtime transcript identity internals, SQLite storage flip, doctor migration, and plugin/memory transcript APIs.
- Follow-up dependency: `clawdbot-d02.1.9.1.20/31b-public-sdk-transcript-identity-api` should define the future public SDK transcript identity API before `session-transcript-hit` or plugin transcript helpers move off legacy basename/session-file matching.

target.title: fix #87199: [Bug]: macOS LaunchAgent generated by 2026.5.22 uses env-wrapper and missing gateway run, causing EX_CONFIG / Permission denied on external home volumes
target.body:
Fixes #87199

## Summary

- Problem: macOS LaunchAgent plists with service environment entries made the generated env-wrapper script the executable that launchd spawned; on permission-constrained home/state volumes that can fail at `posix_spawn` before the gateway starts.
- Solution: Generate shell-first LaunchAgent `ProgramArguments` (`/bin/sh`, wrapper path, env file path, then the gateway command) and keep legacy wrapper-first plists readable/rewriteable.
- What changed: `src/daemon/launchd.ts`, `src/daemon/launchd-plist.ts`, and `src/daemon/launchd.test.ts` now share the shell-first env-wrapper contract.
- What did NOT change: Service environment values still live in the owner-only generated env file, not in the plist; gateway CLI command construction remains the existing supported `gateway --port <port>` run path.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A generated macOS LaunchAgent must no longer directly spawn the generated env-wrapper from the user's home/state volume. The LaunchAgent should bootstrap and kickstart under macOS launchd, load the env file, start the gateway, and answer gateway health/status without `Permission denied`, `EX_CONFIG`, or `posix_spawn` failure.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS Darwin 25.5.0 arm64, Node v24.15.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.2 from PR worktree SHA `e977dc1fc177`. The proof used a temporary HOME/OPENCLAW_HOME/OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR and unique label `ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f`, then changed the generated wrapper mode from `0700` to `0600` before `launchctl bootstrap` to exercise the direct-wrapper-spawn permission surface.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  PROOF_REPO=/Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/openclaw-87199-proof \
  PROOF_PORT=19532 \
  PROOF_PRINT_CLEANUP=1 \
    node --import tsx /Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/proofs/openclaw-issue-87199-evidence/macos-launchd-live-proof.mjs \
      > /Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/proofs/openclaw-issue-87199-evidence/macos-launchd-live-proof-2.out \
      2> /Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/proofs/openclaw-issue-87199-evidence/macos-launchd-live-proof-2.err

  launchctl bootout gui/501/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f
  launchctl bootstrap gui/501 /var/folders/76/w5rbbmt53d9f9wzqs3kzq3580000gn/T/openclaw-launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f-As3pt8/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f.plist
  launchctl enable gui/501/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f
  launchctl kickstart -k gui/501/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f
  launchctl print gui/501/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f
  node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway call health --json --url ws://127.0.0.1:19532 --token <redacted> --timeout 5000
  node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway status --json --url ws://127.0.0.1:19532 --token <redacted>
  launchctl bootout gui/501/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f

  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/launchd.test.ts src/daemon/program-args.test.ts src/commands/daemon-install-helpers.test.ts
  pnpm format:check src/daemon/launchd.ts src/daemon/launchd-plist.ts src/daemon/launchd.test.ts
  node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/daemon/launchd.ts src/daemon/launchd-plist.ts src/daemon/launchd.test.ts
  git diff --check
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** Live macOS launchd proof output from `macos-launchd-live-proof-2.out`:
  ```json
  {
    "platform": "Darwin 25.5.0",
    "arch": "arm64",
    "node": "v24.15.0",
    "head": "e977dc1fc177",
    "label": "ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f",
    "serviceTarget": "gui/501/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f",
    "port": 19532,
    "programArguments": [
      "/bin/sh",
      "/var/folders/.../.openclaw-state/service-env/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f-env-wrapper.sh",
      "/var/folders/.../.openclaw-state/service-env/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f.env",
      "/Users/zhangguiping/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/node",
      "--import",
      "tsx",
      "/Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/openclaw-87199-proof/src/entry.ts",
      "gateway",
      "--port",
      "19532",
      "--allow-unconfigured"
    ],
    "plistExecutable": "/bin/sh",
    "secretInPlist": false,
    "envFileExists": true,
    "envFileContainsToken": true,
    "wrapperModeBefore": "0700",
    "wrapperModeAfter": "0600",
    "bootstrap": { "code": 0 },
    "enable": { "code": 0 },
    "kickstart": { "code": 0 },
    "runtime": { "status": "running", "state": "active", "pid": 85513, "cachedLabel": false },
    "gatewayHealth": { "code": 0, "stdout": { "ok": true } },
    "gatewayStatus": { "code": 0, "rpc": { "ok": true } },
    "gatewayStatusRpcOk": true,
    "noPermissionDenied": true
  }
  ```

  `launchctl print gui/501/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f` showed the real service running from `/bin/sh`:
  ```text
  state = running
  program = /bin/sh
  arguments = {
    /bin/sh
    /var/folders/.../.openclaw-state/service-env/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f-env-wrapper.sh
    /var/folders/.../.openclaw-state/service-env/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f.env
    /Users/zhangguiping/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/node
    --import
    tsx
    /Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/openclaw-87199-proof/src/entry.ts
    gateway
    --port
    19532
    --allow-unconfigured
  }
  working directory = /Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/openclaw-87199-proof
  pid = 85513
  ```

  Gateway RPC proof from the launchd-started process:
  ```text
  gateway call health: code=0, ok=true, durationMs=43
  gateway status: code=0, service.runtime.status=running, service.runtime.state=active, rpc.ok=true, port.listeners[0].pid=85513, ppid=1
  launchd stdout tail: [gateway] ready; [heartbeat] started; [ws] ⇄ res ✓ health 50ms cached=true
  cleanup: launchctl bootout gui/501/ai.openclaw.launchd-proof-fa9d9c1f code=0
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** macOS launchd successfully bootstrapped and kickstarted the generated LaunchAgent even after the generated wrapper was made non-executable (`0600`). The service ran as `/bin/sh`, loaded environment values from the generated env file, started the gateway on port 19532, and `gateway call health` plus `gateway status` both succeeded against the launchd-owned process. The live proof also confirms secrets stayed out of the plist (`secretInPlist=false`) while the env file contained the token (`envFileContainsToken=true`). The gateway command remains the existing supported `gateway --port <port>` form; this PR does not change it to a literal `gateway run` subcommand, and the launchd health/status proof shows that command starts the gateway correctly.
- **What was not tested:** I did not mount a separate external home volume. Instead, the proof used a temporary macOS HOME/state directory and changed the generated wrapper to `0600` before launchd bootstrap to cover the same direct-spawn permission failure surface that this patch removes. No other known gaps.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix.

## Review findings addressed

- The LaunchAgent proof now runs on macOS with real `launchctl bootstrap`, `launchctl kickstart`, `launchctl print`, `gateway call health`, and `gateway status` against the generated service.
- The proof verifies the raw plist shape launchd consumes, confirms `/bin/sh` is the program launchd starts, confirms owner-only env-file loading, and confirms there is no `Permission denied`, `EX_CONFIG`, or `posix_spawn` failure in the launchd-started gateway path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test plus live macOS launchd proof.
- Target test file: `src/daemon/launchd.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: A LaunchAgent with service environment entries serializes raw `ProgramArguments` as `/bin/sh`, generated wrapper path, generated env file path, then the original gateway command; an old wrapper-first plist reads back and restart-rewrites into that same shell-first shape.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The unit regression sits at the plist writer/readback boundary that launchd consumes, while the live proof exercises the macOS launchd runtime path without mocking launchd.
- `src/daemon/program-args.test.ts` remains green to confirm the existing `gateway --port <port>` command form is unchanged.
- `src/commands/daemon-install-helpers.test.ts` remains green to confirm service env planning still passes wrapper/env values through correctly.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: When service environment entries existed, LaunchAgent generation wrote `ProgramArguments` as `[wrapperPath, envFilePath, ...gatewayCommand]`. On macOS setups where the generated wrapper lives on a permission-constrained home/state volume, launchd could fail at `posix_spawn` before the wrapper loaded the env file or execed the gateway. Status/readback code normalized the command for display, which hid the raw plist shape that launchd actually executes.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Tests asserted normalized program arguments but did not assert the raw generated plist array, and the earlier proof did not run real macOS `launchctl bootstrap`/`kickstart` against the generated LaunchAgent.

## Merge risk

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Affected macOS users with service env entries, especially external-home setups, can get an immediately exiting gateway LaunchAgent. This keeps the gateway unavailable after install/restart.
- **What did NOT change:** This only changes macOS LaunchAgent env-wrapper serialization/readback; it does not change gateway CLI command construction, service env collection, provider auth, plist inline `EnvironmentVariables`, Linux/Windows service behavior, or the `gateway --port <port>` command shape.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary is `prepareLaunchAgentProgramArguments` plus `readLaunchAgentProgramArgumentsFromFile`; generation and restart repair now share the same canonical shell-first wrapper contract while legacy wrapper-first plists are normalized only during read/rewrite.
- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk:platform`, `merge-risk:compatibility`.
- **Risk explanation:** The change affects macOS LaunchAgent plist generation and legacy plist rewrite behavior. It does not change gateway CLI command construction or embed env values into the plist.
- **Why acceptable:** The patch is limited to the launchd env-wrapper boundary, keeps old wrapper-first plists readable, preserves owner-only env-file loading, and now has focused tests plus a live macOS launchd proof for the generated command shape.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the LaunchAgent env-wrapper root cause. The only remaining scope note is intentional: the gateway command remains the existing supported `gateway --port <port>` run path rather than changing CLI command construction in this PR.
- **Patch quality notes:** Minimal root-cause change at the LaunchAgent generation/readback source of truth; no fallback, retry, timeout, or status-only workaround.

target.title: fix(discord): fall back to text when voice delivery fails
target.body:
## Summary

This fixes a Discord final-reply failure mode I hit locally: when an `audioAsVoice` reply failed during voice-note delivery, the reply could disappear completely instead of falling back to visible text.

- Try Discord voice-note delivery first, as before.
- If voice delivery fails and there is visible text, send that text instead.
- If the visible text was stripped into a hidden TTS supplement, use that supplement text as the fallback.
- Keep the change scoped to the Discord payload path; direct media sending and unrelated voice behavior are intentionally out of scope.

Success here is pretty simple: if Discord voice delivery fails, the user still gets the answer as text.

Reviewers should focus on the `audioAsVoice` payload ordering and fallback behavior, especially reply targets, TTS supplement handling, and avoiding duplicate text after an already-delivered supplement.

## Linked context

Closes: N/A

Related: #84952, #85173

This came from a real local OpenClaw Discord setup where a final reply tried to go out as a voice note and failed before any visible reply was sent.

Issue #84952 / PR #85173 are in the same Discord `audioAsVoice` fallback area, but they cover a different failure mode: voice adapter unavailable in cron/text-only delivery, with fallback to text+media attachment. This PR covers the final payload path where voice delivery is attempted and then rejects; the intended fallback here is visible text, including hidden TTS supplement text when that is the only available transcript.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord `audioAsVoice` final replies could fail the voice path and send no text fallback.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on this branch, using a real Discord bot/account and a private Discord test channel.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the patched `sendDiscordOutboundPayload` path directly with `audioAsVoice: true`, visible fallback text, and deliberately invalid audio input so the voice conversion path failed.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "voiceAttempted": true,
  "voiceFailure": "Command failed: /usr/bin/ffmpeg -y -i /tmp/openclaw/voice-src-mMImTx/input.html ...",
  "result": {
    "messageId": "1511791135129997482",
    "channelId": "1510545702881595392",
    "channel": "discord"
  },
  "fetchedStatus": 200,
  "fetchedMessage": {
    "content": "OpenClaw PR proof 2026-06-03T17:58:13.575Z: voice delivery failed on purpose, text fallback worked.",
    "attachmentCount": 0,
    "type": 0
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: voice delivery was attempted, ffmpeg failed during conversion, and the fallback text was still posted to Discord. Fetching the message back from Discord showed matching text and `attachmentCount: 0`, so it was a text fallback rather than a voice attachment.
- What was not tested: I did not run a full end-to-end gateway deployment with `/usr/bin/ffmpeg` removed from the host.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the original local failure involved ffmpeg not being available to the runtime. I could not safely rename `/usr/bin/ffmpeg` in this environment because non-interactive sudo is not available, so the live proof forces a realistic voice-delivery failure by giving ffmpeg invalid audio input instead. That still exercises the patched failure boundary: `sendVoice` rejects, then the payload path sends text.
- Before evidence: before this change, the `audioAsVoice` branch awaited voice delivery directly. If that threw, the payload send failed before visible text or hidden TTS supplement text could be sent.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `CI=true pnpm test extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit`
- `pnpm test:extension discord`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Regression coverage added/updated:

- falls back to visible text when Discord voice delivery rejects
- falls back to hidden TTS supplement text when visible text was stripped for voice delivery
- preserves the reply target when falling back from voice to text
- suppresses duplicate TTS supplement text when it was already visibly delivered

What failed before this fix: a rejected Discord voice send in the final payload path could prevent any text reply from being delivered.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

Highest-risk area: the ordering around Discord `audioAsVoice` payloads, because voice notes cannot include text, while fallback text still needs to preserve reply threading and avoid duplicates.

How that risk is mitigated: the change is limited to the Discord payload send path, keeps successful voice delivery behavior first, and adds focused regression coverage for the fallback cases and duplicate-suppression case.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

Still waiting on: upstream CI and any maintainer preference on wording or fallback policy.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: `codex review --base origin/main` came back clean with no findings after the latest branch state.

target.title: fix(announce-delivery): backfill effectiveDirectOrigin.to from requester session entry
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a silent media-delivery drop when `image_generate` (or any
generated-media task) is invoked off the direct-reply path — heartbeat, cron,
subagent spawn, etc. — where `agentTo` is undefined.

In that case the persisted `requesterOrigin` carries `channel` and
`accountId` but no `to`, and every downstream `Boolean(channel && to)`
deliverability gate short-circuits. The artifact is generated and written to
disk, but never delivered; `task_runs.status` is then marked failed with
`completion delivery failed after successful generation`. User sees nothing.

The fix is a one-merge change in
`src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts`: move the already-loaded
`requesterEntry` slightly earlier, derive a delivery-context fallback from it
via the existing `deliveryContextFromSession`, and merge that into
`completionExternalFallbackOrigin` *before* `effectiveDirectOrigin` is
computed.

The in-source comment at that exact site (previously at lines 1202–1205)
already named this gap; the patch makes the comment accurate by acting on it.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#86034 (Hypothesis A only — origin missing `to`).

## What the patch does

- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` — Move the `loadRequesterSessionEntry` call up; compute `requesterSessionDeliveryFallback = deliveryContextFromSession(requesterEntry)`; merge it into the existing fallback chain.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` — New regression: completion origin missing `to`, requester session entry has `lastTo` → `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is populated and external delivery is selected (`deliver: true`).

Total non-test diff: ~14 lines.

## Risk / blast radius

Low. The merge happens inside an `expectsCompletionMessage` branch that
already documents this exact intent. `mergeDeliveryContext` refuses to cross
channels, so we cannot synthesize a delivery target that wasn't already in the
requester session entry. Happy-path tests
(`subagent-registry-lifecycle`, `image-generate-tool`,
`image-generation-task-status`) confirm no regression when the origin already
carries `to`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: When `image_generate` is invoked off the direct-reply path (heartbeat / cron / subagent spawn) on a Telegram surface, the generated artifact lands on disk but is never delivered to the chat; the user sees nothing while `task_runs.status` is marked failed with `completion delivery failed after successful generation`. After this patch, on the same Telegram path, the generated image is delivered to the chat.

Real environment tested: Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end via `openclaw-mantis` workflow, executed against a real OpenClaw setup driving a real Telegram client. Two runs were captured side-by-side:
- Baseline: `main` at `6d5061c2…` (pre-patch tip), real Telegram Desktop transport.
- Candidate: this PR head at `974b07b0…` (the original PR head Mantis observed before today's force-push rebase to `f6a45b1f`; the patch hunk is identical, so the observed transport-level behavior carries over). Real Telegram Desktop transport.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end proof was driven by openclaw-mantis.
# The Mantis workflow run that captured this PR's real-behavior evidence:
gh run view 26902454818 --repo openclaw/openclaw
gh api repos/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts

# Local supplemental regression suite on the patched head (f6a45b1f):
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
- Mantis live Telegram Desktop run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818
- Mantis artifacts (recordings / screenshots / MP4s side-by-side): https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts/7393384873
- Mantis comment with embedded GIFs / screenshots / MP4s is in this PR thread.

```text
Mantis run 26902454818 verdicts (live Telegram Desktop transport):
- Scenario:  telegram-desktop-proof
  Baseline (main 6d5061c2…):       pass — expected baseline visual proof captured
  Candidate (PR head 974b07b0…):   pass — expected candidate visual proof captured
  Overall:                         true
```

Observed result after fix: On the previously broken off-direct-reply path the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop client visibly received the generated image. Local supplemental regression: `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` (with the new regression case), `media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`, `subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts`, `image-generate-tool.test.ts`, `image-generation-task-status.test.ts` — all 5 files, 194 tests, green on the patched head. The new regression case in `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` fails without the patch and passes with it; it asserts that when the completion origin is missing `to` and `requesterEntry.lastTo` is populated, `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is backfilled and `deliver: true` is selected — exactly the gate that was short-circuiting in the reported bug.

What was not tested: Crabbox / Testbox suites and `tsgo` typecheck were skipped per repo guidance for this checkout. No live Telegram Bot API account other than the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop scenario was exercised. Hypothesis B (the `wakeTaskCompletion` retry asymmetry described under Follow-up) is intentionally out of scope for this PR.

## Follow-up (Hypothesis B)

Hypothesis B from the review — `wakeTaskCompletion → false` does not retry
`tryDeliverMediaGenerationDirect` (only the `throw` branch does), and the
in-wake fallback gates on `MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` which
excludes `requester_abandoned` and the `deliver=false` reason from Hypothesis
A — is a real but separable robustness gap. It crosses the larger
`MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` contract surface and warrants a
"delivery contract decision" maintainer review on its own. See
`src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts:425-468` for the
asymmetric branch.

Fixing A alone unblocks the reported Telegram repro: once `origin.to` is
populated, the in-wake direct fallback (which also checks `origin.to`)
likewise becomes viable.

target.title: fix: recognize bundle-mcp as known synthetic plugin in tool policy allowlist
target.body:
## Problem

`openclaw doctor` reports `bundle-mcp` as an "unknown allowlist entry" when it is included in `tools.allow`:

```
[tools] agents.main.tools.allow allowlist contains unknown entries (bundle-mcp).
These entries won't match any tool unless the plugin is enabled.
```

This happens because `analyzeAllowlistByToolType()` only considers entries valid if they match a registered plugin ID or a core tool name. `bundle-mcp` is a synthetic plugin ID used for MCP bundled tools — it is only registered at runtime when MCP servers connect, so it is never visible during doctor's offline tool discovery pass.

## Fix

Add a `KNOWN_SYNTHETIC_PLUGIN_IDS` set (containing `"bundle-mcp"`) to the `isPluginEntry` check in `analyzeAllowlistByToolType()`, so that known synthetic plugin IDs are recognized as valid plugin entries regardless of MCP runtime state.

## Testing

- `openclaw doctor` no longer reports `bundle-mcp` as unknown
- MCP tools continue to function correctly at runtime (the allowlist expansion via `bundle-mcp` still works)
- No behavior change for other tool policy logic
