commands.author: IWhatsskill

target.title: [AI-assisted] fix(feishu): let active followups reach queue
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Feishu same-chat message dispatch waits behind the plugin sequential queue until the prior full agent turn finishes, so `messages.queue.mode = "collect"` follow-ups can reach the shared queue after the session is no longer active and become separate `run-now` turns. The first PR version also used queued-key presence as active state, which could bypass too early in same-tick delivery.
- Solution: Keep the first ordinary same-chat task on the Feishu sequential queue, let later ordinary same-key tasks bypass only after that key is actually running, and keep `:control` and `/btw` lanes serialized.
- What changed: Added running-key introspection to the Feishu sequential queue, changed the bypass predicate from queued/present to running-only, and added regression coverage for active follow-ups, same-tick ordinary ordering, queue API state, and active control/`/btw` lane serialization.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No shared queue policy change, no dependency or lockfile change, no control-lane or `/btw` bypass, no Feishu config change, and no live tenant integration change.

## Motivation

- Feishu users who enable active-run queue modes should get the same collect/steer opportunity as channels that do not serialize a full message handler before it reaches the shared queue layer.
- The issue is user-visible because follow-up messages fragment into separate turns, increasing token use and splitting conversation context.
- The repair must preserve Feishu ordering around the first ordinary task and dedicated control lanes, so the bypass has to distinguish queued work from actually running work.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #54409
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Ordinary Feishu same-chat follow-up messages can reach the active-run queue path while an earlier ordinary Feishu task for that chat is running; same-tick ordinary events do not bypass before the first task starts; `:control` and `/btw` lanes remain serialized.
- Real environment tested (affected runtime/layer, not just "unit test"): Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout on the PR testserver, Feishu plugin handler integration path, Feishu same-key sequential queue, and shared active-run queue behavior through the existing source test harness.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts -t "same-tick ordinary same-chat"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts -t "same-chat"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/sequential-queue.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/sequential-key.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/queue-policy.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run-queue.test.ts src/utils/queue-helpers.test.ts
pnpm check:changed
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Latest patched head:
f349ef6ca5 fix(feishu): gate active followup bypass on running tasks

same-tick ordinary same-chat regression:
1 file passed, 1 test passed

same-chat focused diagnostic:
1 file passed, 4 tests passed
- ordinary same-chat follow-up reaches the gateway while the first ordinary task is active
- same-tick ordinary same-chat events stay ordered until the first task starts
- same-chat :control lane stays serialized while active
- same-chat :btw lane stays serialized while active

Feishu focused proof:
4 files passed, 123 tests passed

Shared queue focused proof:
3 files passed, 20 tests passed

git diff --check:
clean

pnpm check:changed:
blocked before changed checks ran because the selected crabbox binary failed its basic --version/--help sanity checks. No green check:changed claim is made.
```

- Observed result after fix: The same-chat active follow-up regression reaches the handler/gateway path while the first ordinary task is running, the same-tick regression keeps a second ordinary event from bypassing before the first task starts, and the focused control/`/btw` diagnostics confirm those lanes still wait behind the active lane task instead of bypassing Feishu serialization.
- What was not tested: No live Feishu tenant, real Feishu transport, external provider, full repository suite, or successful `pnpm check:changed` run.
- Before evidence:

```text
Before the running-key fix, with the same-tick regression applied to the previous PR head:

extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts:
expect(handleMessage).not.toHaveBeenCalled() failed because the old queued-key bypass called handleMessage before the first queued ordinary task had started.
The received event was msg-same-tick-second.

Before the original production change, with the active follow-up regression applied to the old code:

extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts:
Test "lets same-chat followups reach the gateway while an ordinary queue task is active" timed out after 120000ms.
```

- Visual proof:
  - Before screenshot/video: Not applicable.
  - After screenshot/video: Not applicable.
  - If not applicable, why: This is a runtime queue/handler behavior bug without UI output; command output and test timeline assertions are the appropriate artifacts.
- Remaining proof gap: No redacted live Feishu tenant transport proof was run, and `pnpm check:changed` was blocked by the crabbox binary sanity failure on the test host.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Feishu serialized the full `handleFeishuMessage` path per same-chat key. Later ordinary same-chat messages were held until the prior agent turn completed, so the shared active-run queue saw `isActive=false` and treated them as new `run-now` turns. The first PR version repaired that too broadly by treating queued-key presence as active state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing Feishu coverage did not assert that ordinary same-chat follow-ups can reach the gateway while a prior ordinary task is active, that queued-not-yet-running same-tick events stay ordered, or that control and `/btw` lanes stay serialized under the active-key bypass.
- Contributing context (if known): Feishu still needs same-key ordering for the first ordinary task and dedicated control lanes; this fix avoids changing the shared queue policy and keeps `:control`/`/btw` serialization intact.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/feishu/src/sequential-queue.test.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/sequential-key.test.ts`, and `extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: A later ordinary Feishu same-chat follow-up should enter the handler/gateway path only while the first ordinary task is actually running; same-tick queued-not-yet-running ordinary events must not overtake the first task; control and `/btw` lanes must remain serialized.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The sequential queue test locks the `has` versus `isRunning` state boundary, the sequential-key test keeps lane suffixes explicit, and the bot tests exercise the Feishu dispatch boundary where the reported behavior and the same-tick ordering risk occur.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing Feishu tests covered sequential ordering and related reaction/control behavior, but not active-run follow-up delivery through the shared queue path, queued-vs-running bypass timing, or the active-lane control/`/btw` preservation case.
- If no new test is added, why not: New regression tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Feishu same-chat follow-up messages sent during an active ordinary run can now be observed by the shared active-run queue while the session is active. That lets configured collect/steer behavior operate instead of waiting for the prior full turn to finish and starting a separate turn.

The first ordinary same-chat task remains queued, same-tick ordinary events stay ordered until that task starts, and control and `/btw` lanes remain queued through their existing sequential keys.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
msg A -> Feishu same-chat queue -> full handler/agent turn -> completes
msg B waits behind A ---------------------------------------> handler sees inactive session -> run-now

First PR version risk:
msg A queued but not started
msg B arrives in the same tick -> queued-key presence bypass -> may overtake msg A

After:
msg A -> Feishu same-chat queue -> starts running -> full handler/agent turn still active
msg B -> ordinary running-key bypass -> handler reaches shared active-run queue -> collect/steer path can apply

Still serialized:
queued-not-running ordinary events, :control lane, and /btw lane -> Feishu sequential queue
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable PR testserver checkout
- Runtime/container: Node/pnpm project environment installed with the frozen lockfile
- Model/provider: No external model/provider; tests use existing source harnesses and test doubles
- Integration/channel (if any): Feishu plugin handler dispatch path, same-key lane routing, and shared active-run queue path
- Relevant config (redacted): Queue-mode behavior exercised by the regression harness; no real credentials or private accounts used

### Steps

1. Add the active-key and same-chat active follow-up regression tests to the old code and confirm the old behavior fails.
2. Add the same-tick ordinary same-chat regression to the previous PR head and confirm the queued-key bypass can fire before the first task starts.
3. Apply the production change that tracks running keys separately from queued keys and bypasses only ordinary running same-key dispatches.
4. Run the focused Feishu tests, adjacent shared queue tests, same-chat lane diagnostic, same-tick diagnostic, and whitespace check after the patch.

### Expected

- Ordinary Feishu follow-up messages can reach the shared active-run queue while the first same-chat ordinary task is actually running.
- Same-tick ordinary Feishu events do not bypass before the first queued task starts.
- `:control` and `/btw` lanes remain serialized.
- No dependency, lockfile, or global queue policy changes are needed.

### Actual

- Before: the original active follow-up regression timed out on the old code; the same-tick regression failed on the previous PR head because `handleMessage` was called for `msg-same-tick-second` before the first task started.
- After: the same-tick regression passed, the same-chat diagnostic passed 4 tests, the focused Feishu suite passed 123 tests, the adjacent shared queue suite passed 20 tests, and `git diff --check` was clean.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
Before original fix:
same-chat active follow-up regression timed out after 120000ms

Before running-key fix on the previous PR head:
expect(handleMessage).not.toHaveBeenCalled() failed; received msg-same-tick-second before the first queued ordinary task had started

After f349ef6ca5:
same-tick ordinary same-chat regression: 1 file passed, 1 test passed
same-chat focused diagnostic: 1 file passed, 4 tests passed
Feishu focused proof: 4 files passed, 123 tests passed
Shared queue focused proof: 3 files passed, 20 tests passed
git diff --check: clean

Blocked validation:
pnpm check:changed could not complete because the crabbox binary failed its basic --version/--help sanity checks.
```

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Active-key tracking, queued-vs-running state separation, ordinary same-chat active follow-up bypass, same-tick ordinary ordering before first task start, preservation of `:control`/`/btw` serialization, Feishu bot dispatch regression, adjacent shared queue policy tests, and whitespace check.
- Edge cases checked: First ordinary task still enters the sequential queue; `has` remains true for queued or running work while `isRunning` is true only inside the wrapped task; only an already-running ordinary same-key dispatch bypasses; control and `/btw` sequential keys are not bypassed; bypassed task errors still release processing and are logged; same-chat control and `/btw` events wait for the active lane task before dispatching.
- What you did **not** verify: Live Feishu tenant behavior, external provider behavior, full repo-wide validation, successful `pnpm check:changed`, screenshots/video, or production plugin package behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No inline review threads were changed by this body update. The remaining live Feishu tenant proof ask still needs an environment with real Feishu access.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Not applicable.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Bypassing the same-chat sequential queue could accidentally weaken Feishu ordering guarantees.
  - Mitigation: The bypass is limited to ordinary same-key messages while that key is actually running; the first ordinary task, queued-not-yet-running same-tick events, `:control`, and `/btw` lanes still use the sequential queue, with regression coverage for the active-key API and active control/`/btw` serialization.
- Risk: A live Feishu tenant may expose timing or delivery behavior not represented by the source harness.
  - Mitigation: The PR body calls out the missing live transport proof, and the regression targets the exact dispatch boundary and queue behavior described in the issue.
- Risk: `pnpm check:changed` might still reveal a broader issue once crabbox works on the test host.
  - Mitigation: Focused Feishu and shared queue tests plus `git diff --check` passed, and the blocked changed-check is explicitly reported instead of claimed green.

commands.author: zenglingbiao

target.title: fix(agents): make wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook idempotent to prevent double hook execution (fixes #92973)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook` is not idempotent — when called on an already-wrapped tool (e.g. after `normalizeToolParameters` constructs a fresh tool via `{...tool, parameters}`), the hook executes twice per invocation because each wrapper layer calls `runBeforeToolCallHook` before delegating to the inner `execute`.
- **Root Cause**: Two-fold. (1) `createOpenClawCodingTools` runs `normalizeToolParameters` then unconditionally calls `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook` on every tool. (2) `normalizeToolParameters` uses object spread `{...tool}` which drops non-enumerable Symbol-keyed metadata (`BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_SOURCE_TOOL`, `BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_HOOK_CONTEXT`), so an already-wrapped tool that passes through normalization loses the reference to its source tool — making it impossible to safely rebuild a single wrapper.
- **Fix**: Two changes. (1) In `normalizeToolParameters`: after object spread, copy all Symbol-keyed properties from the original tool via `Object.getOwnPropertySymbols` + `Object.defineProperty`, so wrapper metadata survives normalization. (2) In `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook`: when receiving an already-wrapped tool, extract the source tool via `BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_SOURCE_TOOL` and re-wrap with the current context. Together these ensure a normalized already-wrapped tool is rebuilt as exactly one wrapper with fresh context.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/agent-tools.schema.ts` — `normalizeToolParameters` now preserves Symbol-keyed metadata through object spread (+11 lines)
  - `src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts` — idempotency guard: unwrap source tool and re-wrap with current context (+12 lines)
  - `src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts` — two focused regression tests: direct re-wrap + normalized-wrapper path (+53 lines)
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Individual call sites (`createOpenClawCodingTools`, `openclaw-tools.ts`, etc.) — the self-guard covers them all
  - `rewrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook` — still available for explicit re-wrap use cases
  - Tool execution behavior for non-wrapped tools — the guard is a no-op for tools without the wrapper marker

### Reproduction

1. Register a `before_tool_call` plugin hook that logs each invocation
2. Trigger a cron or isolated-agent run that calls `createOpenClawCodingTools`
3. `normalizeToolParameters` does `{...tool, parameters}` → drops non-enumerable `BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_SOURCE_TOOL` Symbol
4. Unconditional `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook` on the normalized tool cannot recover the source → wraps the already-wrapped execute again
5. **Before this PR**: the hook fires twice, and after the first round of fixes only a degraded fallback path was taken
6. **After this PR**: metadata survives normalization, source tool is recovered, exactly one wrapper is built with the current context

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (92973)**: `before_tool_call` plugin hooks executing twice when tools pass through the `createOpenClawCodingTools` pipeline (`normalizeToolParameters` → `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook`). The fix preserves Symbol-keyed wrapper metadata through `normalizeToolParameters` and makes `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook` rebuild a single wrapper with current context for already-wrapped tools.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — Vitest with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` against the full before_tool_call test suite

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.schema.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
RUN  v4.1.7

Test Files  4 passed (4)
     Tests  127 passed (127)
  Duration  23.47s

Regression tests:
  "re-wraps an already-wrapped tool with the new context instead of
   returning it unchanged":
    isToolWrappedWithBeforeToolCallHook(wrappedOnce) → true
    isToolWrappedWithBeforeToolCallHook(reWrapped) → true
    reWrapped !== wrappedOnce (fresh wrapper with new context) → true
    execute called exactly once → true

  "re-wraps a normalized already-wrapped tool with the current context
   (createOpenClawCodingTools path)":
    wrap → normalizeToolParameters({...tool}) → re-wrap → execute once
    isToolWrappedWithBeforeToolCallHook(reWrapped) → true
    reWrapped !== normalized (Symbol metadata survived normalization) → true
    execute called exactly once → true

All 127 tests pass with no regressions.
```

**Observed result after fix**: When an already-wrapped tool passes through `normalizeToolParameters`, the `Object.getOwnPropertySymbols` loop preserves `BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_SOURCE_TOOL` and `BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_HOOK_CONTEXT`. When `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook` then receives this normalized tool, it finds the source tool reference and rebuilds exactly one wrapper with the current run/session/workspace context. The full pipeline (wrap → normalize → re-wrap → execute) results in the underlying tool executing exactly once.

**What was not tested**: Live cron/isolated-agent end-to-end spawn with a real `before_tool_call` plugin was not driven in this environment (requires a configured plugin + cron schedule + real provider).

**Repro confirmation**: The new "normalized already-wrapped tool" regression test replicates the exact `createOpenClawCodingTools` pipeline: wrap a tool → normalize it with `normalizeToolParameters` (which does `{...tool, parameters}`) → re-wrap with new context. Before the Symbol-preservation fix, `normalizeToolParameters` dropped the source-tool reference and the re-wrap fell through to a degraded path. After the fix, metadata survives and the re-wrap produces a fresh single wrapper with the new context.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: Copying all Symbol-keyed properties in `normalizeToolParameters` may preserve Symbols from other subsystems that shouldn't be shared between tool instances.
  **Mitigation**: The Symbols on tool objects are metadata markers deliberately placed there by the wrapping infrastructure. `preserveToolMeta` already copies plugin and channel metadata for the same reason. If a future subsystem adds a Symbol that must NOT be copied, it should use a non-own Symbol (prototype) or clean up after normalization.

- **Risk**: Re-wrapping an already-wrapped tool discards the previous context in favor of the new one.
  **Mitigation**: This is the intended behavior — the caller explicitly passes a new `ctx`. Callers that need to preserve context should not call `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook` again.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] agents
- [x] plugin-sdk (before_tool_call hook infrastructure)

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.schema.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

- **[P1] Fix the normalized-wrapper path so metadata survives normalization and one wrapper is rebuilt with the current hook context**: ✅ Fixed — `normalizeToolParameters` now preserves all Symbol-keyed metadata via `Object.getOwnPropertySymbols` + `Object.defineProperty`. The `BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_SOURCE_TOOL` reference survives object spread, so `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook` can extract the source tool and rebuild exactly one wrapper with the current context.
- **[P1] Add regression coverage that normalizes an already wrapped tool before rewrapping and asserts one hook call with the new context**: ✅ Added — new test "re-wraps a normalized already-wrapped tool with the current context (createOpenClawCodingTools path)" replicates the full pipeline: wrap → normalize (`{...tool, parameters}`) → re-wrap → execute once.
- **[P1] Add redacted live cron or isolated-agent plugin-hook output**: Live cron/isolated-agent end-to-end requires a configured plugin + cron schedule + real provider, unavailable in this headless Linux environment. The normalized-wrapper regression test proves the code-level behavior for the exact pipeline described in the issue.

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #92973

target.title: fix(memory-core): exclude archive transcripts from dreaming session corpus collection
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: Dreaming session corpus ingestion used `listSessionFilesForAgent` which returns usage-counted files including `.deleted` and `.reset` archive transcripts. This caused archive transcripts to be re-ingested as duplicates alongside the current primary transcript, polluting the dreaming corpus with up to 91% automation noise on cron-heavy deployments.
- **Fix**: Skip archive artifacts in the dreaming session corpus collection loop via `isSessionArchiveArtifactName`, mirroring the existing checkpoint skip pattern. No change to `listSessionFilesForAgent` or any shared helper — memory_search archive indexing is unaffected.
- **Scope**: Only the dreaming corpus collection path in `collectSessionIngestionBatches`. Light/REM phase logic, narrative writer, and all other dreaming paths are unchanged.

## Linked context

Related #90313

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Archived transcripts (`.deleted.*`, `.reset.*`) no longer pollute dreaming session corpus during light dreaming ingestion.

**Real environment tested:** Node v22 on Linux (WSL2), branch `fix/issue-90313-dreaming-corpus-archive-filter`, head `29b6db19b7a`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_CONFIG=test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts \
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts
```

This runs the regression `excludes archive (.deleted/.reset) transcripts from session corpus collection`, which exercises the patched production path end-to-end:

`beforeAgentReply` → `runPhaseIfTriggered` (light) → `runLightDreaming` → `ingestSessionTranscriptSignals` → `collectSessionIngestionBatches` (archive skip at `dreaming-phases.ts:863-865`).

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/princeht/fire_project/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
   Duration  8.51s
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 14.28s
```

Session corpus written by the light dreaming sweep (`memory/.dreams/session-corpus/2026-04-16.txt`) contains primary transcript lines and excludes archive-only content:

```text
User: Document the memory-core plugin setup.
Assistant: I documented the memory-core plugin setup.
```

Archive-only strings verified absent from corpus:

```text
Archive message that must not appear.
I replied to the archived session.
Reset-archive content that must be excluded.
```

**Observed result after fix:** After a patched light dreaming sweep over primary + `.deleted` + `.reset` transcripts, only the primary transcript content is ingested into the session corpus; archive transcript bodies are filtered out before `appendSessionCorpusLines`.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway heartbeat on a production OpenClaw deployment with real cron subagent spawns.

## Tests and validation

- Regression test added: `excludes archive (.deleted/.reset) transcripts from session corpus collection` — creates primary + `.deleted` + `.reset` transcripts, runs light dreaming, asserts archive content absent from corpus while primary content remains
- Existing test `drops archive, cron, and heartbeat chatter from fresh session corpus output` continues to pass

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes — dreaming corpus will no longer contain content from archive transcripts; dream quality should improve on cron-heavy deployments
- Config, environment, or migration change: No
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution change: No
- Highest-risk area: Low — the change is purely subtractive (removes files from ingestion, never adds)
- No data is deleted; archive files remain on disk and remain indexed by memory_search

## Current review state

Next action: `@clawsweeper re-review` on head `29b6db19b7a`.
Waiting on: proof label refresh after re-review.
Bot comments addressed: PR body now documents patched light-dreaming corpus collection proof (not just the filename predicate).

Related #90313

target.title: fix(sessions): persist sender metadata in user turn transcript JSONL
target.body:
## Summary

- Thread `senderId`/`senderName`/`senderUsername` from the channel inbound context into the persisted user-turn transcript message
- Group chat session JSONL records now include `__openclaw` sender identity metadata, matching the metadata already present in direct (1:1) session records
- When no sender metadata is available (empty/null values), the `__openclaw` block is omitted — preserving existing behavior
- `senderE164` (phone number) is intentionally excluded from the persisted fields for privacy

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix / [ ] Feature / [ ] Refactor / [ ] Docs / [ ] Security / [ ] Chore

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / [ ] Skills / [ ] Auth / [ ] Memory / [ ] Integrations / [ ] API / [ ] UI/UX / [x] Sessions / [ ] CI

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Group chat user messages persisted to the session JSONL transcript were missing `__openclaw` sender identity metadata (`senderId`, `senderName`, etc.) that is present in direct (1:1) session records.

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, pnpm test + production function via `node --import tsx`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Production `appendUserTurnTranscriptMessage` with sender metadata:

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { appendUserTurnTranscriptMessage } from './src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.ts';
import fs from 'node:fs';
import os from 'node:os';
import path from 'node:path';

const dir = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'openclaw-proof-'));
const transcriptPath = path.join(dir, 'session.jsonl');

await appendUserTurnTranscriptMessage({
  transcriptPath, sessionId: 'session-proof', sessionKey: 'proof', cwd: dir,
  input: {
    text: 'hello from group chat',
    senderId: '8489979671',
    senderName: 'Ram Shenoy',
    senderUsername: 'ram_s',
  },
  updateMode: 'none',
});

const rawLines = fs.readFileSync(transcriptPath, 'utf-8').trim().split('\n');
const msg = JSON.parse(rawLines.filter(l => l.trim()).slice(-1)[0]).message;
console.log('Has __openclaw:', '__openclaw' in msg);
console.log('__openclaw:', JSON.stringify(msg.__openclaw));
"
```

Regression tests:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
Has __openclaw: true
__openclaw: {"senderId":"8489979671","senderName":"Ram Shenoy","senderUsername":"ram_s"}

No-sender case - has __openclaw: false

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  27 passed (27)
```

**Observed result after the fix:** Group chat user turn messages persisted via the production `appendUserTurnTranscriptMessage` now include `__openclaw` sender identity metadata in the JSONL output. Empty/null sender fields omit the envelope.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway session with real Telegram group chat message delivery.

## Root Cause

The shared `UserTurnInput` → `buildPersistedUserTurnMessage` path only persisted text, media, and provenance. Sender identity from the channel inbound context (`SenderId`, `SenderName`, `SenderUsername`) was carried into the agent runtime but not threaded into the user-turn transcript message written to the session JSONL.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target: `src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts`
- Two new test cases: one asserting `__openclaw` envelope with sender metadata, one asserting `__openclaw` is omitted when no sender metadata is provided
- All 27 tests pass

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes — group chat JSONL transcripts now include optional `__openclaw` sender metadata when channel context provides it
- Config, environment, or migration change: No
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution change: No — only public channel identifiers (`senderId`, `senderName`, `senderUsername`) are persisted; `senderE164` is explicitly excluded for privacy; no new auth/credential/sandbox surface
- Highest-risk area: Persisted session transcript metadata (security-boundary / sensitive-data)
- Mitigation: Omit envelope when all sender fields are empty; exclude phone numbers; fields mirror existing 1:1 session metadata contract; no new config/env knobs; regression tests cover both present and absent sender cases

## Current review state

Next action: `@clawsweeper re-review` after security-boundary risk checklist and proof refresh
Waiting on: `proof: sufficient`, `rating: 🦞 diamond lobster`
Bot comments addressed: removed `senderE164` from persisted envelope per privacy feedback

Ref #90531

target.title: fix(agents): apply stale-run liveness check to aborted subagent orphan recovery
target.body:
## Summary

Fix #90766: stale subagent runs (>2h) with `abortedLastRun: true` were exempt from stale-unended pruning, causing very old aborted child sessions to be resurrected after long gateway downtime.

Changes:
- **`subagent-registry-helpers.ts`**: Remove the `abortedLastRun` exemption from `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` — stale aborted runs are now classified as `stale-unended-run` like any other stale run
- **`subagent-orphan-recovery.ts`**: Add `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` check before attempting recovery; await `finalizeInterruptedSubagentRun` and report `failedRuns` when finalization does not update the registry (scheduler retry path)
- **`subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts`**: Add regression coverage for stale finalization success and finalization-failure accounting

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Before: `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` skipped stale-unended classification when `abortedLastRun === true`. `recoverOrphanedSubagentSessions` had no age gate and fire-and-forget finalization could leave stale runs unended without scheduler retry. After: stale aborted runs are pruned at registry restore and finalized during orphan recovery; fresh aborted runs still recover.

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, production functions via `node --import tsx` and focused Vitest.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Production liveness helper (stale vs fresh cutoff):

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { isStaleUnendedSubagentRun } from './src/agents/subagent-run-liveness.ts';
const now = Date.now();
const TWO_HOURS = 2 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
const stale = { createdAt: now - 3 * TWO_HOURS, startedAt: now - 3 * TWO_HOURS };
const fresh = { createdAt: now - 30 * 60 * 1000, startedAt: now - 30 * 60 * 1000 };
console.log('stale aborted (3h):', isStaleUnendedSubagentRun(stale, now));
console.log('fresh aborted (30m):', isStaleUnendedSubagentRun(fresh, now));
"
```

Orphan recovery + persistence regression:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
stale aborted (3h): true
fresh aborted (30m): false

Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  39 passed (39)
```

New orphan-recovery tests confirm:
- stale aborted run (`startedAt` 3h ago) calls `finalizeInterruptedSubagentRun`, does not resume via gateway
- finalization returning `0` increments `failed` and populates `failedRuns` for scheduler retry
- fresh aborted run (`startedAt` 55s ago) still resumes normally (existing test)

**Observed result after the fix:** Stale aborted subagent runs (>2h, no `endedAt`) are finalized instead of resurrected after gateway restart. Fresh aborted runs (<2h) continue through orphan recovery. Finalization failures are accounted in `failedRuns` so the scheduler retry path can recover.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway with multi-hour downtime and real LLM-backed subagents (default stale window is 2h).

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes — very old aborted subagent runs (>2h) will be finalized instead of resurrected after gateway restart
- Config, environment, or migration change: No
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution change: No
- Highest-risk area: Session-state cutoff for restart-aborted child sessions older than the stale window; fresh aborted runs are unchanged
- Mitigation: Shared `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` policy used at both registry restore and orphan recovery; await finalization + `failedRuns` accounting; persistence + orphan-recovery regression tests (39/39 pass)

## Current review state

Next action: `@clawsweeper re-review` after P2 finalization-accounting fix and expanded proof
Waiting on: `proof: sufficient`, `rating: 🦞 diamond lobster`
Bot comments addressed: P2 await-finalization finding fixed in `8ebc547a5e8`

Fixes: #90766

commands.author: Pick-cat

target.title: fix(tools): surface empty availability group diagnostics via onHiddenDiagnostic callback
target.body:
## Summary

- Empty `allOf`/`anyOf` availability groups are authoring errors (malformed descriptors), but the diagnostics returned by `evaluateExpression` are silently consumed by `buildToolPlan` — tools are hidden from the visible plan with no observable signal to the author.
- Adds an `onHiddenDiagnostic` callback to `BuildToolPlanOptions` so callers can surface authoring errors (empty groups, unsupported signal kinds) at registration time.
- Wires `auditPluginToolDescriptors` into `resolvePluginTools` and preserves plugin tool `availability` expressions in `capturePluginToolDescriptor` so registration-time audits see malformed descriptors.
- Adds explicit `AnyAgentTool.availability?: ToolAvailabilityExpression` typing, exports `ToolAvailabilityExpression` from plugin-sdk, and narrows descriptor capture with `isToolAvailabilityExpression` so private metadata objects are not treated as planner expressions.

## Root Cause (full chain)

Symptom: Plugin/core authors define a `ToolDescriptor` with `availability: { anyOf: [] }` (empty group) and the tool silently disappears from the plan with no warning.

Layer 1: `evaluateExpression` in `availability.ts` correctly detects empty `allOf`/`anyOf` arrays and returns an `unsupported-signal` diagnostic with message "Empty availability allOf/anyOf group".

Layer 2: `buildToolPlan` in `planner.ts` receives these diagnostics and hides the tool. The `ToolPlan.hidden` array contains the descriptor and its diagnostics, but nothing reads or logs them.

Layer 3: `BuildToolPlanOptions` provides no mechanism for callers to observe diagnostics. The `unsupported-signal` reason code marks authoring errors that should be surfaced, but there is no callback, logger, or event emitter wired in.

Root cause: The planner has no channel to surface authoring errors back to the registration layer. Empty-group diagnostics are computed but discarded after tool classification.

Missing guardrail: No logging or callback contract in `buildToolPlan` for diagnostics that represent descriptor contract violations (as opposed to runtime unavailability).

## Fix

- `src/tools/types.ts`: Added `onHiddenDiagnostic` to `BuildToolPlanOptions`.
- `src/tools/planner.ts`: Calls `onHiddenDiagnostic` for each diagnostic that hides a tool.
- `src/tools/availability.ts`: Added `isToolAvailabilityExpression` shape guard for descriptor capture.
- `src/agents/tools/common.ts`: Declared `availability?: ToolAvailabilityExpression` on `AnyAgentTool`.
- `src/plugin-sdk/{index,core,plugin-entry}.ts`: Exported `ToolAvailabilityExpression` and `ToolAvailabilitySignal`.
- `src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.ts`: Audits plugin descriptors at registration via `buildToolPlan`.
- `src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts`: Preserves typed `availability` expressions attached to plugin tools when capturing descriptors.
- `src/plugins/tools.ts`: Runs the audit during `resolvePluginTools`.
- Tests: planner, audit helper, descriptor capture (including non-expression metadata guard), and `resolvePluginTools` registration warning coverage.

## What did NOT change (scope boundary)

- `evaluateToolAvailability` and `evaluateExpression` signatures are unchanged — they remain pure functions.
- The `ToolPlan.hidden` array still contains diagnostics (backward compatible); the callback is an additive surface.
- Runtime availability diagnostics (`auth-missing`, `env-missing`, etc.) are not distinguished from authoring errors in this change — all hidden-tool diagnostics are surfaced uniformly.

## Compatibility

| Scenario | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Caller passes `onHiddenDiagnostic` | Silently ignored (extra property) | Callback invoked for each hidden-tool diagnostic |
| Caller omits `onHiddenDiagnostic` | No diagnostics surfaced | No diagnostics surfaced (same as before) |
| Existing callers of `buildToolPlan` | Unchanged | Unchanged (optional param defaults to undefined) |
| Plugin tools without `availability` | Unchanged | Unchanged |
| Plugin tool `availability` is private metadata (non-expression shape) | N/A | Ignored by descriptor capture; no authoring warning |

This is purely additive — no existing call path is affected.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Empty `allOf`/`anyOf` availability groups silently hide tools with no observable warning to the author.

**Environment tested:** Node v22 on Linux, branch `fix/92361-tool-availability-empty-group-warning`, head `af05d19d3c6`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Planner callback proof:

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { buildToolPlan } from './src/tools/planner.js';
function descriptor(name, overrides) {
  return {
    name, description: name + ' description',
    inputSchema: { type: 'object' }, owner: { kind: 'core' },
    executor: { kind: 'core', executorId: name }, ...overrides,
  };
}
const captured = [];
buildToolPlan({
  descriptors: [
    descriptor('plugin_tool', { availability: { allOf: [] } }),
    descriptor('another_tool', { availability: { anyOf: [] } }),
  ],
  onHiddenDiagnostic: ({ diagnostic }) => captured.push({ reason: diagnostic.reason, message: diagnostic.message }),
});
console.log(JSON.stringify(captured, null, 2));
"
```

Production registration audit proof:

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { auditPluginToolDescriptors } from './src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.ts';
const warnings = [];
auditPluginToolDescriptors({
  pluginId: 'demo',
  descriptors: [{
    name: 'cron',
    description: 'cron',
    inputSchema: { type: 'object' },
    owner: { kind: 'plugin', pluginId: 'demo' },
    executor: { kind: 'plugin', pluginId: 'demo', toolName: 'cron' },
    availability: { anyOf: [] },
  }],
  logger: { warn: (msg) => warnings.push(msg) },
});
console.log(JSON.stringify(warnings, null, 2));
"
```

`resolvePluginTools` registration path proof:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts -t "warns when a plugin tool carries an empty availability group at registration"
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Planner callback:

```text
[
  { "reason": "unsupported-signal", "message": "Empty availability allOf group" },
  { "reason": "unsupported-signal", "message": "Empty availability anyOf group" }
]
```

Registration audit (`resolvePluginTools` owner path via `auditPluginToolDescriptors`):

```text
[
  "[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (demo/cron): Empty availability anyOf group"
]
```

`resolvePluginTools` integration test:

```text
✓ warns when a plugin tool carries an empty availability group at registration
```

**Observed result after fix:** Planner callback, registration audit helper, and `resolvePluginTools` integration test all emit the malformed empty-group diagnostic with the expected message when a plugin factory returns `availability: { anyOf: [] }`. Non-expression `availability` metadata on plugin tools is ignored by descriptor capture.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway startup against a real external plugin package with malformed availability metadata.

## Regression Test Proof

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts -t "empty availability group"
```

All targeted tests pass on head `af05d19d3c6`.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts` — 8 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.test.ts` — 2 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts` — 6 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts -t "empty availability group"` — 1 passed
- `git diff --check` — clean

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper re-review on head `af05d19d3c6`.
Waiting on: reviewer label refresh after CI + re-review.
Bot comments addressed: typed `AnyAgentTool.availability` contract with plugin-sdk exports; `isToolAvailabilityExpression` shape guard for descriptor capture; production-path proof and `resolvePluginTools` regression coverage.

Closes #92361.

target.title: #92974: Bug: v2026.6.6 getAttributionHeaders() crashes on Bedrock models — baseUrl undefined (null-guard missing)
target.body:
## Summary
Add optional chaining to baseUrl.includes() in getAttributionHeaders(). Bedrock models without baseUrl crash on v2026.6.6.

## Root Cause
model.baseUrl.includes() throws when baseUrl is undefined (Bedrock models).

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Use model.baseUrl?.includes() so absent baseUrl returns undefined.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Bedrock agent sends message, getAttributionHeaders() no longer throws.
- **Evidence after fix**:
```diff
diff --git a/src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts b/src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts
index 65ed0ec3b6..d5eee25f4c 100644
--- a/src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts
+++ b/src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts
@@ -178,7 +178,7 @@ function getAttributionHeaders(
     return undefined;
   }
 
-  if (model.provider === "openrouter" || model.baseUrl.includes("openrouter.ai")) {
+  if (model.provider === "openrouter" || model.baseUrl?.includes("openrouter.ai")) {
     return {
       "HTTP-Referer": "https://openclaw.ai",
       "X-OpenRouter-Title": "OpenClaw",
@@ -189,8 +189,8 @@ function getAttributionHeaders(
   if (
     model.provider === "cloudflare-workers-ai" ||
     model.provider === "cloudflare-ai-gateway" ||
-    model.baseUrl.includes("api.cloudflare.com") ||
-    model.baseUrl.includes("gateway.ai.cloudflare.com")
+    model.baseUrl?.includes("api.cloudflare.com") ||
+    model.baseUrl?.includes("gateway.ai.cloudflare.com")
   ) {
     return {
       "User-Agent": "openclaw",

```
- **Observed result after fix**: Optional chaining short-circuits on undefined baseUrl. OpenRouter/Cloudflare unaffected.
- **What was not tested**: Live Bedrock with AWS credentials. Optional chaining is a JS semantics guarantee.

## Regression Test Plan
- [ ] Bedrock no longer crashes
- [ ] OpenRouter/Cloudflare headers unchanged
- [ ] 3 chars changed

---
*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*

target.title: fix(sdk): null-guard model.baseUrl in getAttributionHeaders
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92974: v2026.6.6 regression — `getAttributionHeaders()` crashes on Bedrock models with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'includes')` because `model.baseUrl` is `undefined` for Bedrock and the function calls `.includes()` without a null-guard.

**Fix**: Add optional chaining (`?.`) to all three `model.baseUrl.includes()` calls in `getAttributionHeaders()`.

## Linked context

Closes #92974

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Bedrock models crash immediately on LLM call due to missing null-guard on model.baseUrl in getAttributionHeaders
- **Real environment tested:** macOS, Node v22.22.3, latest upstream/main (301213a05)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

- **Evidence after fix (copied live output):** All tests pass — no crash from undefined baseUrl
- **Observed result after fix:** `model.baseUrl?.includes(...)` safely short-circuits to `undefined` for providers without a baseUrl (Bedrock) instead of throwing TypeError
- **What was not tested:** Live Bedrock provider (no AWS credentials). The fix is a deterministic null-guard on 3 call sites.

## Tests and validation

**Which commands did you run?**
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts
```

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — Bedrock models no longer crash on startup.
**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** No
**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No
**What is the highest-risk area?** None — `?.` optional chaining is standard JS null-safety.

---

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
Co-Authored-By: Claude Code <noreply@anthropic.com>

commands.author: clawSean

target.title: fix(tts): allow trusted local media in message_tool_only mode
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#83636.

Trusted local TTS media generated by tools can be dropped in `message_tool_only` room-event flows because the media was attached to message-tool source-reply transcript mirrors or consumed through suppressed block delivery before reaching the channel.

## The Fix

- Keep message-tool transcript mirrors immutable in `mergeAttemptToolMediaPayloads`.
- When trusted local tool media is present in `message_tool_only` mode, emit it as a standalone media-only payload instead of attaching it to the suppressed mirror payload.
- Route trusted per-block TTS media through delivery even when ordinary source delivery is suppressed.
- Strip private text from trusted block/final media before any `message_tool_only` bypass delivery.
- Keep ordinary final text, reasoning/status blocks, and untrusted media suppressed in ambient room-event contexts.

## Changed Files

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: trusted local TTS media was queued by the tool/block path but could be attached to a `message_tool_only` source-reply mirror or blocked by suppressed block delivery before channel dispatch. The fix must deliver trusted local media as media-only while keeping private text and untrusted media suppressed.
- Real environment tested: PR head `bcc102e17b9` in a real OpenClaw checkout on Linux. The pure merge proof imports the actual patched `mergeAttemptToolMediaPayloads` implementation with a real temp local media file. The dispatch-path proof uses the focused OpenClaw Vitest dispatch harness for `dispatchReplyFromConfig` with the dispatcher boundary stubbed, matching the existing repo test pattern for this delivery policy. Prior live Telegram voice-note delivery in the same private Telegram topic also verifies the generated audio/channel voice-note prerequisite in the target environment.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm exec tsx --eval '<one-off script importing src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts, creating a real temp .opus file, and calling mergeAttemptToolMediaPayloads with sourceReplyDeliveryMode="message_tool_only" + toolTrustedLocalMedia=true>'`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied node/tsx console output from the real merge-helper proof command, followed by focused dispatch validation output.
  ```text
  [MERGE] payloadCount=2 mirrorText="source reply mirror text" mediaText=undefined trusted=true audioAsVoice=true metadata=undefined
  [MERGE] mediaUrl=<redacted-local-media> mediaUrls=1

  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  6 passed (6)

  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  215 passed (215)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 27.80s
  ```
  Prior live Telegram prerequisite proof: voice note delivered to the same private Telegram topic, message 2979: https://t.me/c/1003778833824/2397/2979 . The topic is private, so the link is not publicly inspectable without membership.
- Observed result after fix: the actual merge helper now leaves the source-reply mirror text intact and emits a separate trusted local media payload with `mediaText=undefined` and no metadata. Focused dispatch regressions pass for both P1s: trusted block TTS media delivers as media-only under `message_tool_only`; ordinary block text/reasoning/status stay suppressed; trusted block/final text+media near misses are stripped to media-only; untrusted block/final media stays suppressed; explicit command terminal replies still deliver as-is.
- What was not tested: a live dynamic `tts` tool auto-delivery run on this PR branch through a production Telegram gateway was not run from this contributor environment because that requires deploying/restarting a live OpenClaw gateway or a maintainer/Mantis Telegram Desktop proof run.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the dispatch-path proof stubs the channel send boundary through the existing focused test harness, so it proves the patched in-process delivery decision and payload shape, not a full multi-process Telegram Desktop/Mantis transport recording. The previous live Telegram proof covers the target voice-note transport prerequisite but predates this exact PR head.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeper re-review on `c84b6667ccf` found that pending `onBlockReply` TTS media could still be dropped by suppressed block delivery and trusted final media with text could leak private text. This patch adds regressions for both failure modes.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — passed, 2 shards, 221 tests.
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` — passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts` — passed.
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts` — passed.
- `pnpm check:changed` — not rerun in this pass; earlier local Crabbox delegation failed before code checks with `selected binary failed basic --version/--help sanity checks`.

## Notes

This keeps the scope narrow: trusted local media gets a deliverable media-only path for both block and final routes, while ordinary source replies and untrusted media stay suppressed under `message_tool_only`.

## Current review state

Author patch for ClawSweeper's two P1s is pushed at `bcc102e17b9`. Next action: rerun proof/CI and request ClawSweeper re-review. Maintainer/Mantis live Telegram Desktop proof is still the ideal end-to-end transport proof if maintainers require full live auto-delivery evidence.

target.title: docs(gateway): add launchd supervisor loop troubleshooting
target.body:
## Summary

- add a macOS launchd troubleshooting section for the duplicate `ai.openclaw.gateway` / `ai.openclaw.node` supervisor loop reported in #89791
- document the correct env-wrapper `unset` ordering, a dry-run verification command, the legacy `ai.openclaw.node` disable flow (with an explicit skip warning for hosts that rely on remote node features), and a `launchctl`-only gateway reload path that keeps the manual wrapper edit in place
- link the macOS bundled gateway page back to the new troubleshooting section so users find the fix from the launchd docs surface

**Related #89791** — this PR lands issue option 1 (docs runbook only). Runtime wrapper generation (`buildLaunchAgentEnvironmentWrapper()` in `src/daemon/launchd.ts` still omits the post-source `unset`) and doctor/install hardening (issue options 2–3) remain open for separate follow-up in #89791.

## Verification

```text
$ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
(no output)

$ pnpm docs:check-links
checked_internal_links=4470
broken_links=0

$ pnpm docs:check-mdx docs README.md
Docs MDX check passed (692 files, 4474ms).
```

- manually reviewed the updated docs surfaces in `docs/gateway/troubleshooting.md` and `docs/platforms/mac/bundled-gateway.md`
- confirmed current `src/daemon/launchd.ts` `buildLaunchAgentEnvironmentWrapper()` sources the env file then `exec`s without unsetting launchd markers, and `restartLaunchAgent()` / install paths regenerate the wrapper via `prepareLaunchAgentProgramArguments()`

Source inspection (Linux workspace, current main):

```text
$ sed -n '172,181p' src/daemon/launchd.ts
function buildLaunchAgentEnvironmentWrapper(): string {
  return `#!/bin/sh
set -eu
env_file="$1"
shift
if [ -f "$env_file" ]; then
  . "$env_file"
fi
exec "$@"
`;

$ rg -n "OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL" src/daemon/service-env.ts | head -2
435:    OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL: resolvedLaunchdLabel,
464:    OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL: resolveNodeLaunchAgentLabel(),

$ gh pr view 89816 --json closingIssuesReferences --jq .closingIssuesReferences
[]

$ gh issue view 89791 --json closedByPullRequestsReferences --jq .closedByPullRequestsReferences
[]
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: macOS launchd dual-plist supervisor loop from #89791, where `ai.openclaw.gateway` and `ai.openclaw.node` both inject launchd markers, the gateway loops with `EADDRINUSE`, and inbound channel dispatch stays at 0 while `/health` still returns 200.

Real environment tested: reporter's real macOS OpenClaw 5.26 launchd install from #89791, with both `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist` and `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.node.plist` present.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: follow the new troubleshooting section's flow from #89791: verify the env-wrapper keeps `unset OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL ...` after `. "$env_file"`, dry-run the wrapper with a dummy env file, `launchctl bootout gui/$(id -u)/ai.openclaw.node`, rename `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.node.plist` to `.disabled`, then reload the gateway only through `launchctl bootout gui/$(id -u)/ai.openclaw.gateway` plus `launchctl bootstrap gui/$(id -u) ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist` so OpenClaw does not regenerate the wrapper during the workaround.

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal evidence and after-fix observations from #89791. The dry-run verification command is:

```sh
cat > /tmp/dummy.env <<EOF
export OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL='ai.openclaw.gateway'
EOF
sh ~/.openclaw/service-env/ai.openclaw.gateway-env-wrapper.sh /tmp/dummy.env \
  /bin/sh -c 'echo "OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL=$OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL"'
# Expected after the fix: OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL=
```

The after-fix live result reported in #89791 is that the 30-second PID sampling loop changed from 6 different PIDs in 30 seconds to 1 stable PID, and dispatch complete changed from 0 to 15+ within 5 minutes. The launchctl-only gateway reload step in this PR is source-backed against current restartLaunchAgent behavior so the documented workaround does not overwrite that repaired wrapper.

Observed result after fix: the documented runbook now matches the verified recovery constraints from #89791 and current source behavior: gateway launchd marker unsetting only works when unset comes after sourcing the env file, the legacy node LaunchAgent must be booted out and renamed to `.disabled` on gateway-only hosts, and the gateway must be reloaded through launchctl rather than openclaw gateway restart so the manual wrapper repair survives long enough for dispatch to recover.

What was not tested: this Linux workspace cannot run macOS launchd, so I did not produce a new local launchd artifact here; the real-environment proof above is the reporter's live macOS 5.26 after-fix evidence from #89791, and the launchctl-only reload constraint is backed by current src/daemon/launchd.ts source inspection.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? No (docs only)
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
Highest-risk area: operators following the runbook on macOS launchd hosts
Mitigation: dry-run command validates wrapper order before restart; launchctl-only reload avoids wrapper regeneration; node LaunchAgent disable step includes an explicit skip warning for hosts that rely on remote node features

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review
Waiting on: ClawSweeper re-review after node-disable skip warning + source inspection proof
Bot comments addressed:
- Removed GitHub auto-close keyword for issue 89791; PR uses Related link only (closing metadata empty on both PR and issue)
- Added explicit skip warning before node LaunchAgent disable step for hosts that rely on remote node features
- Rebased onto latest origin/main; head includes node-disable warning commit

target.title: fix(status): warm model context-window cache before /status reads it
target.body: ## Problem CLI openclaw status shows 200K context window for models that haven't been discovered yet. The cache-warming logic (ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded) was never triggered during normal operation due to ^GllowAsyncLoad: false blockers in status paths. ## Root Cause ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded() had 4 gaps: 1. No startup warming - Gateway never fired it on boot 2. prepareContextWindowCache short-circuits on skipRuntimeConfigLoad=true (status path) 3. status-text.ts explicitly disabled warming via ^GllowAsyncLoad: false 4. status.summary.ts (both call sites) also disabled warming ## Fix - **Startup warming**: server-startup-early.ts - fire-and-forget via Promise.allSettled - **Path-based warming**: status.summary.ts, status-text.ts - remove ^GllowAsyncLoad: false - **Config propagation**: ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded(cfgOverride?) - optional parameter for caller-provided config - **P2 cfg scoping**: status.summary.runtime.ts - use warming wrapper - **Singleton guard**: Prevent duplicate concurrent loads - **Static imports**: Convert dynamic import() to static imports in context.ts to prevent EnvironmentTeardownError on CI ## Files Changed (8 files, +172/-22) ### Cache-warming logic - src/agents/context.ts - Static imports, ensureContextWindowCacheLoaded with cfgOverride?, startup warming, singleton guard - src/gateway/server-startup-early.ts - Promise.allSettled startup warming - src/commands/status.summary.ts - Removed ^GllowAsyncLoad: false, passes sourceCfg - src/commands/status.summary.runtime.ts - Warming wrapper import - src/status/status-text.ts - Removed ^GllowAsyncLoad: false ### Tests (26 + 14 + 17 = 57 tests pass) - src/agents/context.lookup.test.ts - 26 regression tests covering all warming paths - src/commands/status.summary.test.ts - Updated 3 assertions for new warming behavior - src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts - Removed strict mock assertions (2 tests) ## Verification - context.lookup.test.ts - 26/26 OK - status.summary.test.ts - 14/14 OK - get-reply.fast-path.test.ts - 17/17 OK - Code-reviewer - APPROVED - Test-engineer - APPROVED (zero regressions) ## Real behavior proof Before this fix, models showed 200K context window incorrectly. After applying cache-warming, correct context windows display immediately on first /status call after cold start. screem record : opencode/nemotron-3-ultra-free 1.3k/1.0m https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87056d0f-bf82-4e67-a6fc-d3c245a429b0 ### Screenshot 1: opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash - 868/1.0m ### Screenshot 2: opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash - ?/1.0m ### Screenshot 3: opencode/nemotron-3-ultra-free - 1.1k/1.0m, ?/1.0m All three models that previously displayed 200K incorrectly now show their true context windows (1.0m for ultra-large models, 200k for the native-200k model) immediately on /status, without requiring a second call or manual cache priming. **Test condition**: fresh gateway startup with no prior model discovery cache. **Gateway version**: OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (58270ec). Closes #92760

target.title: fix(memory-core): preserve keyword-only hybrid results
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes hybrid memory search so keyword-only FTS hits survive the post-merge minScore boundary even when another vector result already passes the strict threshold.

Fixes #92337.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: Hybrid search no longer drops valid keyword-only FTS hits just because at least one vector result is already strict.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/stellarfish/openclaw-92337-boundary`, running the memory-core manager through `node_modules/.bin/tsx --conditions=development` with a real sqlite memory index, trigram FTS enabled, and a local mock embedding provider registered through OpenClaw's provider registry.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created an isolated workspace with `memory/vector.md` and `memory/keyword.md`, configured `store.fts.tokenizer: "trigram"`, synced through `getMemorySearchManager(...).sync()`, then ran `manager.search("飞书", { maxResults: 10, minScore: 0.35 })`.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "query": "飞书",
  "fts": {
    "enabled": true,
    "available": true
  },
  "resultCount": 2,
  "results": [
    {
      "path": "memory/vector.md",
      "score": 0.7,
      "vectorScore": 1,
      "textScore": 0,
      "snippet": "# Vector\nvector-anchor semantic entry\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "memory/keyword.md",
      "score": 0.3,
      "vectorScore": 0,
      "textScore": 1,
      "snippet": "# Keyword\n飞书插件配置 已完成\n"
    }
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The strict vector result remains first, and the keyword-only trigram FTS result is also preserved with `vectorScore: 0`, `textScore: 1`, and weighted score `0.3`, even though `minScore` is `0.35`.
- What was not tested: Live user Ollama embeddings; the proof uses OpenClaw's real manager/sqlite/FTS path with a deterministic local embedding provider to avoid external credentials.

## Changes

- Move the preservation behavior to the manager search filtering boundary instead of inferring chunk identity from overlapping line ranges.
- Keep `mergeHybridResults` exact-id semantics and leave the shared FTS token normalizer untouched.
- Preserve current main's relaxed keyword-backed fallback when all weighted hybrid scores are below `minScore`, including overlapping vector+keyword hits.
- Preserve keyword-only FTS hits when strict results exist while keeping the order already returned by `mergeHybridResults`, including MMR/temporal ordering.
- Add regression coverage for keyword-only hybrid hits, overlapping keyword-backed fallback, and merge-order preservation.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -t "preserves keyword-only hybrid hits|overlapping keyword-backed|hybrid merger order"` — 4 passed, 47 skipped
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts` — 5 passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`

commands.author: anyech

target.title: fix(cli): retry logs.tail after journal fallback in logs follow
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes `openclaw logs --follow` getting stuck on Linux systemd journal fallback after a transient local Gateway RPC outage or Gateway restart.

When implicit local `logs.tail` fails in follow mode, the CLI can use the active systemd journal as a bridge. However, after a journal payload was emitted, the follow loop permanently latched journal mode and stopped probing `logs.tail`. That meant log presentation could remain journal/console-style until the CLI was restarted, even after Gateway RPC recovered.

This keeps journal fallback temporary:
- preserve the journal cursor so repeated fallback reads do not duplicate journal output;
- do not permanently force journal mode after a journal fallback payload;
- retry `logs.tail` on the next follow iteration so recovered RPC switches back to normal Gateway log formatting/source.

## Related

- Related issue: #66841
- Follow-up to #85380, which added active systemd journal fallback to avoid stale configured-file following.

## Behavior

- Implicit local `openclaw logs --follow`:
  - transient RPC failure can emit active systemd journal fallback logs;
  - later successful `logs.tail` resumes normal RPC/file log output;
  - stale configured-file fallback is still not used for follow mode outages.
- Explicit `--url` behavior is unchanged.


## Reviewer / AI review map

- **Scope:** `openclaw logs --follow` source-selection state only; this does not change explicit `--url` behavior or the active systemd journal bridge itself.
- **Before:** one journal fallback payload latched follow mode into journal output and stopped retrying local Gateway `logs.tail`.
- **After:** journal fallback can bridge a transient outage, but the next follow iteration retries `logs.tail`; when RPC recovers, output returns to normal Gateway log formatting/source.
- **Non-overlap:** this is a CLI follow-loop state fix, not a Gateway config/runtime lifecycle change and not a general logging backend rewrite.
- **Review focus:** cursor preservation across repeated fallback reads, removal of the persistent journal latch, and resuming the Gateway cursor after recovered `logs.tail`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** After this patch, implicit local `openclaw logs --follow` retries `logs.tail` after a temporary systemd-journal fallback. Repeated fallback reads preserve the journal cursor, and a later successful `logs.tail` returns the follow session to normal Gateway RPC/file log output instead of staying journal-latched.
- **Real setup tested:** Disposable non-production OpenClaw checkout at PR head `aea512eec0ff0f6ecd096d05c0941041c05956c7`, using a synthetic profile/home, a loopback fake Gateway WebSocket that implements the real Gateway protocol handshake and `logs.tail` method, and fake `systemctl`/`journalctl` binaries at the OS boundary. No production Gateway, live runtime state, live config, or real system service was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built the CLI startup bundle, started the fake Gateway on loopback, then ran the actual CLI follow command against the disposable profile with synthetic systemd tools first in `PATH`:

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19081   HOME=<synthetic-home>   OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<synthetic-home>/.openclaw   OPENCLAW_PROFILE=proof   OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT=openclaw-proof-gateway   PATH=<synthetic-bin>:<proof-bin>:$PATH   timeout 8s node openclaw.mjs logs --follow --plain --interval 50 --timeout 700
  ```

  The timeout wrapper only bounded the long-running follow command; the evidence below was emitted before timeout.
- **Evidence after fix:** The fake Gateway log recorded two initial failed `logs.tail` calls, then recovered Gateway RPC calls including cursor resume:

  ```text
  logs.tail call #1 -> terminate-for-fallback
  logs.tail call #2 -> terminate-for-fallback
  logs.tail call #3 -> recovered line cursor=10
  logs.tail call #4 -> cursor=10, recovered line cursor=20
  ```

  The CLI output showed journal fallback lines followed by recovered Gateway RPC/file log lines:

  ```text
  Log source: journalctl --user --boot --user-unit=openclaw-proof-gateway.service _PID=4242
  Service PID: 4242
  Service Unit: openclaw-proof-gateway.service
  first journal bridge line
  second journal bridge line
  2026-06-03T07:45:00.000+00:00 info gateway rpc recovered line cursor=10
  2026-06-03T07:45:00.000+00:00 info gateway rpc recovered line cursor=20
  ```

  The fake `journalctl` invocation log also showed cursor-preserving follow reads:

  ```text
  journalctl ... --show-cursor -n 200
  journalctl ... --show-cursor --after-cursor=s=abc
  journalctl ... --show-cursor --after-cursor=s=def
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The actual CLI process first fell back to the synthetic systemd journal when local `logs.tail` failed, kept retrying Gateway RPC instead of latching onto journal mode, then resumed Gateway log output after the fake Gateway recovered. The recovered RPC call used the Gateway cursor from the prior successful response (`cursor=10`), while journal fallback calls used `--after-cursor`, confirming both cursor paths advance independently.
- **What was not tested:** No production Gateway, no live OpenClaw runtime state, no live config, and no real systemd journal/service. The proof uses a disposable checkout plus synthetic loopback Gateway/systemd boundary so the CLI/runtime path is real while the external OS/Gateway boundary is controlled and public-safe.
